reviews.author: Craig Swenson
reviews.text: Absolutely love this place to meet up and go out.  Come early and spend some time on the beautiful rooftop!  Hayley is awesome and will help you find a great spot that fits your preferences!  After the rooftop, we went down to the Bunker and got table service.  The music, service, and atmosphere were great!  Capped off the night with Juan bartending in Sketch!  So fun!  I would highly recommend a night out here… or several!

reviews.author: Alric Milton
reviews.text: it was a very lively club , it’s a underground club , the staff is very friendly especially the bouncer they were very accommodating as I didn’t have my physical ID  and they accepted my digital ID , drink are reasonably good and there’s 3 dance floors and the music was amazing

reviews.author: Purple aurora ASMR
reviews.text: I had a terrible experience at this club. The staff and security treated guests with zero respect and created an environment that felt unsafe. I personally left feeling shaken and mistreated. I even learned that if you have issues with staff here, the police say they “can’t do anything” because it’s private property — meaning you’re completely unprotected. As a woman, I would never recommend this place to anyone looking for a safe or welcoming night out. There needs to be accountability and change in how this establishment handles its guests.

reviews.text: Club with lots of live music. If you love alternative, rock, funk than this is place for you. Awesome bartenders. Would recommend to anyone who wants to have a great time.

reviews.author: Terry Wimes
reviews.text: It was comedy night, I couldn’t understand Croatian. 5 stars because the staff was super nice, gave me free drink for walking all that way and no live music. Sometimes the small things along with good people can make your business thrive.

reviews.author: Filipe ZG
reviews.text: I went to see the Christmas Lineup stand-up show at Sax, and it was honestly such a blast! The atmosphere in the club is always intimate and cool, but the holiday spirit made it even better. All the comedians were on point and had the whole crowd laughing throughout the entire set. It’s the perfect way to spend an evening if you're tired of the usual Christmas markets and want some genuine entertainment. The drinks were good, the service was quick despite the crowd, and the energy in the room was fantastic. My only tiny complaint is that it can get a bit cramped when it's sold out, but the non-stop laughs more than made up for it. Highly recommend catching their next show!

reviews.author: CroTours Apartmani Maksimir Rebro Hrvatska
reviews.text: The SAX Club in Palmotićeva is a vibrant venue known for its lively atmosphere and exceptional music. With its chic décor and talented saxophone performers, it offers guests a unique and memorable nightlife experience. Whether you're looking to dance the night away or simply unwind with a cocktail, Sax Club delivers on both fronts, making it a must-visit destination for locals and tourists alike.

reviews.author: Luka
reviews.text:
The show with Goc was great! 3 stars because it was way too cold inside - half of the audience wore jackets. Also, the sound was horrible, I dont know if they have a tone master but many people were covering their ears because of distortions and loudness (Goran’s voice and style have a very wide frequency range). Thats a shame because the comedian did a great job but the cold and sound undermined his effort - everything could have been fixed with a few buttons. You cannot enjoy the show if your body is freezing and your ears are tearing.
Everything else is spot on - location, staff, service, lineup etc.

Credit cards or euros accepted at both Beertija and KSS. Cigar and cigarette smoking is welcomed.  If weather is stormy, windy or cold, Beertija has indoor and undercover seating.

We had an evening of beers and burgers at this bar.

The beer selection is very extensive and the waitress helped in making our choices as we were unfamiliar with the local craft brewers.

Burgers came from an onsite street food vendor. They were massive and very tasty. At €11 for a double cheeseburger (including fries) seemed pretty good value. Worth noting that the burger vendor only accepts cash (cards are fine for the bar).

Besides that, I would say this place is very fun and a great night life destination if you are in Zagreb.

reviews.author: Same Likeyou
reviews.text: Tucked away in the city center, this gem is a local favorite—and for good reason. The garden setting, adorned with trees and lamps, provides a cozy atmosphere perfect for work or casual chats. What makes the place even more inviting is the friendly disposition of both the staff and the patrons. Whether you're looking to engage with locals or simply enjoy a quiet moment, this spot delivers. Highly recommended.

reviews.author: Félix AS
reviews.text: Travelled alone a month ago and found this bar by chance. The jazz environment is cool, the waiters are super kind and the local is beautiful. Really loved that strong black beer I was served, I've been trying to find it in Spain but I don't quite remember the name.

reviews.author: Ross
reviews.text:
Lovely bar tucked away in garden just down from the railway station.

Good prices, friendly service. An oasis in the heat- no camels were sighted!

Good range of draft craft beers as well as the usual suspects in a bottle.

reviews.author: Svetlana Margetová
reviews.text:
I visited Bacchus with Brano, Rolko, and Ondra for about 2 hours at night before heading to the ice rink at King Tomislav Square. This turned out to be one of our favorite stops during our Zagreb Christmas trip! The vibe here is absolutely fantastic – it's a hidden gem tucked away in a courtyard, and you'd never know it existed unless someone told you about it or you stumbled upon it by chance.
The staff were really nice and welcoming, which made a big difference. The drinks were surprisingly good and cheap – much more affordable than most places we visited during Advent. We tried the local drinks, and this is where things got interesting! We ordered some strong local spirit (I honestly forgot the name – maybe travarica or pelinkovac?) and, well, let's just say it wasn't for us. It was VERY strong and had that medicinal herbal taste, kind of like our Alpa back home. But honestly, everything else we tried was great! The regular drinks, beers, and other options were all excellent.
What really makes Bacchus special is the atmosphere. The place has this cozy, alternative, local feel – you can tell it's a favorite spot for Zagreb locals, not just tourists. The courtyard is beautiful even in winter, and I can only imagine how amazing it must be in summer when those huge fig trees are in full bloom providing shade. The cellar bar inside has this vintage vibe with old wooden furniture, brick ceiling, and retro decorations that transport you to another era.

reviews.author: Rođendaonica Epic

reviews.text: We had a party organized by the company. An hour before the party was supposed to end, they turned off our water and acted like they didn't know what was going on. The waiter even said to me, "Do I look like a plumber to you?" when I told him there was no water. It's a disaster, you pay for the service and they turn off your water so you can't even wash your hands, without any explanation.

reviews.author: Malik Ajala
reviews.text: Very nice and cozy place with knowledgeable staff! Cocktails were amazing

reviews.author: Nada Boto
reviews.text:
Lovely bar. I can see why it has become so popular!

The service was fantastic. Kudos to our server, not sure what his name is (tattooed guy, dark hair...)- Made no mistakes and ensured was going well.

The selection for drinks is wide and unique. As you can see they have some interesting cocktails like this cotton candy one.

The atmosphere is nice, you've got a terrace outside- it's quite city vibes, you're between the street and the sidewalk.

On the inside it's like a steam punk (hence "fog") old vintage vibe, very cool. Music is nice, the played some 80s pop hits which I personally liked.

It is precisely what Zagreb needs more of- real bars! Not just cafes and clubs.

reviews.author: Ljubica Banic
reviews.text:
This place is very unique has some amazing pieces of art.... as well as the menu.
I’m Peruvian and for this first time I tried a fantastic chocolate Inca tea. I must say I Was fascinated with the unique flavour.

The waiter was so nice as professional he knew it all the menu by heart.

Definitely I recommend to visit this place.

The place is as stylish as it is Instagrammable, making it well worth splurging a little on their top-notch cocktails. Highly recommend for any time of the day.

The service here was absolutely amazing with very nice servers and bartenders. The main bartender we saw there obviously knew and took pride in his craft.

Our servers have been exceptional at guiding us and creating cocktails made personally.

reviews.text: Cracking bar with a few good craft beers on tap and a few bottles as well. Great outside area with plenty of tables and chairs. Staff very friendly and helpful. They let us bring some food in from one of the fab pizza take away shops

reviews.author: Vanja Matic
reviews.text: Good place to sit in or outside to meet with friends. It’s got two levels inside. The drinks we had were good quality and the service could be a bit faster. The waiter was serving several areas so often we could see him. Other than that it’s a nice bar with good atmosphere and music. Would definitely recommend.

reviews.author: Mohil Kapoor
reviews.text: Great place to hangout with friends and colleagues in the city. We went after dinner and the place is cozy and small. Good music and drinks. Definitely can be better managed especially the seating

reviews.author: Mohil Kapoor
reviews.text: It's a cozy small place but the drinks and music is fantastic. You can definitely have a good time here.

reviews.author: Marino Paradina
reviews.text:
Okay pub near the Cvjetni trg. We sat inside and it was an lovely atmosphere, but it was noisey alot to the point that you had to yell at. The prices shocked me because it was expensive.
All in all, I recommend it to someone who loves livelier atmosphere and vintage decoration.

reviews.author: Bren Kenny
reviews.text:
Excellent cocktails made my Jerome. The music was relaxing and the venue was light in a way that made it comfortable and easy to talk.

If I lived in Zagreb, I think this would be a local of mine.

reviews.author: Jürgen Engel
reviews.text: Unique spot in Zagreb, absolutely recommendable! Really appealing outside sitting with style and lots of plants. Excellent food and drinks at moderate prices and very friendly staff. Prefect for me in all aspects ❤️🤗👏🏻👏🏻🕺

reviews.author: Luka Grgurić
reviews.text:
One of my favorite spots in Zagreb! Tucked right in the city center, yet it feels like a hidden oasis — the terrace is full of greenery, airy, and beautifully designed. The cocktail menu is excellent, and everything I’ve tried has been both tasty and well-crafted.

What I love most is the laid-back vibe — the staff are super chill, and the whole place has a young, relaxed energy. It’s not about polished, formal service, but about feeling comfortable and welcome. Perfect for unwinding with friends and enjoying quality drinks in a casual setting. Highly recommended! 👌🍸

reviews.author: Nikola Brzak
reviews.text: Great place for cocktails. Especially on Wednesdays when the cocktails are only 5€. Keep in mind that not all cocktails are 5€ only the five signature ones. If you’re not sure ask the staff.

reviews.author: I Ivan
reviews.text: One of the best Hostel which i visited. Good price good badroom and nice people around. And breakfast is delicious and big portion. And pets are alowed so is 10/10!!

places.name: Harat's Pub
places.address: Kaptol 4 Trg Petrice Kerempuha, Opatovina 11, 10000, Zagreb, Croatia

reviews.author: Zoe Zoe
reviews.text: Great pub in the centre of Zagreb, gorni grad.  Nice selection of Beers, service is good, food is nice. I had the sliders and chips. Friendly atmosphere, great music and at night they playblues! Awesome!

reviews.author: Kikimora_Taste_Nomad
reviews.text: Tourist or local, this bar is for everyone, Erasmus students, travelers, and locals alike. They have local beers and popular brands, fast and friendly service, and can even play your music requests. Weekends are lively, sometimes with live music, creating a fun, casual vibe. Perfect for a relaxed evening with friends or just enjoying the local atmosphere.

reviews.author: Emma Aghlamazyan
reviews.text: We went to watch the El Clásico match, but unfortunately, the volume wasn’t turned on, so there was no real football atmosphere. That took away from the experience a bit, but overall the place was good.

reviews.author: Josh Baltazar
reviews.text: Came to the Pub at 17:30 on a Sunday evening to watch an FA Cup match. Huge place, lots of areas in the Pub to sit and eat inside and outdoor. The other reviews are pretty accurate, it does take quite a long time to get your order taken. Also wasn’t an issue for us but to note, you can smoke anywhere inside. Overall, a very central Irish Pub with beers from Ireland/UK.

reviews.author: Jason Gordon
reviews.text: My first beer in Croatia was here. I visited late in the evening,  the place was quiet on a weekday night. I only stopped in for a beverage, but it looks like a fun place to watch the footy over a beer or three!

places.name: Roots Bar
places.address: Ul. Josipa Eugena Tomića 5, 10000, Zagreb, Croatia

reviews.author: Spn Nrmn (Spiny Norman)
reviews.text:
If we could put a billion stars instead of 5/5 we would!
Excellent original and classic cocktails and mocktails/non-alcoholic cocktails (which is rare to find in the city, especially with 0% spirits). As a sober person, I didn't feel excluded one minute. Every drink was truly special! We even had the pleasure of discovering a sausage infused one 😁
But also, incredible, if not one of the best services we've ever received. The waitress was super friendly without being overbearing and extremely attentive. She gave great advices and notes about our drinks and we were very impressed with how professional and efficient she was for her age. The mixologist was also very helpful and kind.
In conclusion : don't go to this bar, RUN to this bar. You will have the best drink of your life in a super relaxed and cosy environment.

reviews.author: Fahaad Kazi
reviews.text: Visited Roots Bar in Zagreb twice in two days, and it’s an absolute gem! The service is super friendly and genuine - not the fake touristy kind - making us want to return just for that. Yesterday, we tried two Negroni-inspired cocktails: the “Fig” (fig distillate, Campari, red vermouth, fig leaf soda) offered a nice twist, though not groundbreaking, and the “PBJ” (rye whiskey, red vermouth, peanut butter, and jelly) was a standout - beautifully presented with a jelly-topped spoon for that PB&J vibe. Today, the Lemon Cheesecake (limoncello, lemon, heavy cream, Licor 43) was an absolute explosion of delight - imagine a creamy, zesty lemon cheesecake transformed into the most divine drink you’ve ever tasted! It’s a must-try masterpiece! Highly recommend this place to anyone visiting Zagreb! Oh and the music selection was spot on as well 😉

reviews.author: Atahan Ceylan
reviews.text: Great place to hang out. Good music nice cocktails. I had 5 different types cocktails. Cocktail named after country names.

reviews.author: Mubin
reviews.text:
Absolutely delicious cocktails here! Loved all that we tried. Service was really great, we were able to walk in after 10pm and get seats inside. The waiters were friendly and explained the cocktails well.

Croatia- Tasty. The star is the prosciutto, cheese and olive garnish was the star.. basically you sip the drink to try it. The eat the garnish to taste with a different palate. Really delicious we were offered another garnish so we both can try but were charged for it!

Barbados- the most delicious mango flavored cocktail i ever had!! The foam was out of this world!

Australia- good cocktail, very refreshing and tart. The foam here yet again is the star. If you like berry and tartness this is for you.

reviews.author: Tuğçe Kaya
reviews.text: Great little bar with an authentic vibe and a good atmosphere. They have a wide range of cocktails and the service was fast. Seating is a bit limited so a reservation would be a good idea on weekends. Overall, a solid spot for drinks!

reviews.author: Robin Sewell
reviews.text: Amazing bar with very inspiring interior!

reviews.author: Ivar Kraljii
reviews.text: Our server with the cute pig tails was very sweet and accommodating to new visitors to Zagreb. She made our trip amazing. She made our experience at the mojo bar very pleasant.

reviews.author: Sighting Sarah
reviews.text: Cool cafe bar on a nice quiet street. Great place to hangout. I’ve come in the summer and winter. Always a good time.

reviews.author: Luka Savic
reviews.text: Great place to have a glass of wine. As always they could work on their toilet so you can close inside. When I was last time there they had 2 time power problems and we were in dark. They could have more cheep wine on the menu. But overall a cool bar to come and have a good time.

reviews.author: Senior Junior
reviews.text: We had fun in Mojo. Drinks were pretty cheap and waiters were decent. Interior maybe needs a little bit if work, but really great vibe in total.

reviews.author: Kurt Thomsen
reviews.text: Loved this place. Great vibe, dive bar with great local beer and beer fridges for take-out. One stop place for all the best Croatian micro brews. Best beer bar in Zagreb, probably in Croatia.

reviews.author: Ivan Rubil
reviews.text:
Probably the best-established beer bar in Zagreb. Now with outdoor seating at a pretty amazing and historical location just a minute away from Jelačić Square.

Smoking is allowed, as per the Croatian bar culture, but the new outdoor seating, that's largely available throughout the day, now provides an option for non-smokers. The word "bar" is after all in the name of the place, and it's there with full meaning. This is not a brewpub, nor a taproom, nor a beer hall.

It's a metal bar with a fantastic selection of beer.

You can read the numerized beer table outside, or above the bar on the inside, and then tell the keepers the number and size you're going for. There's also a refrigerated selection of canned beer that can be bought as takeout.

It's not the most courteous place in the world. The keepers have maintained a no-nonsense approach for a long time. It makes the atmosphere safe for everyone regardless of their identity, which is unfortunately not always the case in the country and the wider region.

reviews.author: Jared Longhitano
reviews.text: My favorite bar in Zagreb! I randomly found this bar on my first trip (There's also a bar in NYC named Valhalla - no relation) and had to go in because I heard metal playing! It's a Norse / Viking themed bar, obviously, and awesome music is always on. THe crowd is always generally cool and fun and the vibe is awesome. I often come here and wind up staying the whole evening!

reviews.author: Deniss
reviews.text: Nice beer pub with good selection of beers on tap. Friendly staff. If you are beer lover this beer pub is for you. Recommend!

reviews.author: famousaj1
reviews.text: Decent pub, can get smoky and loud at the weekend. They do have a few outdoor spots tables, but limited. Barkeep was friendly.

reviews.author: Sofiia Fedusiv
reviews.text:
A wonderful place to visit in Zagreb. Every time I have a chance to be a Zg-guide for friends who just came to the city, I always take them here. Every season of the year in this cafe is beautiful (most favourite summer & autumn for sure), the view is spectacular, super-friendly guys working there, lovely drinks (cocktails!) and the place itself - cosy and welcoming.
P.S. The best spot is the improvised table on the top floor, right near the entrance: the best for pictures.

reviews.author: RobertsRoamers
reviews.text:
We found this place by mistake , as you along the road it was just a door with the sign on
So glad we went in there are various beers in cans and bottles and local beer on draft
As you walk in the bar is lower down and a terrace overlooking the church and town it’s a fantastic view and a peaceful place to have a few drinks
The staff are also very welcoming and friendly
The barman also let me try a local spirt , with lemon and ice
It was so nice I bought a bottle

reviews.author: Luka Medurecan
reviews.text: Fantastic cocktails and coffee – the head bartender and staff are exceptionally friendly and knowledgeable. A truly welcoming atmosphere with top-tier service and expertly crafted drinks.

reviews.author: Geraldine Goycochea
reviews.text: Very nice views, hard to reach bc of google maps directions but nice when you find it. The girls that work there are very friendly.

reviews.author: Max Hofmann
reviews.text: Nice setting but cocktails are mediocre at best. Also, take a look at the photo in the menu and what I actually got. Definitely not the same. But the waiter insisted it was. Oh well.

reviews.author: Like Clubs
reviews.text:
Great service, super nice wait staff (Klara, I hope I spelled it right? ) and quite decent tasting Costa coffee. Sure its not my first choice but this coffee with warm milk was quite good.
Overall, the place looks fantastic, you would never guess from outside that its that well designed and smart yet sophisticated look. All coffees come with a small cookie so that is a welcome gratuity.( well its in the price )

Will visit again for sure if we are in the nearby area.

reviews.author: Una Abramović
reviews.text: This bar and kitchen is a go-to for tasty food and creative cocktails. The vibe is lively and the staff make you feel like part of the family.

reviews.author: Jarmai Markai
reviews.text: This spot is perfect for casual drinks and tasty bites with friends. The staff are upbeat and the menu has something for everyone.

reviews.author: Catherine Nixon
reviews.text:
This is a lovely bar. It's one of the few open so late, closing at 2am.
The service was excellent and the prices were also excellent.
A nice outdoor space with tables. I only had drinks so can't comment on the food.

reviews.author: Erwin Rommel Generalfeldmarschall
reviews.text: One of the coolest cafe pubs in zagreb with a lot of cool and fresh people.

reviews.author: Mina Sonoko
reviews.text:
We stopped by Noho Coffee on our first day in Zagreb, and it was such a lovely experience!
coffee was rich absolutely delicious. The staff were super friendly and he recommended some great local restaurants to us. It really made our first day in the city extra special. Highly recommend this place!

reviews.author: jane jovanovski
reviews.text: A very nice place in the center of Zagreb… you can feel a cozy atmosphere around you… the staff is very polite.

reviews.author: Jon
reviews.text: Chic and inviting atmosphere. As others have noted, Neven is a hands on owner who understands service. My wife ordered a cocktail not on the menu, Neven disappeared for a second and came back from a store with an ingredient he didn’t have!  He spent several years in the US which gave us even more to talk with him about. We ended up staying longer than we planned which is always a good thing!  Thanks for a great evening and for the tip of using chickpea juice to make foam!!

reviews.author: ΣΠΥΡΙΔΟΥΛΑ ΕΛΙΣΣΑΒΕΤ ΚΑΡΑΤΖΕΝΗ
reviews.text: The best spot in Zagreb to drink coffee!! We loved it❤️ every morning we came here to drink coffee and then start our day!

reviews.author: Malik Ajala
reviews.text: Great atmosphere and by far the best espresso martini I’ve ever had!

reviews.author: Marija Stojkovska
reviews.text: Tender Zagreb is such a fun spot — small plates, cool vibes, and perfect for sharing with friends. Everything we tried was delicious, but the tiramisu was on another level… WOW!

reviews.author: Igor Mikloušić
reviews.text:
Tender Cocktail & Kitchen Club is one of those rare places that instantly pulls you in. The food is outstanding, the menu full of tasty, creative surprises, and the cocktails are right on point. The atmosphere has a warmth and energy that’s hard to describe but easy to feel—somewhere between comfort and excitement. The music selection rounds it out beautifully, always in tune with the moment.

Credit goes to Mirza, whose string of creative ventures seems to have been building up to this. With Tender, he’s truly come into his own. Supported by an incredible team in the kitchen and behind the bar, he’s created a place that feels both effortless and intentional.

And Tender doesn’t stop at great food and drinks—it has a weekly rhythm all its own apart  from daily specials that just hit right  day after day. Taco Tuesday is the after-work siesta you didn’t know you needed, Smash Wednesday is a hump day  burger fix done just right, Fridays and Saturdays buzz with a vibrant evening crowd, and the Late Breakfast Club on Saturdays is the weekend slow roll you’ll want to make a ritual.

It even got me doing something I thought I’d given up on—enjoying an aperitivo in Zagreb. I left feeling like I’d just rediscovered the city. This isn’t just a restaurant—it’s a club you genuinely want to be part of.

reviews.author: Jeevan Dhugga
reviews.text: Excellent outstanding food. The daily menu was served very prompt so great to catch a 10/10 working lunch while passing by. Highly recommended. GREAT CUSTOMER SERVICE and outstanding

reviews.author: Jana Ivančić
reviews.text:
I keep coming back to Tender as it quickly became one of my favorite spots in Zagreb. We came for the cocktails (which were really great) but were blown away by the food. Every dish was flavorful, fresh, and beautifully presented in a modern, minimalist way– especially the arancini and the caprese gyoza, a twist to a traditional caprese salad. They also offer great daily specials and a tasty Saturday brunch that’s well worth planning for.
The staff were warm and attentive, and the whole place has a relaxed, stylish vibe. A real treat from start to finish!

reviews.author: Josipa Boras
reviews.text: The food at Tender Bar is truly impressive – flavorful, well-balanced, and elegantly served. Each plate shows real attention to detail. Combined with the cozy, welcoming atmosphere, it’s a place that makes dining feel special.

reviews.author: Review Master
reviews.text:
Guys, hear me out!!! This is one of the best casual dining experiences in downtown Zagreb.

There were 5 of us, but there were also bigger groups. Nevertheless, waiting time was reasonable and the atmosphere great.

Food - beyond our expectations. Lately restaurants in Zagreb are not so good. It’s difficult to choose the best dish, but honorary mention goes to the desserts.

The service, especially our waiter Ian, was impeccable. He was so kind, professional, and helpful. Big thanks for making this night unforgettable, Ian.

reviews.author: Flora Kielland
reviews.text: The food was delicious! I had the eggplant (not a big beef eater but my friends loved the beef cheeks) and it was fabulous. The burrata appetizer was also tasty. Our server Luca was super friendly and helpful. Would definitely recommend if in Zagreb!

reviews.author: John Locke
reviews.text: The food and dining experience at Pod Zidom Bistro were all around excellent. Most notably, the meal was made exceptional by Luca, who took care of my party of four. He provided great recommendations, excellent service, and he was a wonderful host. My party and I had the tuna tartare and burrata to start, and for mains, I ordered beefsteak risotto, and sampled the beef cheeks and aubergine my party had (all of which Luca recommended), which were all so delicious. Beyond the excellent quality and taste of ingredients and preparation/presentation of the food, I would return to this location simply for Luca’s very commendable service.

reviews.author: Ruth Miller
reviews.text:
We had excellent service from our waiter. He was attentive and gave us good wine suggestions. We ate the beef cheeks and the whitefish. The beef cheeks melt in your mouth and the knoedells were unique with cheese inside! The dessert was phenomenal with homemade ice cream and apple tartine!
Definitely enjoyed our meal here!

reviews.author: Neil Rollinson
reviews.text:
We came to the restaurant only. The food was incredible, a gastronomic experience whilst here in Zagreb. The tartar and beef wrapped in cabbage were excellent, and the lamb gnocchi was so rich and delicious! I would highly recommend to anyone visting Zagreb.
Luka was exceptional in his service and so informative.

reviews.author: The Tech Expert
reviews.text: Cool place located in a quiet street with friendly staff and good music. Interior looks well-done with wooden accents and the terrace has retro chairs. Logo is well-designed with white monospaced text on a black background. The only disadvantage is that there is no non smoking area :(.

reviews.author: Marjan V.
reviews.text: This place has quickly become my favorite spot in town! The burgers are absolutely the best I've ever had – juicy, flavorful, and perfectly prepared. The staff is incredibly professional and attentive, always making sure you feel welcome and taken care of. On top of that, the music selection is just right – it sets a great atmosphere without ever being too loud. Highly recommended!

reviews.author: Petar Rastija
reviews.text: Hidden gem in one of many courtyards Zagreb has to offer. Nice atmosphere, nice and helpful staff but clearly understaffed, need more of them because the waiting to get served is way to long. Nice music. A bit higher prices than they should be. Toilets are the biggest downside, way to small for the amount of people. A great thing is their basement where they have great parties and great DJs, the only thing is, that it's way to small and get hot and overcrowded fast. Overall, a great place that i would recommend every time.

reviews.author: Attila Kiss
reviews.text: Too small, but in overall good place

reviews.author: Vicki Merkel
reviews.text: Great burger and fantastic fries!  There was a table waiter for drinks. Order food across the way.  Would be 5 for atmosphere if there was a marked non-smoking section. A terrific local find!

reviews.author: Goran Plavšić
reviews.text: Very nice bar with not to expensive beer and cool basement for live music

reviews.author: Henry Pesonen
reviews.text:
Jiggy – Music Bar & Food isn’t just a bar, it’s an experience. The beer selection is top-notch—cold, fresh, and carefully chosen. From smooth lagers to bold craft brews, every pint feels like quality in a glass. This is the place for anyone who takes their beer seriously.

But Jiggy isn’t only about what’s on tap—it’s about the atmosphere. As the night builds, the bar transforms into a high-energy club where the crowd, lights, and vibe keep you going until late. It’s the perfect mix of great drinks and a party that never slows down.

If you want a spot where amazing beer meets an unforgettable club scene, Jiggy is where you need to be. 🍺🔥

reviews.author: T-AMS
reviews.text: I went last night in this location as I was in the town centre and for a while I was planning a visit. The interiors are stylish and modern, and also a beautiful sitting outside. Considering it was happy hour until 8pm, I enjoyed a good craft beer for a competitive price. I will be back soon.

reviews.author: Andrej Golis
reviews.text: Nice and cozy bar with a very friendly stuff :) The beer is good!

reviews.author: J'nai Emery
reviews.text: This place is great! It is multiple floors with various seating styles, outdoor seating, and quite spacious. The drinks are good and the environment is even better. There is also a small club in the basement.

reviews.author: Lovro Sikiric
reviews.text: Beautiful caffe in the city centre. It has a great variety of soft and alcoholic drink you will definitely find what you love. Has many great cocktails. Service if very friendly and professional. Great to go drink coffee on the work days or just relax. Has a great gallery to sit and enjoy. The weekend is something different, its such a great a alive atmosphere. Would definitely recommend it.

reviews.author: Romana Iljegic
reviews.text: Located in the city center of Zagreb i would call it a perfect spot for a night out. Good wine offer, good music, professional staff and time is flying there .. what else can you wish for a perfect night out.. highly recommended, I think this is now one of my favorite places to go out.

reviews.author: Marino Paradina
reviews.text: Great coffee bar which is decorated in a vintage style (by that I mean a lot of wood decorations and designs). The service was a little bit slow and the coffee was alright.

reviews.author: Ljubica Banic
reviews.text:
Nice place, small but cosí.
A friend took me in there to celebrate her friend’s birthday.

People at the door were friendly,the music was good.
The toilets were clean but they didn’t have any  toilet roll.

reviews.author: Justin Mackiewicz
reviews.text: They refused to make me a boulevardier (it's a negroni with bourbon instead of gin). They had a negroni on there menu and had bourbon as well. I had to order the ingredients separately and make the drink at my table. Best worst bar experience I have ever had.

reviews.author: 오혜인
reviews.text:
Very kind and helpful staff!
The local beer they recommended was really nice.
Hvala!

reviews.author: Konstantinos Gkezepis
reviews.text:
We had some beers while resting from exploring the city.
The waitress was so kind and friendly and helped us choose the local beers.

reviews.author: David Gibbs
reviews.text: Stopped here for a quick pitstop, whilst sightseeing, excellent service, lovely craft beers and a very good taste in 80s music. Thank you

reviews.author: Evangelia K.
reviews.text:
Had beers at Carpe Diem twice, great spot! Big shoutout to Stjepan, the friendliest and most polite guy we met in Zagreb.
If you’re seeing this, Stjepan — greetings from the Greek guys!😄

reviews.author: Samantha Krewulak
reviews.text:
Fantastic bar for beer! There was more beer variety here than most places we've found in Zagreb, so we basically tried everything we hadn't heard of before and we were not disappointed.

Atmosphere is great and so was the service. Definitely a comfortable place to relax, enjoy beer, and maybe make some new friends.

reviews.author: Brenda Murphy
reviews.text: Delicious coffee! Love the place cozy and clean! Relaxing place to drink coffee! Rain of shine best to sit outdoor and watch people walking by.

reviews.author: Our adventures Around
reviews.text:
Great bar and restaurant.

Easy to find and acres of room in three different areas, perfect for large groups and couples.

Great selection of drinks, foods and wines.

If you enjoy a smoke then the great outside area, roof top terrace and rooms are all large areas.

reviews.author: Karlo V
reviews.text:
I had a pancake which was rich in flavour and the portion was more than enough for one person.
I also had "Orange Breeze" - apple, orange, carrot and ginger juice, which was delicious.
Prices equal the location because it is one of the most popular places in Zagreb for going out and spendig time with friends and family.
The waiting time was 20 minutes, but the polite and friendly waiter apologized a couple of times

reviews.author: Dimpy Jain
reviews.text: Super charged up amazing place. We just randomly walked in . Flawless preparation. Great staff. Loads youth enjoying.

reviews.author: Tomek Lewandowski
reviews.text: Just terrible. We were around 12, not many people and we waited for around 15-20 minutes to order and that was after we asked 3rd waiter if we can order. The other two promised to come and just disappeared. Also 30 seconds after ordering I wanted to change one of the things I ordered and somehow it was too much for them, it just wasn't possible... When I finally got my order, the bagel was completely burned :( Oh, and I waited 15 minutes to be able to pay. Stay out of this place

reviews.author: TheEmpurpler
reviews.text:
So many good things to say about this place-
Pros:
*their cheese plate we ordered had 3 cheese selections & each one got progressive better & better, especially the TRUFFLE cheese (it's been a few days since we had it & we're still thinking about it! So good!)
*they're open very late (2am) & even though we got there after midnight, they seated us & never rushed us even though we stayed til closing
*inside decor was cute & comfy & they have a large outdoor seating area
*they were patient with our tourist questions & uncertainty

Cons:
*they no longer have the chicken sausage that used to be on their old menu we saw in some else's review
*that's it - they have no other cons; everything was so good - go there!

reviews.author: CJW Sports Therapy
reviews.text: Booked fairly last minute, but I'm glad we did, as the place was buzzing by 9pm! Great selection of wines, and really helpful server, who recommended the platters & cheese to go with our chosen wines.

reviews.author: Lolita Sarkar
reviews.text: Amazing restaurant in Zagreb. All local produce! The meats, cheese and the cod pate were to die for. And once again drank the local red, a Shiraz . Did not disappoint! What was absolutely outstanding was the bread.

reviews.author: Ljubica Banic
reviews.text:
This place has outdoors and indoors tables in summer time I love to seat at the terrace.

The place has a big variety of Croatian wines from red, whites , sparkling and roses, the cheeses highly recommend to try. My favour one is the one with Tartuffe 😍.
The olives were too. Not counting the hams and  etc.
The service was impeccable and friendly
Prices are ok.

reviews.author: Robert Chomicz
reviews.text: A small restaurant located within walking distance of the main square and the Zagreb Cathedral. Nice selection of wine, cheese , charcuterie and bread . All wines are Croatian though not all the wines will be available. Prices are very reasonable and the wait staff really know the menu and the wines. Very pleasant experience.

reviews.author: Yordi G.
reviews.text:
We had already planned to visit here when we went to Zagreb. We figured we would have a few beers and maybe spend 1-1,5 hours but we ended up staying a good 4-5 hours because the atmosphere and the music were so nice and we felt very much 'at home'.

They have really nice local craft beers on tap from Nova Runda and also a variety of other European beers including very good Lord of the Rings themed beers from Mad Scientist.

This is a must visit if you like everything Tolkien, fantasy, board games, good beers and good vibes!

When we left the bartender even recommended a shop in Zagreb where they offer a very wide variety of craft beers.

We will definitely be back when we visit Zagreb again!

reviews.author: Tim Dreyer
reviews.text: Such a cool, unique little place. The Tolkien theme- an obsession of the owner- runs throughout the place. The outdoor area sits on a quiet corner across from an old church. Best pub we went to in Zagreb.

reviews.author: Scott Adams
reviews.text: Absolutely loved the vibes at Tolkien's House in Zagreb! Cozy, atmospheric, and full of character—it's a must-visit for any Tolkien or fantasy fan. The owner is incredibly friendly and welcoming. I had a great time chatting with him about The Lord of the Rings. You can tell this place is run by a true fan. Highly recommended!

reviews.author: Batuhan Işıklı
reviews.text: Safe heaven for any Tolkien enthusiasts! Atmosphere is incredible with lots of LOTR replicas & beer selection is also amazing. Speak friend and enter 😊

reviews.author: Martyn Crawshaw
reviews.text: The “real deal” in terms of a craft beer pub in Zagreb. Good selection of local beers in a quirky bar. Whether you are a fan of hobbits and the like or not, it is a real experience unlike some nearby “craft” bars

reviews.author: lisa haase
reviews.text:
Super cool place that we stumbled on because the other place we wanted to go to was closed, so glad it was!
It’s tucked away in an alley and used to be a horse stable for someone important, the bartender told us. The bartender was super friendly and the beer was the best we had in Croatia.  Unique bar stools too :)

reviews.author: Romain Chapuis
reviews.text: One of the best beer bar in old Zagreb center. Well located, great atmosphere and large choice of local and international beers

reviews.author: Lorena Šalković
reviews.text: This pub is a real gem—great beer selection, friendly staff, and a laid-back vibe that makes you want to stay for one more round.

reviews.author: Vinzenz Kager
reviews.text: Very nice place to have fine drinks, while hearing an outstanding music playlist.

reviews.author: Luka Savic
reviews.text: A cool pub in center of Zagreb. A bit boring if you want a lively environment. Come and have a pint to relax, but not to party hard. Toilet is as usual bad. Music is like some radio it's bad. Interior is good and cool to look at. Overall its a nice pub to come and have a pint. Cheers like if you think this is useful.

reviews.author: Kez Youinou
reviews.text: The glass area at the front of the pub is non-smoking and warm even in January. We asked if they could put the darts on one of their TV screens and there was no hesitation at all. Excellent, friendly service on several visits. Prices in line with others on the strip at about €9 for a beer and a 187ml bottle of wine.

reviews.author: Ivan Galović
reviews.text: Really great pub. Go upstairs for the best ambience! Really great pop/rock music, 70s, 80s, 90s...Great Guinness!

reviews.author: Edward Voskerichian
reviews.text: The Oliver Twist Pub in Zagreb is one of the many places on Ul. Ivana Tkalčića, which is the main street of Zagreb's nightlife. We sat on the terrace with a nice view of the street full of tourists for a break. The location is nice, it will most likely be nicer in the evening... the mise en place is simple and the service is good. The waiter also spoke English and we understood each other.

reviews.author: Sea wolf
reviews.text: They said it was a Irish bar for more then 20 years , I maybe should be called the Twist Oliver, nothing really Irish about it, staff had green T shirt on but no banter bar mat were red white and blue 🔵 , no real Irish lagers only Guinness, But it was a Monday evening, they serve food 🥑 and the menu was reasonable prices and  very good selection , didn't eat and left after one drink 🍻

reviews.author: Premyuda Sutthihara
reviews.text: We went to this bar because they have promotion 20% discount on draft beer. In the end they charged us full price and even lied that’s 500ml beer cost 6.20€. We paid 10.40€ for 2 beers… we even leaved tips and I checked later how much it’s actually cost because 6.20€ - 20% is not 5.20€ !!! It’s not about money, it’s at all but respects toward customers!!! They thought we are foreigners, but we lived in Zagreb and known to read Croatian !

reviews.author: Oleksandra Dzu
reviews.text: Lovely authentic pub right in the heart of the city. The staff are super friendly, the food is tasty and you can easily pay by card. Very cozy and spot to stop to enjoy a pint or two beer👌🏻

reviews.author: Julian Portelli
reviews.text: Amazing good. Taste, service and value for money

reviews.author: Людмила Маринова
reviews.text: A great place is currently advertising and is called Kozel. Despite the busy hour, they quickly found us a place for four. Admirations for the waitress - smiling, quick, with a sense of humor, explains well and takes care of everything. The food was extremely tasty and quickly prepared. Try the cream of mushroom soup and the blood sausage, you will be enchanted. I recommend the restaurant! We will be back again!

reviews.author: Irena S
reviews.text: Wanted to warm up so I got the tomato soup and orahovac.  Nice atmosphere. Food was ok.

reviews.author: Megas
reviews.text: I cannot say that I would recommend this restaurant. The food is quite average - not particularly tasty, though not bad either. Take a look at the burger; it doesn’t even appear appetizing. The beer is served in smaller quantities than expected. The restroom is extremely small, and the faucet only provides cold water, which is especially uncomfortable during the colder months. On the positive side, I can highlight the quick service.

reviews.author: Vjenceslav Erceg
reviews.text: Great place with great atmosphere. Excellent coffee and good beer selection. They also have pool and darts if you're into that... Worth a visit... Moderate prices too...

reviews.author: Milan Rajacic
reviews.text:
Nestled in a cosy nook of Zagreb's bustling city centre, just a leisurely stroll from the Croatian National Theatre and the vibrant streets of Savska and Frankopanska, you'll discover Legenda Pub. This charming establishment offers a tranquil escape under the cooling canopy of ancient horse chestnut trees, a welcome retreat from the summer heat.

As you wander down a few steps from the street, Legenda Pub reveals its character; a snug basement setting that invites you to settle in and stay a while. Despite the decor bearing the charming patina of yesteryear and a somewhat dimly lit ambiance, the atmosphere is anything but subdued. Legenda Pub compensates with its warm hospitality, affordable prices, and a selection of drinks that are both diverse and delightful.

The pub takes pride in serving quality coffee, a fine selection of beers, and, notably, exquisite wines from Iločki podrumi, with the renowned Graševina taking centre stage. Their Gemišt, a refreshing blend of white wine and sparkling water, is a must-try, offering a perfect antidote to the Croatian summer's warmth.

Legenda Pub embraces modern conveniences, accepting card payments seamlessly - a rarity in a country known for its quirky financial transactions. A little forewarning to the staff at the beginning of your order about your payment method ensures a smooth experience devoid of any hiccups.

The pub is a magnet for spontaneous gatherings, often drawing students from Dalmatia, which infuses the venue with a vibrant Mediterranean vibe. Thematic nights add further allure, from Italian evenings to country-themed celebrations, offering something for everyone.

But Legenda Pub's appeal doesn't rest solely on its beverages or events. It's a sanctuary from the urban flurry, with its serene terrace, and the kind of place where good company and conversation take centre stage. The pub's limited snack and draft beer options are easily overlooked, thanks to the quality of what is on offer.

Legenda Pub's charm lies in its simplicity and the genuine warmth of its service. Whether you're in for a quick coffee, a leisurely beer, or a lively party night, you'll find Legenda to be a place where memories are made. With a pool and darts available for those seeking a bit of friendly competition and a staff known for their humour and welcoming nature, it's the perfect spot for socialising.

In summary, Legenda Pub stands out as a testament to the joy of simple pleasures - great drinks, good company, and a welcoming atmosphere. It's a venue that invites you to relax, unwind, and feel at home, making it a must-visit for anyone looking to experience the heart of Zagreb's social scene. Whether it's for the superb coffee, the vibrant parties, or just a quiet spot to chat, Legenda Pub is a true legend in its own right, warmly recommended to all.

reviews.author: Leona Lasić
reviews.text: This pub is a fantastic spot to unwind with friends. Great selection of drinks and a warm, inviting atmosphere that keeps you coming back.

reviews.author: Ihor Haivan
reviews.text:
The pub Legenda, an unassuming little gem hidden away just a stone's throw from the bustling city center, offers a soothing escape from the heat and chaos of urban life. Set amid the lush greenery of ancient chestnut trees, this cozy establishment is the perfect spot to unwind with a glass of beer or a shot of raki, the local favorite spirit.

As you step into the cool shade of the pub, the natural ambience and friendly service immediately make you feel at home. The lack of snacks on the menu is easily forgiven when you experience the refreshing quality of the beverages on offer. While the selection of draft beer may be limited to just one type, the chosen brew is a delightful and satisfying accompaniment to the laid-back atmosphere of the pub.

The simplicity of Legend Pub's offerings is one of its greatest charms. The unpretentious, low-key vibe allows patrons to focus on what truly matters - enjoying a refreshing drink and good conversation in a serene, welcoming environment. This is a place where visitors can relax, mingle with locals, and truly experience the essence of the city's character.

In conclusion, Legend Pub is a delightful oasis for those seeking a break from the bustling city center. Though the food and drink options may be limited, the quality and atmosphere more than make up for it. If you find yourself in the area, be sure to stop by for a glass of beer or a shot of raki, and soak in the tranquility that only the shade of chestnuts can provide.

reviews.author: Stevo Rajevski
reviews.text: The bar is located very close to the Croatian National Theatre and the city centre but is away from all the crowds. The furnishing is very much outdated and the the atmosphere is quite dark but the place makes it up with super affordable prices and decent service. It often has happy hour specials on coffee and liquor.

places.name: Pivnica Mali Medo
places.address: Tkalčićeva 36 Street, Ul. Ivana Tkalčića 36, 10000, Zagreb, Croatia

reviews.author: Maja Lazarova
reviews.text:
THE greatest place - must visit in Zagreb! Excellent food, great beer, but best of all: the waiter Mario! Cheerful, singing, dancing, totally enjoying his work and making a great atmosphere for all guests in the pub!
Dear manager/s, consider offering him a promotion and pay rise! You cannot afford loosing staff like him. He is a real gem!
Thanks for the excellent service Mario❤️

reviews.author: Eran I
reviews.text:
Pivnica Mali Medo on Zagreb's main and vibrant Tkalčićeva Street is a place you can't miss. The location is perfect - right on the central entertainment street, with a large terrace that lets you soak up the bustling atmosphere. The food focuses on traditional Croatian pub dishes, which felt good, generous, and almost homemade. I especially recommend trying dishes like fried chicken fingers.
Despite the crowds, the service was kind and friendly, and the waitress was very efficient and kind.
In summary, this is a great place to combine quality beer, tasty, reasonably priced Croatian food, and a central location with a lively atmosphere in the heart of Zagreb.

reviews.author: Latoyaa Roberts
reviews.text: Cool nice place that I grabbed a meal on News Year's eve considering many places are closed. The staff was friendly and spoke English. The meal was well prepared and the beer was good.

reviews.author: Robert Hlavatý
reviews.text: Beer is good, food is normal, service is friendly and slow (I mean not lazy, they just can't cover it when the pub is full). Nice place for drinking.

reviews.author: Anna Famili
reviews.text:
We had the minced Ćevapčići with fries and the pork medallions in mushroom sauce with croquettes. The Ćevapčići tasted good and were well prepared. However, I wouldn’t recommend the pork medallions at all. The meat didn’t taste fresh, and the flavor was very unpleasant. The sauce could have been much better as well.

The atmosphere was lively, and the staff were friendly and attentive.

reviews.author: High
reviews.text: Nice little Irish pub. Beautiful and welcoming owner Adrian. Good draft and bottled beers. Lovely ambience with friendly approachable local people.

reviews.author: Graham Carlton
reviews.text: Great beer prices, proper pub like atmosphere and a brilliant place to watch English Premier League football, staff were brilliant, will go again soon

reviews.author: PHILIP BAKER
reviews.text:
"THE BEST IRISH BAR IN ZAGREB"

A truly great bar all round,
from there great selection of beers and craft ales, too there helpful and friendly staff.

Definitely worth a stroll out of the city centre.

And quite possibly the cheapest prices in the city.

reviews.author: why don't you just drop dead
reviews.text: Finally a pub without the typical stout you'd usually find in Irish pubs around Europe. Really friendly owner in this lovely pub. If you're in Zagreb you should spare some time for a drink here.

reviews.author: Petra Petrović
reviews.text: This pub nails the Irish vibe with cozy corners and a great selection of drinks. The staff are friendly and there’s always a lively crowd. Perfect spot to unwind after work.

reviews.author: C Cutler
reviews.text: Atmospheric pub. That’ll make them like this anymore. Very friendly bartender. Smoky but there are non-smoking areas. A couple of TVs for sports enthusiast.

reviews.author: Sören Breunig
reviews.text: Great live music and a fantastic atmosphere! The owner and staff are genuine and very friendly. Definitely worth a visit!

reviews.author: Bart Bernaerts
reviews.text: We went there to celebrate new year with a group of 5 foreigners and although there were a lot of other foreigners they only played Croatian music as from 23 P.M.… The program said Disco, 70’s, 80’s, 90’s and CRO Trash… but basically it was only the latter. A disappointment on a New year’s evening. The owner was friendly but also blabla when a foreigner said something about the music. The food was good and the staff was friendly. Certainly also a fair price for food & drinks (all in formula). The place is a bit too small though to host a big crowd for dinner & dance. And like every Croatian bar: although it’s prohibited to smoke inside Croatian people smoke like hell in bars & pubs! You smell terrible when you leave. If you aren’t Croatian I would choose another place for a New year’s party (because of the music)

reviews.author: Gary Scanlon
reviews.text: Lovely old school bar. Too much cigarette smoke for us though. Only stayed for one drink and moved on. If you're a drinker and a smoker this place might be heaven for you 😁

reviews.author: Ante
reviews.text: Very bad experience last Saturday; impolite staff not giving you glasses with this promo wine in IKEA bottle! Had no idea what we drank, but the strong headache next day was there. Extremely nice interior of the place but...

reviews.author: Nika Sabljo
reviews.text: Great pub in the city centre. Beer prices are solid. Smoking is allowed in the whole space. It is a self service pub and they take both cash and card.

reviews.author: Lia Kaza
reviews.text: Such a great place to feel Zagreb's young spirit enjoying a great beer in the local pub. Definitely recommend

reviews.author: Josh Baltazar
reviews.text: Came here at 1:30 pm, on a Tuesday. We were the only ones there. The place is self service so you order at the front counter. Really nice draft Czech beer poured with really nice foam. We also tried to order a latte since it was on the menu but the bartender said only espressos. So we just had the one drink and checked out another place.

reviews.author: Tamara Čuček
reviews.text: Waitress was very rude, decided to close before official closing hours with plenty of insults on our way out because we tried to order 40mins before closing. Old hags not coming back anytime soon.

reviews.author: Ricardo Gonzalez
reviews.text:
This is a self service bar, I don't have anything against that, but the bartenders that helped me were rude.
At least the beer is cheap and the terrace is nice.

EDIT: OMG! Even rude to my review! I am not a tourist, I live in Zagreb for many years and the bar had many tourists around me the day I was there. Anyway that is the wrong mentality to run a business. Terrible place and terrible management!

reviews.author: Jozef Jonas
reviews.text: Large pub, great selection of beer (including beer-to-go), very good meal, excellent service. Non-smoking pub!!! Very hard to find in Zagreb, you can smoke outside on large terrace.

reviews.author: Ray Chen
reviews.text:
Very nice found, friendly service and tasty dishes. There is one chain in the walking street with pubs and restaurants but this one is more in the local neighborhood, quite popular with locals.

Good vibes and we were there on Saturday, live music from 8pm!

Would definitely recommend and will be back again for sure.

reviews.author: Rob
reviews.text:
Good food with tasty beer!

I really enjoyed the pilsner and one of my friends had the 'wheat beer' which had a good taste to it.

The food was tasty albeit a little bland as there wasn't much sauce.

The service was okay.

I would definitely come back for drinks!

reviews.author: Tomo Mo
reviews.text:
Fantasric food, great beer.
You can also buy there specialty beers in a bottle. Great gift from Zagreb.

reviews.author: Jackie Hamilton-Smith
reviews.text:
What a fantastic restaurant with amazing food and service!

Someone recommended Pivnica Medvedgrad Ilica to us and I'm so glad she did! The food was delicious and full of flavour and our server was super helpful with fantastic tips about the menu. Our server recommended trying their pastries to start: a cheese filled croissant and pork-rind flavoured biscuit... WOW they were amazing! I wish I'd bought loads of them to take home! We also tried a mince meat spread on toast and a charcuterie board with local cheeses that was excellent. The burgers were also delicious. Our server, Ivan I believe, also went through the beer menu in detail and made great recommendations for us.

I'm so glad we came here and tried so many different lovely things. It was fab.

Thank you again for a wonderful meal and experience!

reviews.author: Marin Srok
reviews.text: Always a good atmosphere. They have great draft and bottled beer options. Music is rock and metal mix 🔥🔥🔥. Good service. There is a restaurant nearby so you can bring in food.

reviews.author: Alex Mounsey
reviews.text: Great spot for food and drink! Order food on the left and drinks on the right. Got the meat platter fir 3 and an appy tray

reviews.author: Chris G
reviews.text:
Amazing rock / metal themed bar in Zagreb. When I walked in Metallica was playing and I knew I found my new favorite bar.
Food was incredible.  I had cevapi,  a huge portion for the 8 euro price.
Beer was great, they have some good Croatian beers on tap, and the manager Zvonimir was a great guy. All the servers spoke excellent English for my Croatian impaired wife. On the weekends they have free live metal shows.
I'm really looking for my next trip to Zagreb.  Seriously....I'd live here if I was still leaving in Zagreb!!

reviews.author: Michael Faggetter
reviews.text: Great spot for a pint or to watch a game. Cool barbershop there too. Well worth a vist just to see the decor. Food was brilliant and reasonably priced. Staff were welcoming and great craic. Would definitely vist again

reviews.author: Samui Gohan
reviews.text:
Ordered a 'Fungi' pizza, received a pie with thick dough, covered with extremely salty cheese (or a cheese substitute) which made it barely edible. There was almost no mushrooms on the pizza as shown on the photo, just a very few very thin slices. You could not smell or taste the mushrooms, that's how little there was.

I might give them another chance, but definitely not with any kind of pizza.

In response to bff's response - I had other pizzas in Zagreb and all over the world, either it is not the same cheese or someone dropped a salt shaker in it. My point is that it was not quite edible.

reviews.author: K
reviews.text: This place was tucked away inside of a neighborhood. Our last meal out before returning to the states. The food was great. I ordered the Riley medium well. It came with fries, I also ordered what I thiught was a side of mixed vegetables. I received a bowl, they were so good. The wife ordered the steak pasta with black truffles. It looked very good, she was pleased with her meal. We had a bottle of Korlat syrah wine to complement the meal.

reviews.author: Vlaho Ljubic
reviews.text: Great quality of all kinds of food. Steak platter is a must. Only objection is lack of waiters which results in slower service and their "happiness" but they are giving their best

reviews.author: Ivana Živičnjak
reviews.text: Very nice and modern restaurant with free parking right next door.  Pleasant staff, not intrusive.  The food was great for the first time, we tried skirt steak and veal chops and everything was really delicious.  unfortunately there was no room left for dessert 😀 great portions so we are coming back for sure

reviews.author: Go Zagreb
reviews.text:
*Nov 2021* - Newly opened restaurant in a popular location.
We went to try the restaurant/pub as soon as it opened and we were pleasantly surprised by the simple but modern interior and basic but rich menu. The waiters do their job properly, the prices are more than decent. And the food is VERY VERY delicious. Additionally the Live music is one of the reasons we will be back. See you soon :) 🍷

reviews.author: Iva
reviews.text:
Great place to have food and drinks with live music. Small dance floor but with right type of guests you can create train across whole restaurant - we experienced it - 10/10.
Great for bridal shower and birthdays because guests are little bit older, app 28+.

reviews.author: Ivan Marić Šperac
reviews.text:
There is no food offer but all of the drinks they have is really more than enough! It's my pleasure to come here, drinking with friends or even by my own. Atmosphere is always really cool! Mostly younger crowd but even those older people seemed to be chill and funny
Definitely recommend!

reviews.author: wanda C
reviews.text:
I LOVEEEEE THIS PUB!
Great music and you don't need to yell to be heard by your friends at the table LOL!
The owner, Hvartin is the COOLEST guy EVER! I'll definitely party here each time I visit Zagreb...

reviews.author: Wendeth R
reviews.text: Owner and staff I've met are super cool. I was recommended the  Pulfer Hefenwiezen brewed here in Zagreb... Local beer at a local pub can't be beat.

reviews.author: Anka Svagusa
reviews.text: The cafe is really great.  The waiters are friendly, I really enjoyed the atmosphere.  It's also where memorable drunken parties take place!

reviews.author: Laoi Ó Murchú
reviews.text: Always a great time here. Shame it's harder to get to during warmer weather; a hike down nasip doesn't appeal so much in 30 degrees :)

reviews.author: BREZA
reviews.text:
All in all.... Royal indeed! I must emphasise great service - from info about beers to serving them - top notch! 🍀🇮🇪🏆
A Great place you gotta visit!

reviews.author: Augustina Salopek Bogunovic
reviews.text:
The main thing is, here you can drink the best Guinness in Zagreb! Everything said💚💚💚
Maybe expensive, or not, depends how your brain works...maybe crowded but that's good, right!?
...service is great, staff is realy trying to give best even during the night like these in St Patrick weekend. Thank you guys!

Love that place!

reviews.author: Laurence Alexander
reviews.text: I come here often. Great guiness, pool and whiskeys. Super nice bartenders. Great vibe

reviews.author: taggart lewis
reviews.text: Best Irish coffee I’ve ever had! Lots of care went into making this drink and that’s why I love to try new places. The bartender was very friendly. Thanks!

reviews.author: Tomislav Ivanetic
reviews.text: Although nice pub, smokers ruined my morning coffee so I doubt to come here again even I ment since beer and whiskey offer is great.

reviews.author: Tomislav Kralj
reviews.text:
✅️Excellent brewery!!
✅️Nice and very tasty home brewed beers.
✅️Lot's of places to sit.
✅️Fast and capable staff.
➡️ If you want a nice place to talk to your friends and grab a beer, be sure to visit!

reviews.author: Jasna Radošević
reviews.text: Teacher's Pub Brewery is the kind of place you want to hang out in all evening. The house brews are full of flavor and the pub has a friendly, laid-back vibe.

reviews.author: Martyn Crawshaw
reviews.text: Disappointing although there was a range of different beers   All were trying to be copies of other countries own beers. Service was slow, it was a Sunday afternoon. Not what most people would recognise as a “pub”!

reviews.author: Nenad Miksa (DoDo)
reviews.text:
A nice cozy place next to one of the Zagreb's busiest streets, while still having a terrace in a park next to a walkway nearby.

I went there to have a drink with my friends so I didn't try any of the food.
The drinks are the usual for Zagreb - nothing special.
All in all, it's a great place to spend some time.

reviews.author: Anton Chirkov
reviews.text:
Really nice place with atmosphere and good looking tables and chairs.
Worst point our service guy was saying that he is going home and this is why he brought us a bill but he continued to work.
Also all the beer which I asked for was not in service...

First time I met such ugly service in Croatia.

reviews.author: Ljubiša Kukulj
reviews.text: Probably the best pub in the town. Cozy, relaxed and beautiful atmosphere. We will for sure become regular guests over there. True value for money with great stuff members! Love it!

reviews.author: Nathan Szeliga
reviews.text: I give it a 5 stars I wish I could give it a 6 or 7. Best bar in Zagreb definitely worth the visit

reviews.author: Rona Jankovic
reviews.text: Great pub, great beer, the best owner! 10/10

reviews.author: Samantha Krewulak
reviews.text: Great place. It's funny to get to, you almost feel like you've taken a wrong turn, but you haven't. It's a great bar, comfort atmosphere, and the owner is super kind, funny, and helpful to locals and tourists alike. Definitely recommend for a nice cold beer and great pub atmosphere.

reviews.author: David Mandić
reviews.text: Cheap beer and other drinks, "tavern" atmosphere. Not the most charming place but excellent for binge drinking, celebrating old friendships and forming new ones. Especially in colder months - it's always warm inside.

reviews.author: Dinko Jurisic
reviews.text:
Great place ... perfect atmosphere... cool owners.
If you are looking for a place to spend a relaxed evening with a pint of great beer or some other drink this is aplace to be. It would be a shame to visit Zagreb and miss this authentic spot
100% a recommendation :)

reviews.author: Mark
reviews.text: The most friendly bar in Zagreb.  Amazing beer and the music takes you back to a happy era.  Come and enjoy.

reviews.author: Ivan Pavrlišek
reviews.text: Buzzing and vibrant small pub with various different craft beer options. Good music and cold beer! In summer there is option to sit out at patio. Smoking allowed inside, so if don’t like it avoid it during cold days when their terrace is closed.

reviews.author: katarina nekic
reviews.text: Their indoor setup has everything that one pub has to have. Even if you come alone, be sure you will not be bored, and only by looking at the wall photographs you will feel like you're travelling around the world. All that during listening great music and enjoying your drink. Highly reccomended.👍

reviews.author: Tena Tomić
reviews.text: Pivnica Bobi & Rudi is a great spot to grab a cold beer and relax with friends. The pub has a welcoming atmosphere and a solid selection of drinks.

reviews.author: Arantxa Ledesma
reviews.text: A nice place to spend a chill time. The drinks are good but nothing too special, prices are fair. The really stand out thing from there for me are the street tables, it’s a great sitting spot in hot summer days. Can’t wait to go back.

reviews.author: Mia
reviews.text: One of the most wonderful cafes in Zagreb. The atmosphere is very cozy and relaxing. It's pretty quiet in there and the workers are also very kind. I recommend this place if you want to come study in peace, have a simple hangout with some friends, or even just relax!

reviews.author: Dora Bobanovic
reviews.text: Great bar in Zagreb to go for a coffee or a drink. The decor is super unique and they have a wide range of seating options. Note that this is an indoor bar where smoking is permitted, so if this bothers you, you maybe would want to consider coming.

reviews.author: Ana Ercegovic Berovic
reviews.text: Cosy and welcoming place, specially decorated. Coffee is really good and the guy that works there is extremely polite. It’s a nice vibe and perfect place to relax and have a coffee or tea break. It’s also cool to sit by the window seat so you can be inside and still look outside, it’s quite charming I would say.

reviews.author: Ivan Šarić
reviews.text:
A wonderful and pleasant place for coffee. The ambiance is truly lovely, with beautifully designed and interesting decor that creates a cozy and inviting atmosphere. It’s the perfect spot to relax and enjoy your coffee.

The staff was exceptionally kind and polite, providing excellent service throughout the visit. The coffee itself was very good.

I will definitely return here. Highly recommended for anyone looking for a charming spot to enjoy coffee.

reviews.author: Juliet Daniel
reviews.text: Delicious coffee, great cake, and lovely atmosphere. Although more expensive than other cafes, the coffee is the best I've had in Zagreb. The staff are really helpful when buying coffee beans as well. When we were visiting, they were also roasting the coffee right there. Definitely worth the visit for great quality coffee.

reviews.author: Dora Bobanovic
reviews.text: Great specialty coffee in Zagreb that not only does coffee but also cocktails. Their espresso martinis are great! The cafe has plenty of seating options including a closed terrace, indoor, and a few outdoor seating options for smokers since this is a non smoking cafe. They do offer plant based milks at a price. They have some food but not many options.

reviews.author: Luiz Fernando Sardelli
reviews.text: Quahwa is indeed the best café in Zagreb in my opinion as a coffee lover. They make a good sort of drinks as well! The staff is kind and the place is cozy! While in Zageb, I came here several times to enjoy not only the coffee, but also its atmosphere.

reviews.author: Filiz Celebi
reviews.text: As a fan of strong coffee, I was really happy to find Turkish coffee here. It’s rich, well balanced, and easily one of the best Turkish coffees you can find locally in Croatia. I’d highly recommend it to heavy coffee lovers who want to try something different.

reviews.author: Pedro Miguel Pires
reviews.text: Really nice place in Zagreb! Great atmosphere, super friendly staff, and everything tastes great — both the coffee and the food. Perfect spot to relax, work, or catch up with friends. Definitely one of my favorite coffee spots in the city!

reviews.author: Derrick Deans
reviews.text: This is a small funky bar and café in lower town but near the edge of upper town. It’s a nice place to stop for coffee, cocktails, beer, or wine.

reviews.author: Rexety
reviews.text: Went here during our Zagreb visit. Very cool interiors and cute place to hang out on a drink. Outside seating available as well. Juices were fresh and coffee was good. Thing to note is that they do not have any food options to order. They serve only drinks - coffee, juices, wine, cocktails, shots & beer.

reviews.author: Ákos Balogh
reviews.text: A special place, definitely worth a visit.  It takes a bit of luck to get a free seat, but it's worth the wait.  It's a peaceful, cozy bar, I liked that it's not too bright inside, so it's more intimate.  The drink menu could perhaps be more extensive, but this does not detract from its value.

reviews.author: Gorana Cicek
reviews.text: Cozy place with tasty coffee and lovely staff, especially waitress. Safe for kids and dogs as well as they're on the no car pathway. Great place for evening drink as well.

reviews.author: Simon Honnor
reviews.text: We visited here one afternoon while holidaying in Zagreb. The staff were lovely,  friendly, and overall service was good. We had flat whites and they were pricey and quite average I’m afraid. More like weak cappuccinos. Still, loved the atmosphere and chilled vibe here.

reviews.author: Yaua
reviews.text: Love grabbing a coffee here—the vibe is chill and the staff are always friendly. It’s the ideal spot for a quick break or catching up with friends.

reviews.author: Tajana Belković
reviews.text: I was very impressed with how friendly the staff were, coffee was good too and not mega expensive. Dogs are welcome and the water bowl was readily provided. I would definitely recommend this place, especially this time of year.

reviews.author: Steven Baric
reviews.text: Cool local cafe!

reviews.author: Vojtěch Šanca
reviews.text: At first sight this place looked like a tourist trap but when we got in we were positively surprised. The coffee was average but they have quite extensive selection of good cocktails (I have tried several of them) for acceptable prices. The interior is quite cosy and comfy just the staff was quite poor. If you want to order something you have to go to the bar just because the waiter didn't care.

reviews.author: Deniz Ovalıoğlu
reviews.text: We went at 22 on a Sunday night for a coffee/cocktail. The atmosphere seemed very nice, but we were quickly disappointed by the fact that they do not serve coffee despite the name and the menu. Later we decided to order cocktails and despite our polite demeanor the waiter rudely insisted that we ordered a drink that we never ordered (we got corona beer instead of a Negroni, and the waiter walked away). Paloma tasted okay. Would not recommend this place.

reviews.author: Ellh Chris
reviews.text: Quality cafe. Polite staff. Very good capuccino. The hot chocolate was so real. I haven't tasted such a chocolate for a long time. The bar inside is well decorated. It is not far from the centre but in a walkable distance. Tram 11 and 12 can get you there. Excellent choice to get your afternoon coffee.

reviews.author: Magz Bee
reviews.text:
Located a short 10 minute walk from the main square, Cajt is a well know old-style Zagreb boozer with friendly atmosphere and great selection of craft beer, whiskeys and an occasional rum. You’ll meet the locals and drink like a local. And just wait till you see that old-school interior. Home of the world’s smallest bar booths!
Note: There is no food here, it’s an error in the bio.

reviews.author: Paul Porrelli
reviews.text:
What a cool spot. Hidden behind tinted windows that you shouldn't fear. Once inside, you'll feel an old school bar with decent beer on tap, wide selection of beers and niche beer sipping commune. Such a cool place to catch a few beers.

Prices were reasonable and music was tasteful.

reviews.author: Hrabren Dobrotić
reviews.text: A legendary bar in the centre of Zagreb that has almost a small cult following. Augustiner and local craft beer on tap. Very good bottle selection. There's often something new to try for beer lovers. Great staff, amusing and professional.

reviews.author: da2handz
reviews.text: Rated 5* for beer. Always good brew on tap, also a craft beer fridge. Best atmosphere in town.

reviews.author: Ricardo Leguizamon
reviews.text:
Outstanding specialty coffee in Zagreb
Cogito Coffee is the place to go if you value true specialty coffee. They brew with V60 and offer beans from different origins — including El Salvador, Ethiopia, Sofia, and even projects from Colombian women who shifted from coca to coffee farming.

The atmosphere is simple yet welcoming, and the staff are genuinely kind. On my second visit, two baristas were working, and the service was just as pleasant as the first day. Watching the preparation is part of the experience: every variable — water temperature, pouring technique, timing — is carefully managed, and the result is a cup that fully justifies the price.

Highly recommended for anyone who wants to enjoy coffee prepared with real expertise in Zagreb.

reviews.author: Pii
reviews.text: The latte was really good. The atmosphere was lively, but I was able to work without any issues. The Wi-Fi was okay , there were power outlets available, and they accept card payments, so it felt very convenient and reassuring. Thank you for the lovely environment:)

reviews.author: Lera Chegge
reviews.text: Excellent coffee and very good service! I took V60 and pack of coffee home. Everything is nice. Moreover there is a calm place with sunny and shaded places in the yard and the inside tables too.

reviews.author: Murat Uder
reviews.text: Well a nice coffee shop in the city center which opens early in the morning compared to some other shops. The location of the map is correct, but hard to see the cafe once you are there. Look out for the sign of the shop in my photos. Coffee can be improved from my point of view. They accept cards. There is a clean toilet within the shop. Enjoy.

reviews.author: Josh Le
reviews.text: The shop is a very cool place to hang out there. The owners/waiters are very nice and the croissants we could self-service. Its a lovely place. The Coffee is also very special, bit more sour then I might used to...

reviews.author: Freely HR
reviews.text:
I recently had brunch at Hotel Capital’s Bela Café, and I can honestly say that I have never had a better brunch in Zagreb — or beyond.

Everything was absolutely perfect. The atmosphere is elegant yet relaxed, making it ideal for a weekend brunch or a special gathering. The interior is beautiful, the setting feels luxurious, but at the same time very welcoming.

The food was outstanding. Every dish was prepared to perfection, beautifully presented, and full of flavor. You can truly taste the quality of the ingredients. The menu offers a great variety, and everything we tried exceeded expectations.

What impressed me just as much was the service. The staff was professional, attentive, and extremely friendly. The entire experience felt smooth, warm, and high-end.

The prices are more than fair for what you get. Considering the quality, presentation, ambiance, and top-level service, the value is exceptional.

I would highly recommend this place to everyone who lives in Zagreb or is visiting the city. This is definitely Top 1 — a place that simply must not be missed.

reviews.author: Dewi Aldrich
reviews.text: Beautiful, open, airy space with ample seating. Gorgeous décor. Extremely comfortable area. Excellent and accommodating service by Filip. He is cute too!

reviews.author: Filip Parotti
reviews.text: The food was amazing, staff were super friendly and made the whole experience really enjoyable. Would definitely recommend!

reviews.author: Ross Crawford
reviews.text: Excellent cafe in a relaxing setting. Staff is no short of exceptional. Returned to the hotel at 10:52PM and thought we had time for a quick night cap before bed (bar closes at 11PM). Despite the bar being empty and close up already close to completed, Magdalene had no issues serving up a quick round of drinks. We were quick and polite. No bad energy was felt for a slightly later close out and my party really appreciated that.

reviews.author: NicholasRose
reviews.text: This cafe is a cozy spot to relax with a coffee and a pastry. The staff are always friendly and make you feel right at home.

reviews.author: Katia Delta
reviews.text:
