The interior of the cafe is beautiful and quiet. The staff were friendly and helpful. The food was amazing and was freshly made which made it more delicious.
If you are passing through, I highly recommend it to take a break and your coffee and try either savoury or sweet dishes.( I would tell you to eat both because you will go crazy)

reviews.author: Souzie Karagiannidou
reviews.text: The chocolate cake was tasty and unique. The big latte was excellent without the waitress burning my milk. Quiet environment and very good service!

reviews.author: Ihor Konobas
reviews.text:
Great place with tasty coffee. Chai latte is the best of all that I’ve had in Zagreb.
Cozy terrace and welcoming interior.

reviews.author: Ngoc Mai Vu
reviews.text: very small but cozy cafe, the drinks are fine, overall i like it

reviews.author: Sara Rivera
reviews.text: Beautiful place, nice relaxed ambience, great food and service. We tried the salmon and prosciutto sandwiches and the coffee. The coffee is great, from Latin America. You should consider having coffee from Honduras :)

reviews.author: Esanuclaudio
reviews.text:
Cat Caffe Zagreb is Croatia’s first and most charming cat café, where you can enjoy a range of creative drinks while spending time with friendly resident cats in a relaxed, playful setting. Entrance is free — you only pay for what you order.

The drink menu includes affordable options like espresso from around €1.46, milk coffee €1.73–€1.86, latte and iced coffee around €2.12, and tea around €2.39. There are also fun specialty drinks like the Caticorn latte (about €3.70). Overall, most visitors spend around €5–10 per person on drinks and the experience.

The café’s signs, menus, and general information are in English, so it’s easy for international visitors to understand.

Everything about my visit was great — the drinks, the vibe, and the cats themselves — except the cleanliness of the café, which could be improved. Replacing the current seating with more hygienic plastic chairs would make the space safer and more comfortable.

In short, it’s a unique and relaxing spot to enjoy coffee and feline company, perfect for cat lovers visiting Zagreb, with minor improvements in hygiene needed.

reviews.author: Pam Bo
reviews.text: I found my favourite place in all of Zagreb! The place is super chill and the staff were super friendly. Our hot chocolates were fantastic and we really enjoyed spending time with all of the cats in this place. Highly recommend going there if you're visiting Zagreb!

reviews.author: Haris Demir
reviews.text: As the name of the place tells it, it's the cat café so you can be their guest in a very relaxing atmosphere surrounded by these lovely animals. It's their playground! Staff are also great and friendly, thumbs up they do great job too making everyone feel welcome and keeping the place well maintained. Coffee is great!

reviews.author: Codrin Gherman
reviews.text:
Got to say, haven't found anything I can fault it.

Price, to service and food. 10/10 cats also.

Strangest thing to have Nu Metal play in a cat cafe, but I was enjoying it.

Definitely swinging by again next time I'm in Zagreb.

reviews.author: Catalina Bogzoiu
reviews.text:
If you’re a cat lover, this might be the place for you. There are many cats in the café, relaxing on chairs or in their own dedicated spots. During my visit, most of them were sleeping, which made the atmosphere calm and cozy. I appreciated that they have a clear set of rules about how to interact with the cats, as it helps set healthy boundaries for visitors.
A small tip: avoid wearing black, as you’ll likely leave covered in cat hair. A few of the cats looked like they might have been unwell, but I trust the owners are taking proper care of them.

reviews.author: Višnja Gizdić
reviews.text: Very pleasant to sit. Calm atmosphere. They also have coffee to go. They make their own plant based milk and sell vegan bites.

reviews.author: veatriki mos
reviews.text: Amazing 👏 I wish this coffee shop existed in Greece. Such cozy vibe, the staff is so friendly and everything we tried was amazing. Vegan hot chocolate, vegan croissant and vegan banana bread, all delicious

reviews.author: Annette Franklin
reviews.text: This place has such good coffee and fresh baked goods! The Pumpkin spice Latte was so yummy! Highly recommend, staff is very nice and service is very quick.

reviews.author: Hassan Salah
reviews.text:
An absolute gem! The most central yet non touristy place I can think of.
Really cosy, clean and the staff and owner are so friendly and nice.
They have a variety of options and nice goodies to have with your drink. Vegan friendly, aware and conveniently located and very reasonably priced. I think I found the perfect place for a nice coffee in Zagreb.

reviews.author: Cheima BO
reviews.text:
Cute spot and love the local touch.
Nice selection of homemade items.
They recommended I try the homemade milks. I did cocoa with hazelnut was very nice! I missed that the pastry was vegan which is not for me. The staff is super nice!

reviews.author: Sue Rafsin
reviews.text: Most shops are closed on Sunday and Public Holiday. Kudos toChiaCups Shop&Coffee Bar for being opened. The place is instagramable, to chill and have a nice cup of hot drink.  Didn’t have a chance for a cup of coffee as the machine is down. Staff are friendly and welcoming.

reviews.author: Ava Jade
reviews.text: Lovely cafe for a coffee, tea or juice. The handmade ceramics are beautiful and you can purchase them as well. It's a calm, inviting environment with delicious coffee. Oat milk is available for vegans or anyone who can't drink milk.

reviews.author: Lemonade Milkshake
reviews.text: Super cute little coffee shop w/ the neatest display of ceramic cups & saucers in their back room! (available for purchase) Pleasant atmosphere & very nicely decorated & unkept. Thanks for my chai! 🍵

reviews.author: Sunny Berlin
reviews.text:
What a pleasant surprise!
We just stopped in to grab a quick coffee near the train station, and we were blown away by the atmosphere.
Mostly the design (which you can see in the photos)

The shop seems to also double as an Atelier for ceramics (the entire back wall was composed of piece for sale), and is a lot like something you would find in Berlin Mitte or Prenzlauerberg.

The service staff was super friendly (not arrogant like you might expect) and the prices were also fair.

reviews.author: Vlad K
reviews.text:
We had been hesitating to visit this place for a long time and finally, we made it there and honestly, we should have come much earlier.

Although the place isn’t that old, the interior already worn out. Some of the design choices clearly didn’t take into account that everything would be used daily. The chairs, for example, look heavily worn and not very clean. The overall concept is understandable bright black polka dots covering the entire space create a strong “insta factor”.

But in my opinion, it wasn’t the best long-term decision. The atmosphere feels a bit flashy and even slightly off-putting.

The coffee itself is good in both price and quality.

They also sell their branded cups on-site, which are the same ones used to serve drinks.

However, this creates a kind of anti-advertising effect.

The cups don’t seem suitable for frequent dishwashing, as they already look quite worn, noticeably scratched and peeling off.

It’s hard to feel confident about buying a €30 cup when the ones in use already look so damaged.

reviews.author: sandra koprivnjak
reviews.text: Pleasant friendly atmosphere! Super staff! Morning coffee with milk = 7 HRK! While afternoon coffee with milk = 10 HRK!  Price is cheap for coffee in coffee bar cafe that is located in the center of Zagreb.  The cafe has an interior with a traction of trains! Also,  alcoholic drinks can be ordered at this café.

reviews.author: A R Construction
reviews.text: Good spot for early coffee. 8 kuna for cappuccino which is half of everywhere by the square

reviews.author: Aldo Gortan
reviews.text: In my opinion one of the best bars in Central Zagreb. The drinks are priced normally and if you're lucky you might just get into one of the quiz nights at the Zug Bar. They have tables inside and outside, and if you need to you can charge your phone on the wireless chargers they have. 10/10

reviews.author: Adri Kulashica
reviews.text: Interesting interior. Cosy terrace in front. Good coffee. But sometimes really stuffy with cigarette smoke.

reviews.author: Tigi Borg
reviews.text:
Solid coffee nearby retirement home. Decent price, almost 1/3 less than the first neighboring Caffe bar, friendly bartender. All of it is in closed or half-open space.
If you don't look for a fancy place and if you welcome the company of our elderly people, stop for a drink and relax in the very center of Zagreb.

reviews.author: Anja Krneta
reviews.text:
all the tourists leaving bad reviews, this is not the place for you!!
it’s for the locals, they host book clubs and pub quizzes etc.
booksa is the best place if you want to come to a caffe where you can casually sit and chat, study and have good coffee.
the atmosphere is unmatchable, dogs are welcome and the staff is always so kind and helpful :)

reviews.author: PeterClements
reviews.text: This café has such a welcoming vibe, perfect for catching up on a book or meeting friends. The coffee is delicious and the cozy corners make it hard to leave.

reviews.author: Grace DeCoske
reviews.text: They charge a 2 euro per person annual fee, but we felt it was worth it. The coffee and tea menu was good and very inexpensive. For reference we ordered a cappuccino and soy latte and our bill was less than 4€. It’s a cozy environment, perfect for a city break even if you’re just a tourist, like we are.

reviews.author: Laura Bosanac
reviews.text: Book lovers will adore this cozy cafe. It’s the perfect place to unwind with a good book and a hot drink in a peaceful setting.

reviews.author: Asy Matt
reviews.text:
Booksa had been recommended to us by nearly everyone as one of Zagreb’s “hidden gems,” so we decided to check it out. While it’s definitely beloved, calling it hidden is a bit of a stretch—it’s located in a central park area surrounded by other bustling spots, making it easy to find.

The interior has real charm, with vintage furniture and a cozy, intellectual vibe that invites you to slow down and read, write, or reflect. It feels more like a literary club than a typical café, which makes sense—it is one.

What caught us a bit off guard, however, was the requirement to purchase an annual membership just to grab a coffee. It’s inexpensive (around €2), but it’s an odd ask for tourists who are just stopping by for a drink. Still, we signed up and ordered a latte and a macchiato—both of which were excellent.

Unfortunately, the experience was slightly marred by some distracting activity. The staff or owners were moving items between floors, creating quite a bit of noise, which made it hard to relax or get any work done. After a while, we gave up on the idea of lingering and headed out.

In short, Booksa is definitely worth visiting for its atmosphere and great coffee, especially if you're a literature lover or looking for a more offbeat café experience in Zagreb. Just be aware of the membership requirement and that it might not always be the quietest spot

reviews.author: Katarina Radulaski
reviews.text: Whenever coming to Zagreb, I visit Botanicar for some new coctail. This time we brought our toddler with us. 😀 Cool place with great drinks, interesting enterier and on-going pictures exibition.

reviews.author: Josh Baltazar
reviews.text: Really nice vibes from the start! A great place to Fjaka, enjoy a coffee and good company. We ordered 2 coffees with milk. Also a sign of a good coffee place is if they give you a glass of water which they did. Really nice design, good taste and solid quantity. Also it was fine for us but notable for some, you can smoke inside. For payment you can use card or cash.

reviews.author: Ellh Chris
reviews.text:
Very nice place to enjoy your coffee. Very industrial and modern. The latte and the coffee was tasty and good.
The only minus star is because everybody smokes inside and we couldn't stand the smoke after a while. Moreover your clothes and hair smell so bad after a small stay there.

reviews.author: Kai Arsenault
reviews.text: Great coffee, good vibe inside and cool area, lots of smoking indoors

reviews.author: Batuhan Şahin
reviews.text: Ordered strawberry lemonade, it was good. Had to wait about 10-15 minutes by the time I wanted to pay up

reviews.author: Kikimora_Taste_Nomad
reviews.text: Orient Express is a fantastic spot. It’s changed a bit recently, but its history alone is worth a visit. You can find everything from local drinks to international options, all in a relaxed, basic atmosphere. The interior design is perfect for Instagram photos. Smoking is allowed inside, which some will love and others won’t, so choose accordingly. Definitely one of Zagreb’s well-known must-visit places.

reviews.author: Šarūnas G
reviews.text:
A small Cafe resembling a couple of train wagons from the actual Orient Express, the bartender knows his drinks, and recommended the best for our visit.
Would recommend!

reviews.author: Sal Schmidt
reviews.text:
Well worth stopping by this bar/cafe, sit inside and be taken back to the days of travel by train. The cafe is a true replica of the famous orient express train.

It feels like a tourist trap, but the pricing was reasonable, great service and visited by locals. The attention to detail and experience made it memorable.

reviews.author: Andrej Lončar
reviews.text: Orient Express Zagreb has a cozy, retro vibe that makes every coffee break feel special. The baristas are always welcoming and make a mean cappuccino.

reviews.author: Niahn •
reviews.text: Nice atmosphere it is authorized to smoke though and the smell is very strong

reviews.author: Meathead
reviews.text: Very fast service, waiter was very pleasant. Food was alright nothing mind blowing but decent enough. Only bad thing about a place it's very tiny as well as food portions.

reviews.author: Karma Melo
reviews.text: Great vibes and tea is quite fancy but nonetheless really nice

reviews.author: Kawaii I
reviews.text: Perfect place to catch up while having a light meal, and your sweet-toothed friend can try some cake!

reviews.author: Evangelia Klopa
reviews.text: This café was an absolute delight! The atmosphere was cozy — perfect for a relaxing coffee break or dessert outing. The coffee was smooth, but what truly stood out were the desserts. Every pastry looked like a piece of art and tasted even better - highly recommended!

reviews.author: Giang Nguyen
reviews.text: Good price, excellent cheesecake!

reviews.author: narcoleptic_moose 67
reviews.text: This little pastry shop was on my radar for quite a while. I'm so happy I finally visited Meet Mia because it was delightful! The atmosphere is wonderful, very easy to talk, and the cakes we got were outstanding. They were a bit on the pricer side, but worth it in my opinion. I highly recommend this place!

reviews.author: Jens Tait
reviews.text:
I’m a fanatic for fresh cakes and tea!
Meet Mia is such a quaint, beautifully set coffee shop and cakery.

The cakes were incredible, I had the carrot cake and my niece had the pistachio cheesecake. I highly recommend both… in-fact there was so many cakes you could be there all day.

The service was great and if you want to find a little quiet spot on a bustling city then please try Meet Mia 🫖 ☕️

reviews.author: Susan Kern Lambert
reviews.text: Delicious food and wonderful desserts. We ate brunch here and finished out the day with James Bond and Queen Niki. A great place to gather with girlfriends or the special woman in your life.

reviews.author: Alex Balan
reviews.text:
Coffee is good (it is Costa).
Service is good.
Ambient is good, although I would had preferred if it had heaters to stay outside.
The croissant with chocolate would had been good - if they used Nutella. Thus I did not like it. The desert with name “no-name” was good, but still the chocolate is not that intense like it was a bit washed.

reviews.author: Sonia S.
reviews.text:
During my visit on January 17, 2026, I noticed several cockroaches moving around inside the café.

This was very concerning from a hygiene point of view and made me uncomfortable staying there. I hope the management will address this issue and improve sanitary conditions.

reviews.author: Adrian Brdar
reviews.text: There’s something special about the atmosphere here—cozy, vibrant, and perfect for a coffee break. The drinks are always delicious and the staff make you feel welcome.

reviews.author: Marin Perić
reviews.text: This cafe is a lovely spot to relax with a coffee and watch the world go by. The staff are welcoming and the drinks are always on point.

reviews.author: Robert Chomicz
reviews.text: Café located near the central square of Zagreb that serves coffee as well as fruit smoothies and fresh juices. Prices are a bit high, coffee is good, and so are the juices. Service was not the fastest but friendly and courteous. Many smokers hanging out. Nice place to sit down an mellow out if you don't mind the smoke.

reviews.author: Gracy Baek
reviews.text:
cafe / bar / restaurant but only croissants were available for food menu at 10:30am
nice outside seats, they come to the table to get the order but it takes a while
got seats upstairs too quite spacious

reviews.author: S Sargent
reviews.text: Rude. Completely rude. I simply asked her if she had chocolate syrup and she said yes. I asked her to put it in my cappuccino and she asked if I was American. I said yes and she said “yea we dont do that here”. I asked for the largest and got a half full cup of foam. I asked for it to go and she said if you get to go you must leave..well duh.. if you dont like Americans maybe put that on your front window so we know not to support your business. If you’re American just go somewhere else because they dont like you here. If you dont like America, take off your “Yale” hoodie because thats in America. I hope you learn to be nicer to people of all cultures and have a better day than you do this morning.

reviews.author: Georgia Balchin
reviews.text:
I'm glad someone had mentioned it was hidden away in a little courtyard otherwise I wouldn't have found this little gem!
Wonderful atmosphere, great LP collection, some books and magazines to read in various languages.
I decided to sit inside to enjoy the music but the courtyard seating is gorgeous as well. Perfect in the morning.

reviews.author: Nillie
reviews.text: Lovely coffee shop with a spacious garden full of shade and trees—perfect for daytime. The coffee was delicious, and the interior is calm and roomy, ideal for working or studying. The staff are very kind. I especially loved the garden. Definitely recommend!

reviews.author: Daria S.
reviews.text:
There were five of us and it was just a great place to hang out with friends over coffee! (It was one of the best cappuccinos I've ever had🥹) But I also think it's a good place to work or study)
The staff was very friendly; the atmosphere is very nice, yellow and nice lighting….love this place!😄

reviews.author: Manu
reviews.text: Had a cortado here and really liked it! The coffee was smooth and well-made, and the shop has a nice, chill vibe. Friendly staff and a cozy place to relax or catch up with a friend. Would definitely come back if I’m nearby.

reviews.author: thauhidul Islam
reviews.text:
Asked for a Elderflower Juice, got a Domaci Iced Tea - went to get it changed, they said they don’t have elderflower juice anymore and offered to change it with freshly squeezed orange juice.

Nice, right?

Wrong.

The juice comes from a bottle and it’s bitter as hell. Despite my utmost effort to not waste, this is as much as I could drink.

The atmosphere is nice but that doesn’t excuse bottled juices and ready mix powdered hot chocolate.

reviews.author: Puja Limbu
reviews.text: This café truly stands out! From the quality of the coffee to the beautiful interior design, everything feels premium. The environment is cozy and Instagram-worthy. The staff are friendly and welcoming. Will definitely visit again!☕

reviews.author: Sita limbu Limbu sita
reviews.text: One of my favorite coffee spots! The taste is consistent and top-quality every time. The atmosphere is peaceful yet lively – perfect for both meetings and chill time. Loved the overall experience!☕

reviews.author: Ramdhuni Media
reviews.text: Absolutely impressed! The coffee is smooth and flavorful, and the presentation is beautiful. The ambiance gives such positive energy. A perfect place to unwind and enjoy quality time. Highly recommend to all coffee lovers!☕️

reviews.author: zara hrvatin
reviews.text:
This beautiful space that makes you feel like you are home offers the BEST coffee in town and wider! I recommend oat milk flat white that is out of this world! The love for the art of coffee is clearly noticable and it brings new level of regular coffee day!
Wonderful in every way, Nar concept store inside the coffee shop, brings that sophisticated feel to Croatia tru carefully chosen objects that are more of a stories that then travel with you wherever you go.

reviews.author: Sara Maria
reviews.text: Amazing coffee, super friendly staff, and a small but great selection of natural wines. It’s also a gallery and shop with thoughtfully curated cool and authentic products. A place you’ll really want to come back to.

reviews.author: Mubin
reviews.text: Great coffee shop in the area. Nice staff and a cozy inside with plenty more seating outside. The caramel drizzle latte was perfect. We also ordered a Vietnamese Iced Coffee looking for something strong but it was really sweet and didn’t seem to have the authentic condensed milk in it. The ham and cheese croissant was tasty.

reviews.author: Simeon Sadula
reviews.text: Was there few times also with my friends. It was very atmospheric and the prices are cheaper than other coffee shops and similar places. They bake the coffee beans at place which I find it very interesting and the smell oh my God! Love it! It’s a peaceful place I would say. By the way they always serve you tap water with beautiful glasses, again not every coffee shop does that even restaurants. I recommend this place to everyone who loves coffee and more!

reviews.author: Momo Yang
reviews.text: One of my favorite café bars in Zagreb! The coffee is always good, and you have many other choices such as Matcha, fresh juices, homemade ice cream, local beers and so on. You can tell the owners have put a lot of thought and love into every detail. Drinks are always served in beautiful cups, many of the items are homemade, and the atmosphere is warm, people who work there are always friendly and welcoming, for me it’s a perfect place to relax and enjoy a cup of coffee. Highly recommend!

reviews.author: CINAN MONSTER
reviews.text: Probably the best coffee shop I’ve ever been in my life til now. As soon as you step in you enter different atmosphere different realm. Realm of the delicious coffee. Beautiful background music. The owners are super friendly, welcoming every customer with warm heart. I’ll definitely come here when I travel to Zagreb.

reviews.author: Ogi Video
reviews.text:
I do think you have deecent coffe and I know starting your own business in Croatia isn’t exactly easy. But 8€ for 125g … that’s impressive!

Addendum: I have to admit, it is realy good !

places.name: Specialty coffee by Arena Zagreb group

reviews.author: Paulina Lechwar
reviews.text: Cozy atmosphere, top-quality coffee, the place is well-kept down to the smallest detail and, above all, an incredibly nice owner who clearly puts his whole heart into this place. I was in Zagreb for literally 3 days, but this is my favorite cafe among other in European countries I have visited.

reviews.author: Bav Mistry
reviews.text: Great find to start our morning in Zagreb. Delicious coffees and great value. Staff were so friendly and nice. We didn’t eat anything this time but I gave it 5 stars for food because the food all looked incredible, especially the chunky cookies. I regret not getting one!

reviews.author: Ryan Thomson
reviews.text: I am from Melbourne and we are very fussy about our Coffee. I have been in Europe for two months - this is the best coffee so far! Well done!

reviews.author: Mike S
reviews.text: Such a welcoming barista and a clean, comfortable place to hang out. The coffee and sandwiches were delicious too. Even the bathrooms were spotless.

reviews.author: sal burgas
reviews.text: Good coffee in the centre of Zagreb, barista very friendly and professional. The place is modern and absolutely spotless.

reviews.author: Vedrana Radanović
reviews.text: Delicious coffee, friendly service and a beautiful terrace. Perfect addition to open farmers market in Kvatrić! After getting your flowers, veggies, fruit, meat or fish you can treat yourself with this delicious coffee 🥰

reviews.author: Layla Doumont
reviews.text:
This coffee bar on the roof is a splendid place to get a delicious coffee.
When we arrived at the fourth floor, the coffee place seemed closed but there was a sign indicating us to go down to the second floor. There, a very kind vendor will take your order and bring it up for you.
I would definitely recommend to come here.

reviews.author: Chris Fung
reviews.text:
Loved our friendly host and barista!! 3 Great espresso macchiato!!
Highly recommended!! Cheers🙏🌸♥️😆

reviews.author: Palina Panamarova
reviews.text: Amazing specialty coffee roasters & bar! They have a gorgeous terrace on the 4th floor. The barista is just amazing, your will definitely enjoy your cup of filter coffee!

reviews.author: Patrick Debuire
reviews.text:
It was really nice to meet the owner
Very great place for coffee& mâcha

reviews.author: Igor Vita
reviews.text: This animal cafe is great. First of all, the idea, the atmosphere and the friendly staff. The owners are welcoming and really try hard. If you are an animal lover, this is the place for you.

reviews.author: Sara Jandrić
reviews.text: Lovely kitties that loves to cuddle and sleep, staff was amazing. You can see that waiter really loves his job , cuddling with kittens and singing while making your drink it made me feel like i was at someone’s house. Thank you 🫶🏻❤️

reviews.author: T Lundall
reviews.text:
Absolutely love this place! Such a cute little cafe with amazing and friendly staff, and beautiful and friendly cats, can't wait to come back for when they have the reptiles 🥺😍 the atmosphere is so calming and absolutely loved every second here.

It's better than the cat cafe in zagreb, they have no crazy sweet funky drinks that makes you sick and the prices is really good and normal prices not as crazy expensive as the cat cafe, (not trying to hate on the cat cafe but this place is waaaaaay better)

Definitely going to be my new go to and definitely going to tell all my vet school friends to come stop by at this cafe!! 😍😍

Love it!!! 😍😍🫶🏻

reviews.author: A “Prince Honey” Puđa
reviews.text: The atmosphere is cozy, the workers are very kind. And all of the kitties are friendly and are very cuddly. But the drinks ARE THE BEST!

reviews.author: Tamara Kušić
reviews.text: Great new place with cats and fish for now (more animals coming soon). Great coffee and hot chocolate. And big thanks to Nika who was serving us. Very professional, kind and friendly.

reviews.author: Marko P.
reviews.text: Very nice new place in Zagreb. Amazing coffee, great service and a chill atmosphere. A definite recommendation!

reviews.author: Filip
reviews.text: Beautiful and cozy place to grab a coffee and a snack! Love their Pastel de Nata also. Highly recommend! 😄👌

reviews.author: Petra Petrisic
reviews.text: A cute place to stop for a good cup of coffee and the best chicken sandwich

reviews.author: Ана Марија
reviews.text: Very pleased! I loved the coffee and the pastry! Would recommend and will be definitely coming again 😇💌

reviews.author: Rowan Hulsey
reviews.text: Barista is so sweet and makes a great latte!!

reviews.author: Alba Knežević
reviews.text: Great place and excellent service!! Also is pet friendly 💚

reviews.author: Ivica Miskovic
reviews.text: Fantastic service. I'm not even close to pretty, but look how it looks with a lens in it. Really relaxed. Friendly. Highly recommended.

reviews.author: Marin
reviews.text: Very friendly staff, pleasant atmosphere, first class coffee, lots of sun on the tables outside

reviews.author: Mirta Nikša Papić
reviews.text: Baby gives it a 5, got his own bebačino 🤗 and natural beetroot and apple juice, and we adults also liked the coffee and wine. The waitresses are young, friendly and approachable 😊.

reviews.author: Donna G. Laubach
reviews.text: A hidden gem for coffee lovers! The drinks are always delicious and the cozy atmosphere makes it a perfect spot to unwind.

reviews.author: Danica Maričić
reviews.text:
Beautiful interior, joyful colors, and a cozy atmosphere... 😍 Great selection of vegan food. I ate chia pudding with a banana, blueberries, and mint. After the pudding, I drank a tea mixture named "The Fountain of Youth". All was stylishly served. Also, great service. 👏
Location - the center of Zagreb, a few minutes' walk from the main square.

reviews.author: Clint White
reviews.text: A wonderful little hidden gem off the beaten track in Zagreb. Well worth the short walk from the city centre for outstanding coffee and great food. Brunch/lunch/light-bites that are all Instagram worthy, coupled with friendly service.

reviews.author: Bor Greiner
reviews.text:
Perfect little Instagramable but real place with a chef and politest service ever.
Got sunny side up eggs with avocado, noone minded it was not on a menu. Waitress forgot the croissont but I did not mind at all. Coffee was that great too.

reviews.author: Anita Bilaver
reviews.text: An unexpected oasis of whimsy! Good food. Good vibes. It’s like walking into a capsule of ‘light’ - especially in these gray winter months.

reviews.author: Drazen Nikolic
reviews.text:
Marelica may not look particularly fancy from the outside, but once you step in, you are welcomed by a very warm and pleasant atmosphere.

The selection of food is well balanced, and both the sandwiches and smoothies are excellent—fresh, tasty, and well prepared. However, the real highlight for me was the cappuccino: beautifully presented, great taste, and clearly made with care.

On top of that, the service was very friendly and welcoming, which made the whole experience even better.

Definitely worth a visit if you are in Zagreb.

places.name: Teneo Coffee Shop
places.address: b.b. (desno pored Konzuma, Trešnjevački trg 2, 10000, Zagreb, Croatia

reviews.author: Alissa Carver
reviews.text: Excellent Turkish coffee and getting to know the owner and his experience with coffee culture in Zagreb.  Definite must stop if you want to get out of the tourist traps.

reviews.author: Jared Theisinger
reviews.text:
Seriously good coffee, for seriously cheap! €5 for two (huge) coffees, and two kiddy chinos.
Seems to be very popular with local traffic, and for good reason!
The barista was super friendly and very good at his job!
Would recommend making a trip out here

reviews.author: Jeffu C
reviews.text: Great coffee, particularly shakerado. Brilliant summer coffee cocktail! Owner barista is genuine and friendly. Ask him where to go for pita, bakery, and cevapi. True gentleman!

reviews.author: The Tech Expert
reviews.text: Nice little coffee shop with delicious coffee and comfortable chairs. Amazing atmosphere. Nice little terrace with an amazing view on the street. The only thing that needs to be improved is that dogs aren’t just allowed outside, that they could be inside too in case if it’s bad weather outside and that we can socialize more.

reviews.author: Matko Zurak
reviews.text: I’ve been trying coffees all over the city for a while now. This one is in my top 3 for sure! Don’t let the artistic style fool you, we’re dealing with pros here :))

reviews.author: Anamarija Hrvoić Đurić
reviews.text: One of the best non-smoking bars in Zagreb. Very good coffee, breakfast and snacks, lots of space, working space included. Casual atmosphere with a few outdoor tables. Walking distance to a public garage and public transportation.

reviews.author: Diana Špoljar
reviews.text:
Cogito has the best coffee in Zagreb, and this specific place is the one I prefer because of the interior design. An urban place that’s great if you want to meet up for a cup of coffee, a casual business meeting or you just want to sit by yourself and do some work or chill. The staff is nice, friendly and professional, and the coffee is amazing! They offer non-dairy options, so it’s great for vegans, too.
Take notice that the entrance is not directly from the street, but you have to go through a passage in the building.

reviews.author: Palina Panamarova
reviews.text: One of the best specialty coffee shop in Zagreb, they have really nice breakfast sandwiches and also this place is perfect to work a bit with your laptop. Hrvoje is the best barista! :)

reviews.author: Amalia S
reviews.text:
I love the vibe of this place. Amazing design and you just feel like you are not in Zagreb. Coffee is excellent.

I’d give this a 5* but the music was a bit too loud (most people there were working/studying, but even if you are just having coffee with friends it might be a bit difficult to talk) and the prices are a bit high. Service was okay, nothing special.

reviews.author: Paola Mužić
reviews.text: Cogito is a very cool coffee spot. I went here for a brunch but not actually much of a brunch place 😅 they had only three options so I wouldn’t recommend it if you are looking for a brunch but the place and coffee is super cool and worth a visit. But if you would like a tasty snack with your coffee, Cogito breakfast is quite delicious😋

reviews.author: Gzr Fox
reviews.text:
This must be one of my most favourite places in Zagreb! We ended up there one night after walking through the less crowded streets of the old town and we couldn't have done better!

It's a lovely place and you just get the vibe the minute you step foot inside, it's got very good beer and a selection of other drinks and all that at very good prices!

The bartender was also a very nice, very helpful and cheerful man which was a breath of fresh air compared to some other places we went!

I seriously can't recommend this place enough, it was just perfect and you're probably going to meet the nicest people there! Not to mention a few fellow metalheads as well which is always a plus in my book!

Just go get a beer!

reviews.author: Gabriel Gimenez
reviews.text:
One of the most amazing places in Zagreb. Really cold beer, and nice place. Smoking also allowed inside 👍
Nice atmosphere

reviews.author: Denizhan
reviews.text: All thumbs up for this amazing place. It is the oldest tavern of Zagreb dated from 1830! And its location is on a beautiful alley, and in a district of Zagreb, where we absolutely fell in love with. Moreover, we found a totally empty bar while we were expecting a fully packed place. Therefore, I believe it is not highly advertised, which is another big plus.

reviews.author: C Cutler
reviews.text: Just one beer on tap. But it’s a good one.Pilsner Urquell.

reviews.author: Gozo Bozo
reviews.text: Legendary Croatian bar. 😀 Made famous by the movie "One Song a Day Takes Mischief Away" from 1970 📺 Some of the quotes from the film are framed.  Sadly, they do not take a card. Also the service could be better - as  the waiter was missing that old Zagreb class that this place is known for. 😐 On the other hand..."gemišt" ( white wine + sparkling water, 50/50 ratio ) was tasty. Very refreshing.

reviews.author: Diana
reviews.text: The gratinated strukli are amazing! It’s like a lasanha but the pastry is lighter and the filling has only ricotta cheese and any extra you choose. We had the truffle and the pumpkin seed one and they were both great, very different from one another. Pumpkin seed is a must, it’s very local and the combination with the cheese was divine. For dessert we tried the blueberry one and it was just perfect. There are not a lot of seats available and staff was a bit hurried, but nothing terrible

reviews.author: Ivy Summer
reviews.text: This is the best štrukli you will ever have in your life—in fact, it was so delicious that I got it twice in one sitting. The truffle one is the most popular savory option and the blueberry one is the most popular sweet option. I also had a yogurt drink which paired very well with this traditional classic Croatian dish. The vibes here were cozy (inside seating) and perfect for solo travelers who need some comfort food on a cold/rainy day or after an emotional visit to the Museum of Broken Relationships. If you’re not going alone though, then definitely make a reservation!

reviews.author: Ioanna Katsa
reviews.text:
We ate two times at La Štruk and we loved both of them!
The service is very friendly, polite and professional!
The dry white wine is exceptional and not so dry.
We had the salty cheese
The truffle paste and the roasted paper,which was a little bit spicy.
And of course they are pet friendly inside and outside !

reviews.author: Ai
reviews.text:
Came here after reading reviews and hearing about its popularity whilst only serving the Croatian dish, strukli.
I've had struklji before in Slovenia so I had some expectations of what it would be like, but they were slightly different.
To try a variety, we as a two ordered the truffle flavour savoury strukli, blueberry flavoured sweet strukli, and also a deep fried one, together with a couple of drinks.
The service was fairly good and the strukli came out steaming hot from the oven. My favourite turned out to be the truffle flavour, as I loved the delicious creaminess of it whilst having a subtle truffle taste. In this sense, I felt like the savoury strukli in Croatia was better than the one I had tried in Slovenia which were very different.
However, for the sweet one, although the blueberry flavoured was tasty, it didn't beat the flavours at Moji Struklji Slovenije in Ljubljana. Still, the blueberry flavour was very delicious, and sweet in just the right amount.
My least favourite was the deep fried one with cheese. It was still good but felt a little boring in its flavour and I struggled to finish it all. Although the dishes are good, it can get a bit heavy and monotonous so I would recommend sharing a few different flavours.
Overall, I enjoyed my experience so I would definitely recommend it as something to try in Zagreb and I would go back there if I returned to the city.

reviews.author: Mirko Logožar
reviews.text:
LOCATION & ATMOSPHERE
In the city center, 1 minute away from the main square. For the atmosphere, cant tell much as we were at the terrace and the only ones. 😂

SERVICE
Professional, nothing more or less.

PRICE
Reasonable prices when you take into consideration the location.

FOOD
There are traditional recipes for štrukli and the modern ones. This is a modern version of štrukli with various tastes and they are decent. If you are a tourist, you will be satisfied for sure. Portions are right as they should be.

OVERALL
Come and try, for sure part of the gastro touristic experience. If you want a traditional štrukli, go outside of Zagreb. A recommendation!

reviews.author: Victoria Grekova
reviews.text:
We ordered chicken, tofu, dumplings and bao bun. The most delicious was chicken.
High recommended. Atmosphere was great as local place.

reviews.author: Maria Kozari Mela
reviews.text: Amazing food, great vibe, cool music and excellent service. The lead waiter welcomed us with a huge smile in Greek because he could instantly tell from our accent that we're Greek. We tried the chicken Sando and the beef bao buns with sweet potato fries on the side. For drinks we had the fizzy mint soda and the hibiscus ice tea. It's a hidden gem in Zagreb's center and definitely a pleasant surprise. It is a tiny  space so you better visit outside rush hour. Kai Street food is a must if you're into delicious, quality street food. Will definitely come back to taste the whole menu. Cheers!

reviews.author: Margarida Silva
reviews.text: A outrageously incredible place to try an innovative cuisine type. All food was prepared with  extreme careful and knowledge, the flavors are fantastic and wonderful. We have tried the tune dish and the pulled pork and this blew our minds. An open invite for the chefs to create new ideas, I’m sure the audience is looking forward ✨

reviews.author: Büşra Özdemir
reviews.text: We visited kai Street food and had such a great experience. The chefs were so sweet and welcoming, and the food was absolutely delicious. We tried the sand and the noodles — we loved all of them! Also, a special thank you to the chef for me to take his photo. I really appreciate it.

reviews.author: Ivana P
reviews.text: Amazing food!! We ordered three dishes and all were great. Food came quickly, the staff is super friendly, music great, atmosphere very alive. Make reservations in advance because it is full house all the time I presume. No wonder! Definitely coming back to try other dishes from the menu.

reviews.author: Diana
reviews.text: Definitely a must try in Zagreb! We had two of the traditional meat flatbreads: Istrian Pearl, with ham and truffle spread, and the Slavonian Bride, with a traditional sausage and pepper. The latter was super tasteful, while the first one was good, but nothing special. The place is cute and staff was lovely, you truly feel like living a part of Croatian life

reviews.author: Aaron Leong
reviews.text:
Small place! Might have to wait/ make a booking!
Simple ingredients but damn the sandwich was delicious! I was suggested the white wine with it and it was perfect.
The service here is friendly and genuine and they actually wanted to engage explaining where the ingredients came from which was a nice touch!

reviews.author: Ana Rivas
reviews.text:
Unexpected good discovery. Highly recommended to taste Croatian km0 ingredients.

It is very tiny with only a few spots in bar stools. However, the girl will make sure that you feel comfortable. We arrived without reservation and there was waiting list.

She offered us to wait having a bier in the bar in front and picked us just a few moments later when our seats were free. We could take our biers with us during our meal in Heritage. That is service! If I could I would rate it as 7!

Unfortunately, it started to rain heavily for the people in the two tables outside. The waiter made all she could to relocate a couple with baby inside. Very helpful.

We enjoyed very much! Nice surprise in Zagreb. Highly recommended.

reviews.author: Vidcar Gonzalez Montiel
reviews.text: What an incredible dining experience. Not only do you get to taste Croatian flavors, but you also understand what you’re eating. The team takes the time to explain each dish, and the best part comes when you taste them. Absolutely delicious.

reviews.author: Jane C
reviews.text: Amazing restaurant! The staff were so friendly and helpful and explained where the components of the fish came from throughout Croatia! The food was absolutely delicious - I got the Istrian Pearl and I'd highly recommend :)

reviews.author: Stjepan Kujadin
reviews.text: Beautiful restaurant located in the city centre serving delicious food. Lively presentation as well. My compliments for the music. Well done! Lovely staff and host. I’ll be back soon! I know it’s not a Croatian cuisine but I would love to see more Croatian wines on the menu, per glass and bottle. Thank you!

reviews.author: Jonil Novela
reviews.text:
We had valentines day here. Been craving dumplings for ages and this restaurant offers unlimited dumplings on Mondays.

The ambiance was amazing, food was top notch, service and staff are top tier! My experience made it really good because we had a wonderful waitress named Kristine! She was very hospitable and took care of us.

I cant say any negative thing about this place.

reviews.author: Anton Huzhov
reviews.text:
Enjoyed this place. I did visit Kiyomi on Monday noon. It was surprisingly full, all tables were reserved so we were proposed to sit next to the bar.
Staff is attentive and pleasant. They speak English well.
We ordered the “sushi lunch menu for two” and portion was perfect for us. Fish slices were big and fresh. 2 types of wasabi, great presentation and well cooked rice. Really loved the food.
I recommend visiting this place

reviews.author: Ivica Kavasaki
reviews.text: The best Asian restaurant in Zagreb when it comes to whole dining experience. I wholeheartedly recommend its Business Lunch and their Sommelier's Table series were outstanding.

reviews.author: Matija Merc
reviews.text:
From start to finish a great experience, with tasty food and excellent staff.
If you want to continue in the Yakuza cocktail bar in the basement, just tell the staff and they will assign you a table!

reviews.author: Sarah Scicluna
reviews.text:
So Good! Delicious food and top service. If you’re in Zagreb, this is a great restaurant to eat at. We went for dinner and booked a table on the same day.
Very generous portions and reasonable prices.
Great value for money :)

10/10 ⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️

reviews.author: famousaj1
reviews.text: So good, we went twice! Amazing food with good proportions. That is to say, almost every dish can feed 2 persons especially at lunch. Local wine in abundance! I'd make a reservation at dinner for sure. Luckily we were able to be squeezed in as a party of 2 on the terrace. They do have seating indoors which will be at a premium during the cold months.

reviews.author: Zey S
reviews.text: Super friendly staff and a very cozy atmosphere. I tried Sarma and fell in love with it. Definitely became my favourite restaurant in Zagreb. I wish I had enough time to try the entire menu.

reviews.author: Tihomir Kolaric
reviews.text: Authentic Zagreb's dishes made from fresh and organic ingredients. 100% original and tasty dishes, snacks, soups, main dishes. Great local craft beer selection. Friendly staff, great atmosphere, top location. Make sure if reservation is needed when planning to visit due to rush hours due to business lunches and after work dinners.

reviews.author: Maria Kozari Mela
reviews.text: Avoid this restaurant at all costs. We were misguided by the good reviews and we thought we would taste quality Croatian cuisine. However, we had a very unpleasant and disappointing experience at Ficlek. We ordered strukli, fried chicken with mashed potatoes, traditional chicken soup and a pint of beer. The quality of the chicken was dreadful at both plates. The chicken soup was overly salted, greasy and smelled weird. It contained small portions of chicken with its skin as well. The fried chicken was also extremely salty and undercooked. The mashed potatoes were ok. As for the strukli, it was nothing exceptional. The service was quite indifferent and rude, they didn't even bother to ask us what was wrong with our food when we asked for the bill. I wonder how it's possible it got so many good reviews. Total lack of respect.

reviews.author: Naveen At
reviews.text: Had mixed meat(Turkey and pork), lamb grill and English potato’s. Food and the service was great, one of the finest restaurant in Zagreb. Also had the local draft beer, it was good 🍺 .. They still keep their old ambience with the wood. We really loved that😍

reviews.author: Cooper Gold
reviews.text: Amazing traditional restaurant with lots of vintage stuff from past that gives you creatin charm feeling and flair of the past with exquisite and super delicious traditional cuisine,  def a place to go to eat and enjoy the nice moments in a authentic time machine 💖

reviews.author: Maty Vasataky
reviews.text:
For those who want to try a truly typical Croatian cuisine, it is impossible to miss the great restaurant Při zvoncu.

The restaurant is very stylish and the wooden interior breathes on you.

The service is very friendly and when they saw that our whole group was looking at the menu and didn't know what to order, they came up with a solution for us to order what we wanted to eat.

Already during the order when we received a colorful description of the possible food from the service I got a taste for it.

Everything that arrived from the kitchen to our table was perfect, great portions and great taste.

Plus everything was well priced, considering how yummy it was.

If I'm ever in Zagreb again, I'll definitely check if the standard of the restaurant still shines and especially the stuffed Pleskavica.

I have nothing to complain about, just go there! :)

I DEFINITELY RECOMMEND IT!

reviews.author: Marvin I Portillo
reviews.text:
It was my first time trying out a Croatian restaurant and did not disappoint. Ambience was nostalgic with memorabilia showing day to day items across the decades. I even saw a sewing machine that I remembered from my childhood.

My companions and I tried several dishes with the meat platter (can't remember what's it called) being the best. Protein overload.

We were also served their craft beer and quite sweet.

Definitely a place I will try again.

reviews.author: Sergio Romano
reviews.text:
Very disappointing experience. We paid 100€ for two people for a very average dinner.

The most frustrating part was the wine pricing. We ordered two glasses of Graševina, but on the bill it appeared as 4 units (0.1L each) for a total of 24€, meaning about 12€ per glass for a very basic local wine. This is not clearly explained and feels very misleading.

Even a simple plate of kulen and cheese cost more than 22€, which is extremely overpriced for what you get.

Another thing that says a lot: the restaurant was almost empty on a Saturday night. That already tells you that locals probably avoid this place. No Croatian would normally pay 12€ for a glass of basic Graševina.

Food was ok, but prices are clearly targeted at tourists. Check the wine pricing carefully before ordering.

places.name: Restaurant Konoba Time4wine Zagreb Gornji Grad (Tradicional food/Sea food/Pasta/Pizza/Grill)

reviews.author: Alexandra Häßler
reviews.text: Thank you for the delicious food, a wonderful evening, and the very attentive service. We have been here in summer and winter, and each time it was very tasty and very nice. Be sure to make a reservation, as the restaurant is small and very cozy. We are already looking forward to our next visit.😋

reviews.author: becca fenech pace
reviews.text: Amazing food and great knowledgable staff. Our server kindly recommended food which tasted absolutely divine. He was very well informed about what pairs well with eachother. Overall a very positive experience, good music at the perfect volume and a cute interior. I highly recommend

reviews.author: Bereishit W
reviews.text:
I genuinely and wholeheartedly recommend this restaurant. During my short visit to this city, I came here every single day.

On my second day, something happened that truly took me by surprise. For dinner, I ordered my usual dish along with a portion of spinach dumplings. It was my first time trying Croatian dumplings, and I didn’t know how they were made. The restaurant was extremely busy at the time, so I didn’t ask in detail about the ingredients. When the dish arrived, I saw cheese on top of the dumplings — something I personally can’t eat — so I decided not to have them and only ate the other dish I ordered.

I thought to myself, since it was my own decision to order the dish, I should pay for the full cost — after all, it was my own lack of understanding of the local cooking style. But then the staff did something completely unexpected: they took the dumplings off my bill and kindly told me, “Your feelings matter more.”

I’m not exaggerating when I say this was one of the most surprising and touching experiences I’ve had in my travels to over 100 countries. I truly love this restaurant — not because of the money, but because in my conversations with the staff, I felt that they saw me not just as a customer, but as a friend whose feelings they genuinely cared about.

reviews.author: Bee Lee
reviews.text: We were not very hungry so we simply ordered 2 main to share. Food wise is good enough but not as awesome as what we had read in the review but we like the service. They gentleman asked if we are sharing our food so gladly gave us sharing plates even we didn’t order much. We also appreciate that we are allow to sit on a table for 4 pax which I is comfortable. The lemonade we ordered was made with squeezing lemon and mixed with water so nothing fabulous, do skip and order mineral water instead.

reviews.author: Katarina Tošić
reviews.text: We had lunch at this restaurant. There were six of us, and we were seated right away, but the table was already set with cutlery for five. We never received the additional set. When the food arrived, the waiter was nowhere to be found to ask for more, so we had to take cutlery from another table. Even though the restaurant was empty, we waited a really long time for the food. It was served partially, and we waited far too long for the last portion, so our dishes got cold by the time it arrived. We even had to get up to look for the waiter around the restaurant. The food was tasty, but the prices were a bit too high. Especially since my risotto was so oily that it was unpleasant to eat. In the end, we received a bill that included two flatbreads we never ordered (they were offered to us, but it wasn’t made clear that they would be charged even if we didn’t take them). I’m really not satisfied with the service.

reviews.author: Ivica Kavasaki
reviews.text:
This restaurant is a lovely spot for a relaxed yet refined dining experience. The creative menu keeps things interesting, with fresh ingredients and thoughtful combinations that showcase the best of the time of year.

The ambiance is warm and inviting, making it a great choice for both casual dinners and special occasions. Overall, a charming place that delivers quality and creativity in a comfortable setting.

reviews.author: Annette Franklin
reviews.text: The atmosphere of this restaurant is so peaceful! The food is absolutely delicious and the menu changes daily, highly recommend coming here! Staff is very friendly.

reviews.author: Charlie Baudoin
reviews.text:
In a few visits, Mali Bar has become my favorite restaurant in Zagreb. It is a rare thing to be able to serve humble and yet creative food and this is what we get every time in this place.

The chef and the staff offers fresh and modern cuisine and some dishes with a little twist for the adventurous at heart (like me) whether it be olives in a dessert or even an croissant infused purée next to a steak, there is always a great idea and the perfect execution. I honestly wish I could eat there every week.

Great location, great vibe and friendly staff. What else could we ask for ? I hope they keep going with the same philosophy for a very long time.

reviews.author: Ares Bursic
reviews.text: Nice ambient, great food, kind and helpful staff. A bit pricey, but it's worth it.

reviews.author: Bojan Kuruc
reviews.text: Had dinner here with 3 friends, we shared some appetizers and then ordered main dishes. There is some difference between meal sizes, for instance my lasagna looked like half a portion, and was indeed too small for one person. On the other hand my friend was struggling to finish his meat meal, as it was too big. In general good is good, but nothing special. Service was very good, fast and professional. Make sure you book table in advance as the place gets packed in the evening. Prices are acceptable, as these days everything is expensive in Zagreb. Good wine selection but pricey

reviews.author: Stanislav Cvitkovic
reviews.text:
An exceptional restaurant that excels in every aspect. The food is beautifully presented, full of flavor, and made with great attention to detail. Service is professional, warm, and highly knowledgeable, with excellent presentation of each dish. The wine list is well curated and perfectly complements the menu.

The atmosphere is elegant, relaxed, and welcoming, creating a complete and memorable dining experience. Highly recommended.

reviews.author: Bren Kenny
reviews.text:
We are early in the evening, thus ensuring we got seated.

We splurged on cocktails, wine and  the taster set menu. The waiter was very good. When I went to order a given wine, which unfortunately was out of stock, he was able to suggest an alternative ( off the menu) at the same price point, and quality.

It was an excellent way to experience quality food of Croatia in pleasant surroundings. Overall it was not inexpensive at 80 per person. But worth trying.

reviews.author: Ana Petrić Glibota
reviews.text: Dinner for two, made reservation that morning. The place itself is small, not much space between the tables, but location in the city centre is a plus, especially if you are tourist. We shared multiple tapas and drank babic testament. Also worth mentioning Navis olive oil, available during dinner on the table (amazing oil). Our waitress (forgot the name, very kind and professional woman from Sibenik) was perfect host, I can imagine she also contibutes to our overall great experience. We are coming back for sure!

reviews.author: Finn Talen
reviews.text: A wonderful experience at this restaurant. We're from the Netherlands and were lucky enough to get a table for five at 9:15 pm. The staff speaks excellent English and is very passionate about their work. It was a warm Tuesday evening, and they were very cheerful to everyone and enthusiastically explained everything on the menu, the drinks they had, and what we had ordered. They make almost all the drinks themselves (think lemonade and juice, as they only have Coca-Cola and bottled water, which they don't make themselves), and we had a fantastic evening with the waiter, Ivica. All the food tasted fantastic and there was absolutely nothing to complain about. Anyone thinking of eating here should definitely do so. Also, be sure to ask about the homemade drinks.

reviews.author: Jason McGee
reviews.text: One of the first restaurants we dined at while here. They do a modern take on classic dishes such as their tasty "Dalmation Pašticada in Gnocchi" as well as new creations. We enjoyed the "Shrimp Skewers" and the "Prosciutto Croquette". The "Sea Bass Fillet" was amazing. The "Roasted Tomatoes" was a good choice as well. And I can't forget the table bread with a delicious butter. Definitely recommend this place. Make a reservation.

places.name: Restoran "Kod Pere"
places.address: Croatia, Ul. Cvjetka Rubetića 25, 10000, Zagreb, Croatia

reviews.author: Gabriel Radu
reviews.text: Absolute fantastic hearty home cooked food and service. Not sure how many tourists wander out here, and they already have a steady stream of local clients, but everyone visiting Zagreb should try it. The portions and huge, the house wine is plentiful and good, really can’t fault it!

reviews.author: Graham Riach
reviews.text:
Super tasty food and really friendly staff. The portions are generous, so prepare yourself! The setting is lovely, with grapes hanging from the vines above the terrace and little birds eating the breadcrumbs.

The two staff members we spoke with were so nice and helpful, and both spoke good English, which helped with our nonexistent Croatian.

reviews.author: CHUN TO LEUNG
reviews.text:
beef delicious and don’t forget use bread dip remains sauce is perfect match.
dessert is cherry pie ,the cherry not too sweet so good for asia taste.
staff is polite ,helpful and efficient
If you travel to Zagreb don’t forget to get here to taste traditional Corita food.

reviews.author: Anne
reviews.text:
Visited on account of the great reviews.  Didn’t disappoint - huge portions - the beef was tasty (sweet as cooked with fruit), the duck with lasagne sheets was good but we found some
of the duck a bit dry. Restaurant was on a hillside away from town so less touristy.

reviews.author: Marko Jovanovic
reviews.text:
One expects that a restaurant in Zagreb, serving a crown jewel of Croatian cuisine like Pašticada, would approach it with respect, if not reverence. This establishment does neither. It serves a version so profoundly incompetent, it feels like a deliberate mockery.

Let's move beyond subjective taste and discuss the objective, technical failures that occurred in their kitchen:

1. The Lukewarm Indifference: The dish was served at a tepid, room temperature. This is worse than serving it cold. It indicates the dish was haphazardly left to sit, allowing the fats to separate and the sauce to lose its soul. A proper Pašticada is served piping hot, its sauce glistening and vibrant. This was a lifeless, stagnant puddle.

2. The Butcher's Chaos: The slicing of the meat was not just wrong; it was chaotic. Some pieces were cut against the grain (as they should be), while others were cut with the grain, guaranteeing a chewy, stringy experience. This demonstrates a stunning lack of basic, consistent knife work. There is no standard, no technique—just random hacking.

3. The Ultimate Sign of Amateurism: The fillet was not cleaned of its silverskin and membranes. This is the most unforgivable sin. Any first-year culinary student knows that these connective tissues do not break down during cooking and become tough, rubbery, and impossible to chew. Serving this is the clearest possible message: "We cannot be bothered to do the most fundamental preparation."

The combination of these failures—the sloppy butchery, the unforgivable lack of trimming, and the lazy, lukewarm service—creates a perfect storm of culinary malpractice. This isn't a matter of a bad night; this is a fundamental lack of skill and pride.

You are not just serving bad food; you are misrepresenting Croatian culture to visitors who trust you. You should be ashamed to put your name next to this dish. Take it off the menu. You have not earned the right to serve it.

For anyone seeking an authentic taste of Croatia: run. This place is a factory of disappointment that turns national pride into culinary shame.

reviews.author: Tihomir Kolaric
reviews.text: One of few left public restaurants with local/national/regional cuisine and specialties in Zagreb's strict centre. Modest prices, old school working staff, freshly cooked meals on daily basis....not only proper "Gablec", but peoper lunch or dinner restaurant.

reviews.author: bobjohnsonstp
reviews.text: Food was good. Waiter doesn’t mess around and is fast at getting your orders. Decently priced. Portion sizes (especially the schnitzel ) are almost as massive as the low taper fade. Overall good experience.

reviews.author: Lynne Hopkins
reviews.text:
We had a great experience at this local restaurant. The waitress was so helpful and friendly, and the food was delicious
The stuffed cabbage was so tasty that we planned to return again on this trip and have it for a second time.
The portions are very large and the prices are quite reasonable.

reviews.author: Michael J
reviews.text: This restaurant provides an authentic local experience. They offer beer on tap, and the dinner portions were huge! The schnitzel, veal medallions, and pork chop all tasted great, as well as their potato side dishes. With some bread on the side and olive oil, it made for a filling meal. For two people I’d recommend splitting an a la carte plate and adding a salad or two.

reviews.author: Dan _gaming
reviews.text: The food was mediocre overall. The duck was okay, the beef soup tasted like it came from powder, and the bread was stale. The service was unfriendly and slow— though to be fair, that seems to be the norm almost everywhere in the Balkans. The food feels as if I’m eating at a cheap eatery but without the cheap eatery prices.

reviews.author: Ai
reviews.text:
For a restaurant with such a big menu in a touristy area, I was expecting the food to just be average but I was pleasantly surprised.

Being new to Croatia, we wanted to try some of their local dishes so we ordered the black risotto and local gnocchi dish. The black risotto was striking to look at with its cuttlefish ink, and there were pieces of tiny seafood within it. It had a mild seafood risotto flavour and not too fishy.

As for the gnocchi dish, the gnocchi tasted fresh and pillowy and the sauce had a nice tomato/bacon like flavour.

I would say that there probably are better versions and possibly more authentic versions of both dishes but for someone trying these dishes for the first time, it still tasted really good and they were also quite good value for money at around €11 per dish.

The restaurant also had a nice atmosphere and the service was very good.

reviews.author: Paula
reviews.text:
Having lunch here was such a nice experience. The staff were super friendly and attentive, and one of the waiters even let me charge my phone inside, which saved my life a bit.
The food was really good too. I ordered the ćevapi with fries and it was delicious. Great value for money overall, the only thing a bit pricey was the drink.

reviews.author: Rin in the area
reviews.text: food here is so damn good! must try the black risotto, it’s super well-seasoned, and even as someone who’s not usually into risotto, I really liked it. the grill set is great value for money, the pork ribs are tender and flavourful, and the pizza’s amazing too. Highly recommended, I’d definitely come back again!

reviews.author: Gabriely Thairine
reviews.text: My experience in Nokturno was amazing, it was our single night in Zagreb and we chose this place to have our night. The people who work there are friendly and helped us to choose the typical beverage from Croatia. The food is really delicious and accessible. Thank you for this wonderful night in Zagreb 💖

reviews.author: Damir
reviews.text: The fact that this place is always crowded speaks for itself—and rightfully so. If you come hungry and aren't one to nitpick over minor details, you will be very satisfied. The portions are truly generous, so you probably won't even need an appetizer. Considering the quality and quantity, the prices are quite fair, and the staff is polite and very helpful.

reviews.author: Bonnie Mallory
reviews.text: Jeff was our waiter and he was outstanding!  Kept a close eye on us to make sure they everything was to our liking and made suggestions, plus presentation of food was very nice.  The food we ordered was wonderful and pictures are attached.  The price was very reasonable for the amount of food ordered, which included chicken cordon bleu and beef cheeks with a gravy, along with zagorske strukle.  For dessert -- you've got to try -- was Lanterna strukle.  It was so wonderful!  Everyone at the table was very pleased with their meal.

reviews.author: Annabelle Fletcher
reviews.text: Amazing experience and the best duck I’ve ever eaten. Service was also perfect, the waiter that’s served us was amazing and even gave me a gift at the end!!! The food was very reasonably priced for the amazing quality. Couldn’t recommend more !!!!

reviews.author: Nicole Wan
reviews.text: Me and my partner had the perfect time at this restaurant. The waiter especially was very polite and attentive. (Think his name was Jeff). Very welcoming and informative on the dishes and wines. Although they had sold out of Beef Cheeks I had the most perfect fillet steak, whilst my partner had duck breasts. Would definitely recommend and visit again. The food and wines were absolutely delicious. We had the sweet potato and grilled veg sides, and chocolate cake dessert. Salivating at just the thought of the meal we had. Romantic music playing in the background, there wasn’t a single fault I can point out about our experience at Lanterna only thing is I hope they have beef cheeks available when we next visit as I was craving it. We were gifted a gingerbread heart at the end a traditional Croatian souvenir. Extremely impressed with our visit!!!

reviews.author: Hannah Clark
reviews.text: 100/10 experience! We were greeted with open arms and smiling faces. Jeffrey, our server, was so kind and personable. He was amazing as well as the owner. The food…. AMAZING. Their caprese salad…. I’ve never had anything like it. It tasted like he prepared the mozzarella balls himself, as well as grew the olives for the Olive oil. All of it was so fresh. I will be dreaming about that caprese salad. Our main courses were phenomenal as well — striploin steak & pljukanci. Thank you guys soooo much for an amazing experience!!!! Love, Hannah & Miles

reviews.author: SOF FOT
reviews.text: We discovered this place on our trip to Zagreb. Located in the old town near the market, this place has very good food (the black angus was exceptional, even though I asked for it well done which I know most people would disagree with) and the personnel is very welcoming, friendly and professional. They even gave us a little hand made thank you souvenir to take home. Although a bit on the pricy side I highly recommend this place for its great food and fantastic service.

reviews.author: HopefulBites
reviews.text: We had a good time ☺️ I loved the pea cream which came with the trout. Delish 😍 One slight disappointment was that the trout was proposed to be coated with 'hazelnut crust', which was missing. I didn't get any explanation for that, but perhaps was it because I asked a garlic -free dish? It would have been nice to have some hazelnuts !

reviews.author: Murphy Pang
reviews.text:
A very good local restaurant with some traditional Croatian food.  The Monkfish and Zagreb cutlet are delicious.
Only one downside is waiting quite long for the dishes.

reviews.author: Ana Rivas
reviews.text:
Very enjoyable experience. We were advised several local dishes that we liked. Since, I Wished to know the local wines, I asked and received good recommendations by glass.

We paid a bit high but no complains.  I do not understand some of the bad reviews.

reviews.author: Dan _gaming
reviews.text:
The setting is beautiful, but the prices are very high for local standards. The food is good, but the service felt inconsistent. At times the waitress seemed dismissive, maybe because of the language barrier, but it came across as if my questions were brushed off, and I felt rushed.
At the end, I specifically asked to round up the bill, but instead, like in many touristy and pushy restaurants, the waitress handed me the tipping screen. I find that screen very annoying: you’re suddenly faced with a bunch of numbers, which feels confusing and pressuring, as if you’re being forced to tip on top of already expensive prices.
the place charges Western European prices rather than Croatian ones, without the western european feel or service which makes the pushiness around tipping even less pleasant.

reviews.author: Oliver Scholl
reviews.text: This used to be one of the best places in Zagreb. Not anymore. The portions are now less than half the original size and the prices increased. Meanwhile ingredients became worse. A shame. Last visit to this former gem of Croatian cuisine!

reviews.author: Spn Nrmn (Spiny Norman)
reviews.text:
An absolute must-go if you're in Zagreb. We went there for lunch, then came back for dinner AND breakfast during our trip because we loved it so much. It is 100% worth waiting in line for, everything is SO DELICIOUS, the hype is very real.

They're famous for breakfast but the real stars are the lunch and dinner menu (the Zagreb breakfast is really good tho).
We loved every dish we tried, but all the very traditional foods like the homemade blood sausage or mlinci were stupidly good. I'm still dreaming about the pork belly and mashed potatoes. The DESSERTS oh my god...that pumpkin dessert...a dream.
They also have a really impressive drink menu, with delicious specialty coffee too.

Also, really worth mentioning: the whole staff was very nice and professional, always make sure you're having a good meal. Every time we went, if was a different waitress and they all were great! The place itself is comfortable, chill music, great vibes.

reviews.author: Andreas Karaviotis
reviews.text: Cool place to enjoy your brunch and have a great coffee.  It’s in one of the most busiest areas in Zagreb 2-3 minutes walk from Josip Jelacic Statue which is in the city’s main street.  You can sit outside as well which especially is very nice during winter with heating of course.

reviews.author: Hilda Johansson
reviews.text:
My friend and I shared a “Zagreb breakfast” and a French toast with Nutella and banana here and were completely speechless afterwards. The Zagreb breakfast is potentially the best breakfast I’ve ever had and the French toast was made to absolute perfection. A must-do in Zagreb!

If we had stayed longer in Zagreb, we would have returned for drinks too as that menu looked promising.

reviews.author: Robert Musgrave
reviews.text:
Very welcoming elegant, and yet homey type of restaurant near the main square in downtown Zagreb Croatia.
The staff here at Otto and Frank is patient, kind and very friendly. I enjoy the egg on toast 😋, since I am Ketovore, I didn't eat the toast.
They served coffee but like everywhere in many countries in Europe. They serve lukewarm coffee not hot coffee. You don't get free refills on anything you can order to drink except water. And the larger coffee cups are half the size of the average coffee cup you get in the United States. Half coffee and half warm milk is served everywhere. It doesn't difficult.
To find any place that has a normal American size coffee cup. And it's difficult to get anybody to serve coffee steaming hot, which is the way. Coffee is supposed to be
But that's the way it is throughout Europe. Lukeworn coffee, that's half coffee half milk.
And no refills, unless you pay for each refill. That's my biggest complaint about being able to enjoy coffee while in Europe. But I would recommend Otto and Frank's restaurant to anybody who is visiting Zagreb Croatia.

reviews.author: Sara Kasavica
reviews.text: We had brunch at this place two mornings in a row. Everything was excellent, especially the French toast! Special praise for the waiter who truly enhanced the whole experience with his great energy and positivity!

reviews.author: Nenad Trifunovic
reviews.text: A house classic, Gratinated onion soup was a nourishing experience second to none, and a venison ossobuco was a bliss melting in mouth, together with hand made gnocchi. This is an exemplary cooking in my book. Just the right intensity, flavors and textures. And all in a somehow cosy and friendly environment despite beeing the quite prestigious place.

reviews.author: catalina fulger
reviews.text:
A pleasunt evening in Zagreb, becouse of a rain become a long pleasunt eve.
I kindly recomand
Service impecable
Antre
Roasted figs with rosemary and honey, goat's curd mousse, smoked almonds pesto, fig leaf oil- Excellent

Poached  Salmon , swiss chard , wasabi cream, rhubarb marmalade, coriander foam

Venison ossobuco, homemade gnocchi corn flour fava beens, herb and wine sauce very good

Dessert
Fig   sponge cake, lavender blossom syrup, ricotta and Amalfi lemon cream -very good

Chocolate  cake good

reviews.author: Andrew OT Hui
reviews.text:
Plane stopped over Zagreb a day, stopped at this elegant bistro for a long lazy lunch, a treat!
Warm welcome, good service. great ambience!
Local Chardonnay was burgundy style 🥂🥂 4 starters to sample, best onion soup I have ever tasted, beef tartar must have, fish carpaccio & foie gras also good, duck confit ok, veal cutlet average… pinot noir 🍷🍷 also local, nice..
Overall a very nice meal, must have in Zagred!

reviews.author: Oliver S
reviews.text:
We recently visited Le Bistro Esplanade, and unfortunately, our experience was quite disappointing.

The service left much to be desired — the staff were not particularly friendly or attentive. At the start of our meal, our plates were removed immediately after we had just added oil, without any acknowledgment or apology. Throughout the evening, no explanations about the dishes were provided when they were served, leaving us to guess what we were eating. When it came to dessert, no recommendations were offered, which felt impersonal and lacking in hospitality.

The ambiance was acceptable but clearly dated and in need of a refresh. While the food itself was of good quality, it wasn’t particularly memorable — certainly not enough to justify the price.

Overall, it felt like the restaurant relies too heavily on its reputation rather than delivering an experience that matches the cost.

reviews.author: Dom Dub
reviews.text:
Really dissapointed and a shame for a restaurant with this price. The place looks hold and they could renovate a little bit.
The understandable thing is, how you could have nice products and destroy them like this? Every one can do better at home.
The octopus salad (around 30E) is just octopus with patatoes.
The veal is over cooked and this is shame.
Same for the turbot fish. They not care about the products.
Service is good.
Wine is nice with good references.

reviews.author: Matías Carrasco Jiménez
reviews.text: Shut up. We would like to start this review by thanking our amazing waiter Marko, who, with all the friendliness and patience in the world guided us through the menu and helped us enjoy a wildly delicious dinner. There is not much to say, we were amazed by the quality of the food. Please visit this place if you’re looking for authentic Croatian food in Zagreb. You’ll thank us later ;)

reviews.author: Oya Çötok
reviews.text: if you have to choose a dinner option in Zagreb, i highly recommend you pick this place. It’s quite successful in terms of ambiance, service, and flavor. waiters are attentive and very kind. we ordered the duck and calamari for our meal and we liked it. Crotian white wine was also quite delicious

reviews.author: Vasiliki Papaioannou
reviews.text:
Very nice experience in that restaurant for Donner.We booked the table online and then we contacted by email. The response was prompt and professional. The menu was typical Croatian and it was overall d
Tasty and filling. Thw environment was pleasant and the staff was friendly. Recommended!

reviews.author: Ivy Summer
reviews.text: The roasted duck/turkey were sold out, so the waiter recommended the veal, which was good but the pasta that originally comes with the duck/turkey was just a touch dry for my taste. I also ordered the Štrukli which came later, but I would’ve preferred if I got it at the same time because in hindsight, it would’ve paired well with their tender roasted veal. Overall good experience! The surprise dessert was really delicious, thank you! Absolutely love the traditional atmosphere and music at the perfect volume. Great place to eat on a rainy and cold day.

reviews.author: Igor K
reviews.text:
I visited Stari Fijaker last night with my family, and overall, our experience was mixed.

On the positive side, the atmosphere was wonderful. The restaurant has a lively, joyful energy, and the staff were genuinely pleasant and attentive. From the moment we walked in, we felt welcomed and comfortable, which made the evening enjoyable.

However, the food didn’t quite live up to the front-of-house experience. While nothing was terrible, the dishes lacked seasoning, and the pork filet in particular was dry and underwhelming. It felt like the kitchen was a step behind the high standard set by the service and ambiance.
