If the culinary side matched the charm of the dining room, Stari Fijaker could easily be one of the best spots in town. Hopefully, with a bit more attention to flavor and preparation, it can reach that potential.

reviews.author: Andrea N
reviews.text: Delicious and good quality food; fine portions. Helpful waiter with lot of information on food. They even have Grk vine (great vine from island of Korcula) which is very rare to find.

reviews.author: Dany Le Goaix
reviews.text: Make a reservation for this Michelin recognized bistro.  It’s small and has upbeat music to go along with great dishes based on local fare.   The white chocolate sauce on the  pear clafoutis was delicious.   One of our best meals over 10 days in Croatia.

reviews.author: Y Y
reviews.text: Simply excellent! Recommend shrimp on toast and rice with mussels and shrimp. Both taste really nice.

reviews.author: Low Zong Han
reviews.text: Definitely the best meal I've enjoyed in Croatia. Service was fantastic - no need to look at the menu, just tell the staff what you are looking to enjoy. Wine recommendations were on point as well.

reviews.author: Jin Woo Chung
reviews.text:
We just walked in and asked for a table. The answer was a little too friendly, as I was expecting something more like, "Unfortunately, we are fully booked today. Would you like a table outside?"
​The outside area has its own atmosphere, but be aware that the restroom is just over the wall from the tables. But the inside looks good.
​Overall, the main courses were quite good—the filet mignon and the mackerel, in particular. But at around €30 each, they should be.
​I ordered the focaccia as a starter. For a restaurant with a Bib Gourmand rating, it looked and tasted weird, and I couldn't find any olive oil. The side dish, a summer salad, isn't worth mentioning. It was just a kind of green that didn't seem like a proper salad and was too salty.
​The wine was good, except that the glasses were placed on the left side of the table.
They didn't charge for the salad, but I'm not sure if it was a generous gesture due to its saltiness or if they just forgot about it in the first place.
​If you visit with a positive attitude, the main dishes are absolutely worth the price.

reviews.author: Annie Vedo-Myszkorowski
reviews.text: I had always wanted to eat at this restaurant but it always seemed full.  On this evening, there were six of us who attended this restaurant.  It was fine dining, reasonably priced.  The waiters attentive, friendly and as well as funny.  We had an amazing evening and we will come again next time we are in Zagreb.

reviews.author: Paepae S
reviews.text: Good hand made pasta, and carpaccio is also fresh. Prices are on the higher side. Be prepared to wait even with a booking…

reviews.author: Vreju Florin
reviews.text:
I recently visited this restaurant and ordered the shrimp risotto, which unfortunately turned out to be a real disappointment.
The rice was severely undercooked — not pleasantly al dente, but genuinely too firm and unfinished. A proper risotto should be creamy and cohesive. This one had absolutely no creaminess at all — none. It felt dry, separated, and poorly executed.
The flavor profile was equally lacking. There was no noticeable garlic, no depth, no balance. It tasted flat and bland. The shrimp themselves had virtually no flavor and added nothing to the dish.
Overall, it felt like a rushed and technically incorrect preparation rather than a carefully made risotto. For a restaurant dish, this was far below expectations.

reviews.author: Mirjana Ristovska
reviews.text:
We had a very disappointing experience here. We waited 1.5 hours for our food, despite ordering relatively simple dishes like pasta and steak. To make matters worse, there was no apology or communication from the staff regarding the delay.

When the food finally arrived, the quality didn't justify the wait. The ravioli was incredibly bland—the cheese filling lacked any seasoning or depth of flavor. Overall, it felt like a complete waste of time and money. I wouldn't recommend this spot if you value good service or flavorful food.

reviews.author: Fahaad Kazi
reviews.text: Stopped by Boban in Zagreb and tried the crostino and Spaghetti di Gragnano. The crostino was decent - good even - but us adding their table olive oil (not specifically meant for it) to enhance the flavor left us feeling a bit off, like it relied too much on a extra touch. The pasta, priced at 19.00€, sounded exciting with roasted pepper cream, pepperoncini, cherry tomatoes, pine nuts, and Pecorino espuma, but it was bit of a letdown - bland and unexciting, almost like a dish for kids we felt. For 19.00€ and with the restaurant’s reputation (and prices of their other dishes), we expected flavor fireworks, but it was so lackluster that adding salt was the only way to taste anything - something you wouldn’t expect at a place like this. Even with salt, it was just okay, still not great. That said, the service was great - friendly and attentive, no complaints there. Overall, a mixed experience.

reviews.author: Mohil Kapoor
reviews.text: It's at the corner and serves amazing food. The service is decent and food is delicious

reviews.author: Abdulrahman Alzamil (Zamil)
reviews.text: One of the best dining experiences I’ve had! I tried the beef tortilla and the chicken tortilla, both were incredibly delicious and full of flavor. Everything tasted fresh and homemade. The restaurant itself is clean, nicely decorated, and has a welcoming vibe. Staff are friendly and helpful. Highly recommended if you’re looking for quality food and a great atmosphere

reviews.author: Antonia Jagodić
reviews.text:
This was my first time in Good Food. I tried their new Fit&Sit bowl - Balanced Body with Chicken, white cheese, arugula, red cabbage, spinach, marinated beets, broccoli, cucumber, purple onion, cherry tomatoes, apple, cashews, dried cranberries + Libra sauce.
It was so delicious and has everything your body needs to be perfect and balanced.
I will be back for sure. 🙂

reviews.author: Mubtasim Sabik
reviews.text: The best brunch I've had in a while. Their soups, wraps and salads.. all great! The place is quite small though so come only in 2-3 people or takeaway. Conveniently located, 'good' (but actually great) and healthy food! I'll come eat here again on my next trip to Zagreb. Please never lower the quality :)

reviews.author: Hrvoje Herceg Jaklenec
reviews.text: A popular spot to recharge your energy while sightseeing or taking a walk around the city. You can find well-prepared, quality food served in a short time, with excellent and professional service. Prices are also very reasonable and attractive.

reviews.author: Annabelle Rosin
reviews.text: The food tastes amazing, the service is great, he is soooo kind and even when it‘s cold outside you can enjoy his food because there is a heating lamp to keep you warm. To go place if you are hungry! Prices are great as well! Can only recommend this place!

reviews.author: Mauricio Campello
reviews.text: I bought the crispy BBQ burger, which I thought it very tasty and with a good size.  The portion of fries is very generous, too, and crispy.  I'm only visiting Zagreb for two days and I'll definitely have dinner there again.

reviews.author: Foxfire ninja
reviews.text: Beyond amazing and for such a good price. The food was massive and very generous. Lovely staff and awesome outside seating, i love the lights and decorations. 100% recommend to anyone near by. No burger will ever be the same as these ones!

reviews.author: Mikola Tudor
reviews.text: Beste food! 10 / 10.Very good food and serving. Price accesibile. Good job! Thanks for the meal 😊

reviews.author: Michael Licwinko (tchrtrvlrwrtr)
reviews.text: I was surprised to find pulled pork in Zagreb. Don't know why, but I was. And happy I found it, even if it was by accident. The BBQ sauce was different from what I'm used to but it was tasty. The fries were good but I couldn't finish such a large order.

reviews.author: Eduard Jakupi
reviews.text:
One of the best burgers in the centre of Zagreb, without any doubt.

From the very first bite, you can tell this place takes burgers seriously. The brioche bun is perfectly toasted—soft, buttery, and just sweet enough to hold everything together without falling apart. The meat is outstanding: juicy, flavourful, and cooked to perfection, with that rich, beefy taste that real burger lovers look for.

The sauces deserve special praise—balanced, bold, and clearly made to complement the meat rather than overpower it. Every ingredient feels carefully chosen, fresh, and of high quality. Nothing is random here; everything works together in perfect harmony.

You don’t just eat a burger here—you experience it. This is the kind of place that sets the standard for what a proper burger should be. If you’re in Zagreb and appreciate top-tier food, this spot is an absolute must-visit.

Highly recommended. 🍔🔥

reviews.author: Rayen Lim
reviews.text:
My husband and I ordered the spicy burger and we devoured it within minutes (hence no photos)✨✨

The buns held its shape and was not soggy!! The patties were smash-like and had maximum flavour 🫨🫨🫨 The chilli was subtle and not overpowering!!! The truffle fries were freshly prepped - crispy, hot and fluffy!! TLDR; the staff cooked 🔥🔥🔥

Super value for money!
Staff was friendly!
Food was freshly prepared~

Thank you for the yummy food 🌸✨🍔

reviews.author: Mak Gojnić
reviews.text: Excelent and delicious burgers! One of the best I’ve ever tried. Definitely recommend.

reviews.author: Aa96
reviews.text: The food is amazing, the meat and sauce are sublime! Friendly and fast serving staff :D huge recommendations to all.

reviews.author: Beauty centar Linda
reviews.text: Great food very tastey and affordable

reviews.author: Robert S
reviews.text: Assortment of freshly baked pizza slices available with almost no wait. Large slices for 2,5 Euro make this great street food value. Chicken nuggets and fries fresh and hot, again great value for 5,00Euro.

reviews.author: Predrag Matić
reviews.text: Craving fries? This place serves them hot, crispy, and just the way you like. Friendly staff and quick service keep me coming back.

reviews.author: Jess Kwon
reviews.text: Well worth the €2.50 for a huge slice of tasty pizza. Temperature was luke warm but good as a snack on the go.

reviews.author: Thi Mai Huong Vu
reviews.text:
Went for a margherita slide which was surprisingly more pleasant than I had expected. It was big as well
Totally worth the price

reviews.author: Sain Dihana
reviews.text:
Great for late night snack, price is fair for the small portion. The pizza is not great but ok if you just want to grab a bite.

Service could be improved. Basically we were left hanging after paying because the staff didn’t show us where to collect our order - there were no signs.

Finding seats upstairs could be a challenge, it’s a small space.

reviews.author: Ole Kristensen
reviews.text:
Best burger everything. And the French fries was delicious 😋
Got the smoked burger and fries.

reviews.author: Greta Witkowski
reviews.text: If you’re in Zagreb, these burgers are a must try! 10 out of 10. My husband and I had two smashed burgers and beers and they are one of the best burgers I’ve had. Would 100% recommend!!

reviews.author: Gökhan
reviews.text: It was a delicious burger. I ordered the original one and the fries was amazing, as well. The atmosphere was outstanding.

reviews.author: Joe Hardane
reviews.text: I tried the french and the original burgers. Very tasty, and juicy. The french sauce and cheese gives a distinguished smooth taste. I also enjoyed the soft buns. I tried the place's own beer. It is a lager beer with a sweet fruity after taste I would definitely recommend it. The fries themself are average, however the cheesy toppings were delicious. I would recommend the owner to improve the fries, to the Belgian authentic style for instance. In brief a very good burger, one if the best

reviews.author: John
reviews.text: Just watched the only brunette/brown haired waiter stick his hand in food to remove something before serving it to a table. The group of guys tonight working 10/18 @ 8pm are the rudest people I’ve encountered in Croatia and Europe. Astoundingly unsanitary and disgusting to put your hand in someone’s food. There was also no soap in the bathroom and they were very annoyed at me bringing this up. Do NOT eat here. The blonde guy wearing the turquoise/blue shirt was also very rude when I simply tried paying my bill. This place badly needs a manager onsite to keep these kids in check. They’re so hostile here I didn’t even feel safe calling that waiter out.

reviews.author: Anchit Kher
reviews.text:
Was out late and struggling to find a place to eat. This came through as a hole in wall shop that had delicious food at near fast food prices.

I had the pita sandwich for €4.5 and it was great in taste and size.

No toilets nearby or place to sit though!

reviews.author: Annesha Deb
reviews.text: Stays open till late night, great food, great hosts

reviews.author: Ioan Enchev
reviews.text: Best burger I have EVER EVER tried in my life. You are TOP!

reviews.author: Aletheia
reviews.text: Legendary sandwich bar that still keeps up with the seervice and quality after 20 years of eating here  almost every weekend after  a drink in the centre

reviews.author: xMotivationFix
reviews.text: Good place, I recommend! Two burgers with one box of fries and a coke for 23 euros.

reviews.author: Andrea Usenik
reviews.text: Amazing taste of both the chicken and regular kebab. Many thanks to the so very nice staff for respecting my special request on Wolt, much appreciated. The prices are more than ok!! Beautiful and clean restaurant, especially the terrace on a sunny day!

reviews.author: 전상호
reviews.text:
He was very kind.
And flavor is also so good.
Thank you for kindness!
- From korean guy -

reviews.author: Max Racki
reviews.text:
Guy working was nice and welcoming, quick to take my order.
Food was great and filling, can't go wrong there, the price is what you would find in other kebab places in the center of Zagreb.

reviews.author: Dark Shard
reviews.text:
Very good kebab, really spicy.
But somebody clogged the toilet and I was forced to use the girls' bathroom 😎💦😞

reviews.author: Nico Salon
reviews.text: I am giving this review just now. You can check your cctv, the staff working on last night shift with the black sleeves is very rude towards Asians. He said some Bad and Rude Words where I’m just asking about my order, he should be polite with any nationalities. That’s not because he’s local he can say anything about what he can say. Very disappointing. We are always eating here but we didn’t encounter anything like this before. Better to fire this person. Bad on your business.

reviews.author: Aida Lifestyle Traveler
reviews.text:
If you’re craving a delicious, juicy, perfectly crafted burger in Zagreb, Burger Station is the place to go! The moment you walk in, you feel that cool, urban vibe that makes you excited even before the first bite.

The burgers are incredibly flavorful — soft buns, tender meat, and toppings that are fresh and well-balanced. Everything tastes like it’s made with real care and passion. Their fries are crispy, the sauces hit just right, and the portions are more than satisfying.

They also offer doner kebab, which was my personal choice — juicy, perfectly seasoned, and honestly so satisfying that I’d come back just for that! And if you’re a fan of Balkan classics, their ćevapčići are pure njami — tender, tasty, and full of that authentic flavor we all love.

Service is fast, friendly, and genuine, which makes the whole experience even better. Whether you’re grabbing a quick bite or sitting down to enjoy your meal slowly, this spot guarantees quality and taste every single time.

If you’re in Zagreb and want a place that offers amazing burgers AND delicious Balkan favorites — Burger Station is absolutely worth the visit! 🍔🔥

reviews.author: Nina Lorenčak
reviews.text:
I ordered the cheddar burger and it was really good. Meat was perfectly cooked, the bread was extremely delicious (just like freshly baked pastries from a bakery) and I really loved the fact that it also had a lot of delicious vegetables and sauce, as I don't like my burgers to be too dry/salty/meaty.

One thing I really need to comment on is the incredible kindness of their staff - I can't remember the last time I went out to eat and be served by such genuinely nice people. Sadly I'm not from Croatia, but I would love to eat again at that place.

reviews.author: John van der Velden
reviews.text: Good and friendly service. Really good and clean toilet.  The order was taken really fast and food prepared directly. Quality of food was good en very tasteful

reviews.author: Habib
reviews.text:
It's certified Halal restaurant. I had large portion of ćevapi in bread and was great. The meat were perfectly grilled and stuffed in fresh bread. Second day I had Texas burger and was also great.
Comfortable sitting indoor and outdoor. Nice ladies staff and very good service.

reviews.author: Dimitris Kostidis
reviews.text: Never thought that someone can serve a burger like this, and people will eat it. I ordered an American Special burger with chicken and beef inside. The bun was nice but the chicken and the beef was like chewing a bubble gum!! Super bad taste and texture. The chicken was probably cooked days before and they simply reheat it and put it in my burger! The glass that they gave me to drink my soda, was full of lipstick from previous customer, and it wasn t even washed!!! The lipstick was fresh!!! Hygiene seems to be missing from this restaurant. Personel was polite. Very unpleasant experience for me.

reviews.author: G E
reviews.text: Yum! Loved the food here. One of us got a poke bowl and two got burgers and fries. Everything was so delicious and fresh tasting, delivered in a short period of time, and we felt it was a good value. The staff was friendly and helpful. We definitely hope to come back.

reviews.author: Kikimora_Taste_Nomad
reviews.text:
Sweetest gesture for Women’s Day!
This cookie is not just delicious, but a perfect little symbol of appreciation. A true ‘dobar znak pažnje’ for all the amazing women out there 🍀💕

reviews.author: Jamie Wojciechowski
reviews.text:
In Zagreb for a few days and looking to try some vegan options and Soho Sushi Rolls & Poke Bowls offered some tofu dishes, so we decided to try it for lunch.

My partner and I both ordered the Tofu Trendy with a white rice base (no quinoa), smokey tofu, feta cheese, baby spinach, green apple & tomatoes with a basil sauce on top. Very good flavors and perfect lunch on the go.

Highly recommend for a fresh vegan lunch.

reviews.author: Lalos SAW
reviews.text: We chose this place because it has vegan options (it actually has two), but both dishes were nothing special. The establishment itself is beautiful and modern. The service, however, falls short; it was the only place where we noticed the staff having an attitude.

reviews.author: MBgmail
reviews.text: The food is great but they cannot get my delivered orders correct. They just can't do it. It's less than 50% that what I ordered actually shows up in the bowl I receive. So close to being great but this ruins it.

reviews.author: Ivan Doer
reviews.text: Both the look and taste were great! It's definitely worth trying if you're in Zagreb.

reviews.author: Hallgrímur J. Jónsson
reviews.text: Best burger joint in Zagreb. Fresh local ingredients, great beer. It’s not often you experience that the food you get looks better than on the pictures! Great value for money.

reviews.author: N G
reviews.text: Very good burger, very good fries (and their signature sauce) and the salad was also amazing. Overall basically a perfect experience. Service was also friendly. Highly recommended, and not only for burger fans!

reviews.author: Anna
reviews.text:
Small restaurant with seating indoors as well as outdoors. The burgers were delicious! The sauce was good however, the BBQ flavour was very mild, so mild it didn't taste anything like BBQ. The bacon was perfectly crispy! The fries were a little disappointing. All the flavour came from the truffle sauce and the parmesan. The fries itself were soggy (not from the sauce) and didn't taste like they were salted at all.
The waiter was nice enough. Wasn't attentive though. Didn't ask if everything was good, or ask if we wanted more drinks. But this seem to be a common theme in Zagreb though?

reviews.author: Maria Glushenkova
reviews.text: Great atmosphere, young vibes, and stylish design with an open kitchen. I appreciated that they had an English menu. However, the food was quite disappointing. The French burger had very thin meat, no cheese, and was overloaded with mayonnaise. The salad was also drenched in the same sauce, which was too fatty and lacked flavor—it would have been better without it.

reviews.author: Nikola Stosic
reviews.text: Very good restaurant, ladies and boys were so nice to us, we have 4 meals and all were prepared very good. Definitely recommendation to all visitors. May favorite is smash burger with bacon and potato with sauce and cheese!

reviews.author: logan humphrey
reviews.text: Amazing burgers amazing fries went here while on business in Croatia and best food I had while here

reviews.author: Matt Plazek
reviews.text: Sorry I don’t have pictures of the food I ate it too fast! Servers were friendly, food came out hot and fast, and it all tasted great! Wish I had this place back home I would come here all the time!

reviews.author: Liza Muradian
reviews.text: The burger was very average.  We are your clienst for many years,would be good you have consistency in all your outlets.

reviews.author: Emil Čupić
reviews.text: Insane price for the quality level and size, kids snack sized burger. Miss this place when it actually had good burgers and portions for adults. This was 7,8 year ago tho so nostalgia is strong. Attaching picture to help people with the portion size. Btw the burger from the image was 10.70€.

reviews.author: Steven L
reviews.text:
Delicious! We really loved the food. I got the extra large kebab and she got the salad. It was one of the best Kababs I've had in a while. This place has large portions for a reasonable price. A good helping of meat for both dishes. 100% recommend.

Coke products in a glass bottle and other bottled options available. Fast and friendly service. Large portions for the price. Outdoor seating.

reviews.author: Umed Ahmedov
reviews.text: Family operated small business. Located in the heart of Zagreb, very popular street. Not so expensive. Polite staff. Fresh local beer is very delicious.

reviews.author: Zen Wolf
reviews.text: Food is good, staff speaks English, kitchen is clean. This fast food shop is fantastic! The menu offers a great variety of delicious options, from burgers to salads, all made fresh and served hot. The staff is friendly and efficient, ensuring quick service even during busy hours. Clean and comfortable seating makes it a great spot for a quick bite. Highly recommend!

reviews.author: Wanida Pannin
reviews.text:
Waited to order for about 10-15 min. They had many orders to do, and worked two people. Would recommend having a third person taking orders, and those two making the food. We asked the guests also standing in front of us, if tthey had ordered, which they didn't - but they ensured us that it is worth the wait, because they'd been here yesterday! 👌🏽Nice, so we waited.
Had a wrap with classic kebab and the picante dressing...and it was really worth the wait. The meat was so juicy and tasted delicious, wrapped with fresh salad. They guys making the food, also brought us the food to our table, which was really extraordinary, despite having many customers! 👍🏽
The one less ⭐ in service, is because of the wait, tonight. We'd still recommend tasting their kebab!

reviews.author: Marcel Tworus
reviews.text: Guys, humans are not able to fit double Balkan burger. Ćevapi were pretty good. Prices are ok. It was fun to order with that Lady.

places.name: Kusina Filipino
places.address: Galleria Business Center -1 kat, Trg Drage Iblera 10, 10000, Zagreb, Croatia

reviews.author: Karen Claire Abne
reviews.text:
At Filipino Kusina, we didn’t just enjoy the food we felt truly welcomed. The staff are incredibly accommodating, warm, and attentive, making the whole experience feel even more like home. The dishes reminded me of the Philippines, but the kindness of the people there completed the feeling. It’s not just a meal it’s a piece of home served with genuine care
We eat sisig,lumpia,lecheplan pansit of course and drinks is Coca Cola

reviews.author: Mitch San
reviews.text: The authenticity of the Food is outstanding. The  leche flan and the maja blanca is really delicious it melts in your mouth. The lechon kawali and the sisig is perfectly cooked and tastety ..  The service is very friendly .

reviews.author: Arnold Cornelio
reviews.text:
As a Filipino, i approve that recipes are authentic. dishes were’nt overpowering and owner was super friendly. Portion sizes and taste did not disappoint
would definitely recommend the restaurant to my friends.

reviews.author: Roberto Albino
reviews.text: Service very fast,and the food was so delicious I ordered siomai, wok rezanci, turon and siopao.Authentic pilipino style.Very good experienced.love it

reviews.author: Claerk shawn
reviews.text: During a holiday trip to Zagreb with a friend, we discovered Kusina, a Filipino restaurant located inside the Galleria Business Center. The food was amazing and delicious, I would recommend this restaurant to anyone, it's worth the money. We really enjoyed our meals! Big thanks to Mr. Don, the owner, who was very approachable, friendly, accommodating, and attentive. We'll definitely be back and hope to see more branches in other cities. Superb!

reviews.author: Zdenko Vidakovic
reviews.text: Great atmosphere, American diner style, good burgers. I would recommend.

reviews.author: Agne Geguzyte
reviews.text: Very nice, 70s style spot. Food very tasty

reviews.author: Dejan Šimić
reviews.text: Great place for hamburgers if you are with friends. The menu is funny (Croatian), along with lots of funny messages around the walls (mostly in English). Decorated as retro diner 50-60 '. Staff kindly and very kind. I recommend to everyone who is looking for good food and to laugh at mek Per's Zagreb. Greate place

reviews.author: Antonio Sečen
reviews.text: Great food, very polite and fun service, cozy atmosphere

reviews.author: Alfer Chaser
reviews.text: I remember when this place was kind of new, the food is still good but the old recipies were better. And the prices have gone up.. But its a really good fast food/diner?

reviews.author: Denisa Broscareanu
reviews.text: perfect hangout and date spot. great food, great staff, great experience overall. Will definitely come back when I visit Croatia again

reviews.author: Tomislav Lukic
reviews.text:
Absolutely amazing experience at StreetFood DB in Zagreb! The restaurant is run by a single man who puts his heart and soul into every detail. You can tell he truly cares about his place and his customers. While we were eating, an elderly lady came in asking for a glass of water because of the heat, and without hesitation, he kindly gave her one for free. That moment alone showed how genuine and kind he is.

The aesthetic inside is beautiful, inspired by an anime , but it gives a really cool and unique vibe. We ordered two burgers and two portions of ćevapčići, and everything tasted absolutely delicious. You can really taste the quality and effort in the food.

The owner spoke not only great English but also German, which made communication super easy. We had a nice small talk with him, and he was incredibly friendly and also very kind towards kids.

There’s only one small table for two outside, but inside there’s enough space to sit comfortably. If I had to mention one thing that could be improved, it would be the small toilets, but everything else was spot on.

Definitely a 5-star place, and I’d recommend it to anyone looking for great street food and a super friendly atmosphere!

reviews.author: Grga Jureković
reviews.text: The place was great! Food was really good and filling, portions were big and if you are not a big eater I recommend splitting. The owner who is also a waiter was super friendly. The prices are reasonable and you just can't not enjoy the ascetic which is super cool and a paradise for a dragon ball z fan which I am not but I was here with my friends who are really big fans and they loved it. Overall a really good place which you should visit even if you are not a DBZ fan because of great food, highly recommended.

reviews.author: Nicola Morgan
reviews.text: Cute place with Dragonball art and memorabilia everywhere. Food was delicious and a good price. We loved the spicy cheese sauce. Staff were super friendly and helped us translate the menu. Definitely recommend 😋😁🍔

reviews.author: Mr. Cold
reviews.text: Great place where you can eat while watching Dragon Ball episodes on the TV. Everything is covered in DB art and the atmosphere is fantastic, highly recommended!

reviews.author: Max Mlinarić-Subotec
reviews.text:
Dont be fooled by the location (next to a fuel pump 🫣). This place has very Tasty burgers that are juicy and have some of the best buns I've ever had.
The guys are very chill and friendly, even when very busy. We did wait for our order a bit longer, but that just says how they have a lot of orders(justified).
Recommended spot definitely!

reviews.author: Petar Kamenecki
reviews.text: The servers are very polite, and even though it's a small location near the gas station, it has personality and a generally likeable vibe to it; it's enjoyable to stay in there! The food itself is fabulous, I've come from the other side of town just to eat here multiple times. The interior is well-kept and the toilets are clean, with hygene stuffs like paper towels and soap always supplied. Inportant to note is that since there are two employees running both the kitchen and the serving, so if it's jam-packed you'll have to wait 10-ish minutes for your food. That being said, the wait is well worth it because of how delicious the food is. Not to mention that the combo is 13€ (at the date of writing this) where you get a burger, plenty of fries and a juice of your choice. It's truly worth the drive even if you aren't near and, despite its small appearance, it's a great smash bar where you can get a tasty bite at a reasonable price.

reviews.author: Arna Ksi
reviews.text:
Went today for the first time and will definitely be back. There are only four types of burgers on the menu, but what more do you need… They’re simple, made with straightforward ingredients, well-crafted, and really tasty. I totally recommend this place. And the guys working there are good fellas too. :)

Didn’t even get a pic - burgers disappeared way too fast.

reviews.author: Dean Zulich
reviews.text: Great Burger overall, high quality ingredients yet simple and delicious . Staff is on point too.

reviews.author: Ales Horvatek
reviews.text:
This is a fantastic burger, easily one of the best I've had recently and a guaranteed spot in my Top 5 burger joints in the Zagreb area. If you consider yourself a burger enthusiast, this place is a must-try.

However, the pricing is truly outrageous. At €14.20 for a single burger and a mere handful of fries (albeit very tasty ones), the value proposition is severely skewed. This price point (well over 100 kunas) for just the meal, and without a drink, pushes it into premium territory without justification.

The Bottom Line: If money is no object, this is a five-star culinary experience, no question. But the high cost dramatically lowers the overall score, making it a pricey indulgence rather than a regular recommendation.

reviews.author: Nicole Padilla
reviews.text:
The casual atmosphere is inviting with a sunny enclosed patio that gives you the feeling of alfresco dining but not with the weather elements. I had the New Jersey Burger and my husband had the BBQ Special Burger. We both loved ours! He got his with an extra patty and mine came with an extra patty (it was my lunch and dinner lol).

The flavors were great and we both agree it’s one of our best burgers on this side of the world. Although the burgers have more common ingredients they are done in fun ways, it’s delicious.

The sweet potato fries were crispy and had a powder of pepper on them which balanced the sweetness of the potato. We are picky with our sweet potato fries and these were one of our favs.

The prices are reasonable for what you get.

We will be back!

reviews.author: Steffen Seger
reviews.text: I had the „BBQ Special Hot & Spicy“ burger and it was great! Good meat, good bun, good toppings, good sauce - and the fries were good as well. Fair prices and a nice little selection of tasty beers as well. Would definitely come back here again once I’m in Zagreb again.

reviews.author: Marijana Z
reviews.text: Great value for money! Big, juicy burgers stacked with ingredients! Beautiful home made airy brioche bun is there just to hold all the ingredients together, like it should be. Cute interior, nice staff, high quality for such an affordable price. Home made chips come with the burger and parking is free too. Love it!

reviews.author: Aleksandra Vukovic
reviews.text: Had a really good Philly cheese sandwich. Meat quality is excellent, green paprika is a bit overcooked for my taste, but still the sandwich is really good.

reviews.author: Kevin Z
reviews.text: Unquestionably the Prime is the best burger I've ever had in my life. Everything is great, I don't have the words to describe it. My whole family also thought their meals were incredible. Great staff working there who all seemed to take pride in making this a great place to eat.

reviews.author: Robin Storm Hauge
reviews.text:
I came here as it was the only place open at night. I ordered my food, and after (while waiting) I saw the insane dedication and attention to detail these people have.

I’m so glad I came here, they deserve a higher rating. I also saw one customer
Trying to edge them on whir making food for the rest of us. The people working here are amazing with what they do!

And the first impression (and last) of my meal was awesome!

reviews.author: Stella
reviews.text:
Delicious food and great service!
I went to Ali Kebaba and it was such a good experience. The staff were really friendly and spoke English, and there was even an English menu which made ordering super easy. The food was amazing, fresh, full of flavour, and the portions were big. There aren’t many chairs inside, but there are more seating options outside which is perfect when the weather’s nice. I ordered for my family and cousins and everything came out just right. Definitely recommend if you’re in Zagreb and craving a good kebab!

reviews.author: Jonatan Pleško
reviews.text:
Really nicely done interior, very hygienically overall (the people making the kebab use gloves, have separate salad utensil for each of the types of salad or cheese), a wide variety of kebabs with each one being quite tasty.

Pros:
+ Two types of a kebab, two types of cheese, and a lot of different salads
+ Cheese isn't being paid for extra
+ Lovely interior for a kebab place
+ The most hygienic kebab place in Zagreb

Cons:
- Chicken kebab is for some reason a lot more expensive

EDIT: Just realized I was talking about a different Ali Kebaba place, but nevertheless, most of the earlier points still stand so I only modified the review a bit!

reviews.author: Edward Alexander
reviews.text: A pleasing surprise. I wasn't expecting much from this place but it was tasty falafel with plenty of cheese and salad options, nice spacious and clean interior. Prices are OK, not so cheap but fine.

reviews.author: RR MV
reviews.text: The food is really good and fresh. The portions are also very big! The restaurant itself is really loud. The female cashier is very slow but the preparation itself (by the male workers) was really fast.

reviews.author: Troy Placid
reviews.text: Stars 57 is a hidden gem. The burgers are juicy and full of flavor, and the wok dishes are fresh, fast, and perfectly seasoned. You can tell everything is made with care, and the vibe is relaxed and welcoming. Great food, fair prices, and definitely a spot I’ll keep coming back to. ⭐🍔🥢

reviews.author: Szymon Warzybok
reviews.text: Very nice place. You can eat a good burger or bowl here. Additionally, you can buy good beer. The atmosphere is pleasant. The service is nice and helpful. The prices are affordable considering the taste of the dishes. I highly recommend it!

reviews.author: Halime Arsln
reviews.text: We visited this restaurant at a quiet time. I ordered three mixed wok and my friend ordered chicken wok. Service was very fast and food is delicious. The atmosphere is calm, all staff is polite and smiling, thank you.

reviews.author: Vitali Sokhin
reviews.text: Small portions, and the beer is a bit more expensive than in other places. Also, they don’t fill the glasses completely.

reviews.author: Nina Zlatič
reviews.text: Not worth it. We waited an hour for our food. The chicken katsu was cold and the wok was a little warm, it tasted bland. On the menu the wok is €9.30, the waitress charged us €10.50. When we asked her why it was less on the menu, she told us that they had increased the prices.

reviews.author: Mirko Logožar
reviews.text:
LOCATION & ATMOSPHERE
Location is 10 minutes by public transport from the city center. Terrace is nice, you have high and low seats. It is near the road so the car sounds are something you will constantly have.

SERVICE

PRICE

OVERALL

reviews.author: Cláudio Cardona
reviews.text:
Bundek City Park, nestled in the Novi Zagreb area, is a beautiful and versatile public space that offers an array of activities and experiences for visitors of all ages.

Historical Background

Bundek Park has an interesting history. Initially, it was a gravel pit, excavated in 1955 for the construction of the Zagreb Fair pavilions and transportation surfaces. The extraction continued until 1960 for the construction of the Freedom Bridge. As the pit was excavated below the groundwater level, it eventually filled with water, transforming into a lake. Over time, the area evolved from a gravel pit into a popular recreational site for the citizens of Zagreb. However, in the early 1980s, the park faced challenges due to unsanitary conditions, leading to a ban on swimming in the lake. This resulted in Bundek becoming somewhat neglected until its renovation and transformation into the modern park it is today .

Present-Day Features

Today, Bundek City Park is a beloved location for relaxation and recreation. It spans approximately 550,000 square meters, offering a beautiful landscape with various activities. Visitors can enjoy walking around the lake, biking, running, and picnicking. The park also has a boat service for those who wish to experience the lake from the water. Besides its natural beauty, Bundek is frequently the site for events and festivals, including the famous Bundekfest music festival and Christmas markets .

Accessibility and Facilities

Access to Bundek City Park is straightforward, with public transportation options available from different parts of the city. The park is well-equipped with numerous parking spaces, and its location near the eastern parking lot of the Zagreb Fair makes it easily accessible for visitors. The park is also equipped with bars and cafes around the lake, offering refreshments and meals .

Suggested Itinerary in Zagreb Starting from Bundek City Park

1.	Start at Bundek City Park: Begin your day with a leisurely walk around the lake, enjoying the serene environment and lush greenery.
2.	Explore Gornji Grad: Head to Gornji Grad, the historic upper town of Zagreb, known for its picturesque streets and squares rich in history and cultural heritage.
3.	Visit St. Mark’s Church: Don’t miss the iconic St. Mark’s Church, famous for its colorful tiled roof.
4.	Tower of Lotrščak and the Zagreb Cathedral: Climb the Tower of Lotrščak for panoramic city views and visit the Zagreb Cathedral, a stunning example of Gothic architecture.
5.	Relax at Zrinjevac Park: End your day at Zrinjevac Park, close to the main square, perfect for unwinding amidst charming fountains and lush greenery.

Overall Impression

Bundek City Park in Zagreb is not just a recreational area but a symbol of the city’s transformation and commitment to providing green spaces for its residents and visitors. It offers a perfect blend of natural beauty, historical significance, and modern amenities, making it an essential stop for anyone visiting Zagreb.

It was so refreshing to see families and people of all ages and walks of life enjoying such a magical place in the middle of a city.

reviews.author: Cláudio Cardona
reviews.text:
Descending from the historic churches nestled in Zagreb’s heart, we stumbled upon the quaint yet captivating Park Bele IV. This small park, contrary to expectations of sprawling green landscapes, reveals its charm through simplicity and the remarkable presence of a tower that stands as its centerpiece. Despite its modest size, Park Bele IV offers a surprisingly expansive vista of Zagreb, providing a unique vantage point from which the city unfurls in all its splendor.

A Serene Escape

Park Bele IV, though diminutive, serves as a serene escape from the bustling city streets. The park’s limited greenery does not detract from its allure; rather, it highlights the tower, inviting visitors to explore its historical significance and architectural beauty. This park isn’t about the expanse of nature but about offering a moment of tranquility and a window to Zagreb’s soul.

The Tower’s Tale

The tower within Park Bele IV is not just a structure but a narrative holder of times past. While specifics about its history and origin might require a deeper dive into local archives or expert knowledge, it’s clear that this tower is a silent witness to Zagreb’s evolving skyline and the stories woven through the city’s fabric over centuries.

Inclusion in a Zagreb Itinerary

Incorporating Park Bele IV into a Zagreb itinerary offers a delightful contrast to the more frequented tourist spots. It’s a testament to the city’s hidden gems that await discovery by those willing to venture off the beaten path. A visit here can be a brief yet enriching experience, ideally suited for those seeking solace and a panoramic view of Zagreb without the crowds.

What to Do and See

While the park itself may not boast an array of activities, its essence lies in the contemplation it invites and the perspectives it offers. The tower, subject to accessibility, could provide an elevated lookout over Zagreb. Visitors are encouraged to capture the scenic views, immerse in a moment of reflection, or simply enjoy the juxtaposition of historical architecture against the modern city backdrop.

A Recommendation

For travelers weaving through Zagreb’s historical tapestry, Park Bele IV is a reminder of the city’s layered stories and visual poetry. It’s worth a pause in your itinerary, not for grandeur or spectacle, but for the intimate glimpse of Zagreb it presents. As you stand in this park, with the city spread out before you, there’s a sense of connection to the past, present, and the ever-evolving narrative of Zagreb.

In essence, Park Bele IV, with its modest parkland and commanding tower, is a niche of tranquility offering one of the best vantages over Zagreb. It exemplifies that sometimes, the true essence of a place is not in its size or the flamboyance of its attractions but in the stories it holds and the views it shares.

reviews.author: Cláudio Cardona
reviews.text:
Park Opatovina, a lesser-known yet charming green space in Zagreb, offers a tranquil escape in the city. It’s a place where locals and visitors alike can find a peaceful retreat from the urban hustle.

Tranquil and Serene Ambiance

Park Opatovina’s primary appeal lies in its calm and serene atmosphere. It’s an ideal spot for those seeking a quiet corner for relaxation or contemplation. The park’s layout, with its lush greenery and shaded areas, provides a perfect setting for reading a book, enjoying a leisurely picnic, or simply unwinding amidst nature.

A Break from the City Bustle

Located away from the main tourist areas, Park Opatovina offers a break from the bustling city life. It’s a place where you can enjoy the quieter side of Zagreb, away from the crowded streets and busy tourist spots. This park is a hidden gem for those who wish to experience the city’s more laid-back side.

A Space for Community and Nature

Park Opatovina may not boast extensive facilities or activities, but it stands out as a space where community and nature intersect. It’s common to see locals walking their dogs, children playing, or individuals enjoying a moment of solitude. The park’s simplicity is its strength, offering a slice of everyday life in Zagreb.

Overall Impression

Park Opatovina, with its understated charm, is a testament to the beauty of Zagreb’s smaller green spaces. It may not feature in the top lists of must-visit tourist attractions, but it offers a genuine and peaceful park experience. For those looking to step away from the tourist trail and enjoy a quiet, local atmosphere, Park Opatovina is a delightful choice.

reviews.author: Svetlana Margetová
reviews.text:
We discovered Park Grič during our Christmas walk through Zagreb's Upper Town with Ondra, Rolko, and Brano. We visited during the daytime, and while the park was beautifully decorated with Christmas lights everywhere, we didn't get to experience the full magical effect since the lights weren't turned on yet. I can only imagine how stunning it must look at night when everything is illuminated!

Even during the day, this charming little park was absolutely worth visiting. It's a peaceful green oasis tucked into the medieval part of Zagreb, right between Strossmayer Promenade, Lotrščak Tower, and Mesnička Street. The park is built on two levels with lovely pathways, and you can see parts of the original medieval defensive walls of Zagreb integrated into the landscape – beautifully restored stone walls that are over 700 years old! The park has gorgeous views looking out over the city, and it's easy to see why locals love this spot. There are plenty of benches to sit and relax, a decorative fountain, and the whole atmosphere just feels very authentic and historical.

The park was clearly set up for Advent Zagreb celebrations, with all the light installations ready to create that romantic, fairy-tale atmosphere after dark. We could see the arches of lights, the decorations draped through the trees, and all the careful preparation. According to what I've read, at night the park transforms into the "Most Romantic Advent" spot with Chopin's Nocturnes playing, tranquil light displays, and a very peaceful, meditative atmosphere – quite different from the busier Christmas markets in other parts of the city. Definitely a place to return to after sunset!

Park Grič is actually the oldest public park in Zagreb! Its name comes from "Grič," which is the name of the hill where medieval Zagreb was built – in fact, "Grič" and "Gradec" were more commonly used names for Zagreb than "Zagreb" itself until the mid-19th century. The park sits on an incredibly important archaeological site, which is why its recent renovation took an astonishing 14 years to complete (from 2003 to 2017)! During the renovation, archaeologists had to carefully excavate and preserve findings that included the western city walls from the 13th century, remains of a fortress from the Anjou period, and even a cemetery that existed at this exact location from medieval times until 1630. The medieval defensive walls you see in the park today are authentic – they're the same walls that once protected the free royal city of Gradec from attack. The park was designed by architect Marijan Hržić, who personally inspected each of the 29 benches (replicas of the originals) and oversaw the creation of a replica fountain by sculptor Zvonimira Obad (the original fountain was removed in the 1950s and its remains are now in the Museum of Arts and Crafts). The park also features the only two public drinking fountains in the entire city of Zagreb! Every year during Advent, there's a beautiful tradition called "Advent Under Lanterns" where people walk from Lotrščak Tower through the park to light 246 gas street lamps by hand while choirs sing Christmas carols – a 160-year-old tradition that brings light to Zagreb's dark winter evenings!

A must-visit spot in Zagreb's Upper Town, especially beautiful at night during the Christmas season!

reviews.author: Seokjin Ham
reviews.text: As part of Lenuci’s Horseshoe, this park was named in honor of Josip Juraj Strossmayer, a Croatian Roman Catholic bishop, politician, and patron of the arts. He was a strong advocate for Slavic cultural and educational autonomy and played a key role in the founding of both the Croatian Academy of Sciences and Arts and the University of Zagreb. At the center of the park stands a statue of Strossmayer, created by renowned sculptor Ivan Meštrović. On the northern side of the park is the Croatian Academy of Sciences and Arts, housed in a Neo-Renaissance building constructed in 1880. During Advent in Zagreb, the park is beautifully lit and decorated, hosting festive performances and holiday markets.

reviews.author: Sjur Svenning
reviews.text:
In the past I used to live in London (Lewisham to be exact, but that’s not important as I am still alive and kicking).

Before moving to London and whilst living there I was a fan of the idea of parks. The thought of going to a preserved area prospering with plant life and a few but select type of cultivated people that would all leave each other alone and in peace, was to me a wonderful thought.

But despite whatever park i came across or sought out either in central, south, north, west or east London. (You think of a park and I have probably been there) It seemed as too many people had exactly the same idea and or a slightly different version of it. And so even though I’d might find a park that I was slightly at peace in, it would never be for more than 10 minutes tops. Before some bloke with a boombox would settle down. Or a group of middle aged women would come to practice yoga for 3 hours straight. And don’t even dare to think about the idea of arriving late in the night (I am sure you as well as I am aware of the chances of survival should you chose to stay in a park for some “peace after the break of dawn” in London)

Anyway, the reason I am talking about this is because today in this very park Petra Krešimira IV, I finally had my ideal park fantasy fulfilled. Peaceful rest on a bench surrounded by trees and passed by some but not too many people. Left to myself and my own thoughts just like in the films.

reviews.author: Seokjin Ham
reviews.text: As part of Lenuci’s Horseshoe, this square is also known as Tomislavac. It was originally named after Austrian Emperor Franz Joseph I, but in 1927, it was renamed to honor King Tomislav, the first king of Croatia. At the southern end of the square stands a bronze equestrian statue of King Tomislav, created by Robert Frangeš-Mihanović in 1938 and installed in 1947. On the northern side is the Art Pavilion, built in 1898, which now serves as one of Zagreb’s key art exhibition spaces. Each winter, the square transforms into a popular ice skating destination, with a large outdoor rink set up for both locals and visitors. During this festive season, the entire area is illuminated and beautifully decorated, creating a magical holiday atmosphere.
