Kids can play while parents can drink they cofe. It is the middle of buildings so kids don't have direct access to street and traffic. Trocadero has best staff ever ❤

reviews.author: Ante Krpan
reviews.text: A hidden gem in Zagreb especially if you're a tourist with kids and need a coffe break. It's right next to a great open bar and you can relax and sip your coffee while looking at your kid playing in the sandpit. There is one entrance from Dalmatinska street, the other one from Meduliceva and the third if you go through Pivnica Medvedgrad Pub in Ilica but you will have to pass through the pub and then through the pubs terrace open during summer. But the Dalmatinska entrance is recommended. Just look for the sign for caffe bar Trocadero. It's right there just go through the courtyard.

reviews.author: UPSET Theatre
reviews.text: Great place in the yard between the buildings. Is known for the children's playground within the park. The park has a cafe where you can drink your favorite drink with other parents.

reviews.author: Ivan Rinčić
reviews.text: Great place for a walk!!

reviews.author: Alan Kralj
reviews.text: One of the most beautiful places in Zagreb. A lot of green. Very romantic.

reviews.author: Aleksandar Gregurić
reviews.text: Great place to chill & relax 🙂

reviews.author: Miles
reviews.text: It's a very  nice space with plenty of trees a few paths and lots of benches if you want to sit. There are also toilets here in a green shed.  But the greenery actually stretches much further than shown by the park running south down the hill and a bit more to the east. I've just included photos of this particular part only. Oh yes, there's also a children's play area.

reviews.author: Peter Luketić
reviews.text: Nice getaway in the city but don't  forget the bug spray in the summer eves. They'll eat you alive!

reviews.author: Ozana
reviews.text: Very nice; great for a dog-its big they can run and have fun!

reviews.author: Danijel Milcic
reviews.text: Nice little local park with night lights and fenced playground for kids. Pretty quiet and a nice view.

reviews.author: Fazzie M
reviews.text: Beautiful view and seating areas. You can walk your dog ,watch your kids at the park or simply enjoy the view. Very small and serene park.

reviews.author: Davorin Cmrecki
reviews.text: Nice green spot in the most beautiful quarter of the City. Easy to reach from the centre square of ban Jelacic by foot, to find a peaceful hill...

reviews.author: Marin Krapac
reviews.text: It could be better without dogs.

reviews.author: Filip Elez
reviews.text: Adorable, little park hidden away near the city center. There’s not a lot of traffic near. It’s well-maintained & picturesque with a kids playground, lots of green are, benches & a cute little pond with a fountain. There’s also street parking.

reviews.author: Tibor Keser
reviews.text: Very cozy and relaxed little park, ideal for a quick bite from local bakeries or markets.

reviews.author: Željko Mandić
reviews.text: Nice Park

reviews.author: Amel Faraj
reviews.text: Small park with a playground suitable for young kids and toddlers. There is a public restroom in the gated playground, but not very clean.

reviews.author: Musheer Alam
reviews.text: Not bad

places.name: Park Bartola Kašića
places.address: Unnamed Road, 10000, Donji Grad, Zagreb, Croatia

reviews.author: Ivan Milić
reviews.text:
The biggest kiddie park in this part of town. Especially busy in the afternoon, after the kindergartens and schools close.
There are parking places all around. The park is sorrounded by a tall hedge, and a childproof metal fence.
Beware of the dogs that can often be found in the open part of the park.

reviews.author: Pavle Zobundžija
reviews.text: Good location, large area, several items for kids to enjoy. I like it. And my kids like it as well. Can be a bit too crowded on afternoons, but generally fine. Some parts of it could use a bit of refurbishing, but still nice to go there

reviews.author: The Tech Expert
reviews.text: Very nice park with a children's playground and benches to sit, but a lot of people walk their dogs without a leash which is uncommon in developed countries :(. They should put a sign "Please keep your dog on a leash". Also, the park should be better maintained since it looks run down.

reviews.author: Ivan Biljan
reviews.text: I mean it is just a patk without additional amenities except the playground for kids. It is ok for that I guess. Does not seem like the place to come to chill...

reviews.author: Maja Goričanec Ordanić
reviews.text: I am very pleased with this park. Equipment is great. Kids from 1 year till 10 can use this park. It is also large for a park in the center.  Definitly a place where kids enjoy

reviews.author: Ivan Milić
reviews.text:
Huge park, ideal for kids 0-10 years old. It is very well equipped.
It's sorrounded by parking places and streets with very low or no traffic. Between the park and the street there is a hedge and meadow for security.

reviews.author: vzrilic
reviews.text: One of the hidden gems of our city, lovely and relaxing place.

reviews.author: Danijel Dubičanac
reviews.text: The old climbing installation brings back the memories while integrating nicely with the rest of the park. Check the sculpture at the opposite end of park and enjoy high trees.

reviews.author: Davor Krivdić
reviews.text: Nice place for kids. Highly recommended.

reviews.author: Marko Repar
reviews.text: Nice park for the kids although a little outdated...

reviews.author: Mat Bienczyk
reviews.text: Nice little park, which is located right beside the Glavni Kolodvor and the hotel; has a fountain and several benches.

reviews.author: Marino Paradina
reviews.text: Beautiful small park located near the Glavni Kolodvor.It has benches all around the park and a fountain in the midle.The whole park is decorated with flowers.

reviews.author: Maitham AlFardan
reviews.text:
Beautiful small park, gives a great view to the guest on esplanade hotel next to it,,
And a great place for a quick walk,,

reviews.author: Suzana Budanec
reviews.text: Top

reviews.author: The Tech Expert
reviews.text: Park is nice and has a fountain, but there are a few defects: the lamp post is raised with the bolts being visible and a lot of tiles are wobbly or damaged. The city should provide maintenance more often so that the park doesn’t look like this in the future.

places.name: Mallinov Park
places.address: Zagreb - Mallinov park, 10000, Gornji Grad - Medveščak, Zagreb, Croatia

reviews.author: Ivan Milić
reviews.text:
Beautiful park! It has two sequoias, a rare landmark in this parts of Europe 🌳
It has three entrances: north, west, and east. You can park you car near all the entrances. The northern one can be busier because there is a retirement home near.

reviews.author: Martina Batinica
reviews.text: Just a perfect place...

reviews.author: Rasp Berry
reviews.text: There you can see the only sequias (as far as I know) in Zagreb, two nice old trees with lightning protection

reviews.author: Luka Draženović
reviews.text: Nice place to rest and enjoy beautiful trees and nature

reviews.author: Natasa Vrbetić
reviews.text: An info board of some kind about the seqouias would be great

reviews.author: blanka puž
reviews.text: The cinema has a parking lot in the basement, otherwise it says the first few hours are free, later hours are quite expensive. The cinema is tidy, I got the feeling of being in an ambience from the 80s, the seats comfortable, quite a big screen. The staff is friendly. The center is nicely decorated, you can have a drink in the pubs, or even something to eat. It was a film that was never made in our country.

reviews.author: Јуњи
reviews.text: Went to cinestar at least once a week while I was living in zagreb, with the student card you get 50% off. Miss those 5 euro tickets. Hvala!

reviews.author: STUART CONRAD OSTERMAN
reviews.text: Great place we sat in vip seats why not less expensive than uk paid 110hk for 2 watched the movie bullet train great film a bit gorry at times but funny parts throughout the film, alot of people must think we are mad to go to cinema abroad thinking it is in a different language, then you realise thats why everyone around Croatia speaks perfect english, Our 1st time here and I can honestly say we have meet all nice wonderful people highly recommended you come to Zagreb be proud

reviews.author: Yousef A. Almubarak
reviews.text: Although the screen is huge, the cleanness and quality of the place are not great. It was also too crowded and annoying to some extent. The sound on theater is not well balanced. Overall, good theater to watch movies in huge screen for 40-42 kuna. All movies are played in their original language with subtitle.

reviews.author: Ivana Djurasin
reviews.text: While in general I do like going to Cinestar (there is not so many other choice in Croatia either), I am surprised by their response on claim that I raised for slightly less than 5 EUR. We parked in the parking lot (not owned by Cinestar) and Cinestar is claiming that with the paid ticket you have 5 hours of free parking there (Branimir Mingle Mall). We confirmed the parking ticket on Cinestar machine that BTW looks terrible (half broken), but green light confirmed validity?!). Before the exit, we put the parking card into parking machine -> to re-confirm we will not stuck with the car on the ramp and we were surprised that we had to pay for the parking (EUR 4,80) - we had to process payments to get the card back and I thought - it not our mistake so this would be clear claim - however we got fast response from Cinestar that we need to raise the claim to company owning the parking (Best-In parking) - even thou it is disputable if Cinestar machine was working properly. That's not how good & reliable company behave to their customers. I can swallow these 5 EUR but negative feeling of Cinestar reaction remains...

reviews.author: Viktor Grujevski
reviews.text: This place is simply amazing. We went there mostly before noon. The tables are set so you'd have plenty of personal space to relax and feel comfortable. The ambient is beyond words, staff were great, the coffee even better. We'd definitely visit the cinema, next time.

reviews.author: Ivn
reviews.text: This place has a great vibe. It’s a studio-style bar with abstract, vintage, and retro decorations. The drinks are decent, though nothing extraordinary. The service was a bit slow, but not a big deal. What stood out to me the most was the Marilyn Monroe picture—I absolutely loved it! 🙈

reviews.author: Alexandra Chioureas
reviews.text: Kris the illusionist was seriously amazing. What an entertaining person!! Loved him (before New You See Me)

reviews.author: Alena
reviews.text: A cool, stylish place, but unfortunately, they allow smoking indoors 😣

reviews.author: Martin Danko (matodanko)
reviews.text: Nice place, good drinks but the service needs some improvements. Waitress should always ask upfront which way of payment a customer prefers and not to blame him that he should said earlier that he wants to pay with card. Waitress should ask first!

places.name: CineStar Novi Zagreb (Avenue Mall)
places.address: Avenue Mall, Avenija Dubrovnik 16, 10000, Zagreb, Croatia

reviews.author: Luka
reviews.text: It is on the top floor of Avenue Mall Shoping Center. Alot of cinema halls with plenty of seating spaces. Also you can buy popcorn/nachos and other soft drinks there.

reviews.author: Korina Keti Filiar Zlatec (Keti)
reviews.text: Wonderful after the pandemic lockdown and its aftermath. Freedom to go to cinema is such a wonderful feeling! Good discounts simetimes, when the tickets are only 19 kn (2,52 Euro)...

reviews.author: yosy Supanto
reviews.text: Free parking, has many studio, cozy place

reviews.author: Nicolò Scialpi
reviews.text: The cinema isn’t very well-maintained, and the snacks you buy aren’t worth the price. Some screening rooms are good though.

reviews.author: Hrvoje Kaić
reviews.text: This venue desperately needs to be renovated, chairs are basically falling apart with holes and wrinkled fabric everywhere, feels like sitting on bricks as well.

reviews.author: Dream Catcher
reviews.text: The staff were really friendly and informative. The nachos is delicious. I just hope they have more flavor for the popcorn. We tried the 3D first and the cinema wasn't packed.. it is also clean and it doesn't have any weird smell. Overall I enjoyed my first movie and we will be trying the IMAX next.

reviews.author: Darko Bernobic
reviews.text: Superb parking area with a lot of space, quick and easy entrance in the mall. IMAX is OK, a bit too loud foreplays, visitors reckless, leave a lot of trash behind. A nice experience.

reviews.author: naveen nathan
reviews.text: The 4dx experience was amazing. There is a food court. The seats are stacked snuck but are comfortable. It felt like a roller-coaster through out. The audio-visual experience was worth for the price. Its on the top floor of a mall and hence has food, shop, luxury, clothing, boutique, grocery and all that is necessary under a roof. Ample space for parking. Recommended!

reviews.author: Savaş Okyay
reviews.text: Worst IMAX XP ever. Bad seating design, hard to read subtitles because of the heads in front of you (I am glad I do not know Croatian). Talking audience. Helpful staff anyways.

reviews.author: Daniel Zibert
reviews.text:
You need to wait 30min just tp get food.
I already orderd it before.

reviews.author: Caer Cadarn
reviews.text:
Great local cinema with options for everyone!
This cinema gets a solid 5/5 from me. What I really appreciated was the thoughtful approach to languages. For the little ones (or those who prefer it), the cartoons are dubbed in Croatian, which is fantastic. But for the big Hollywood blockbusters, they show them in the original English with Croatian subtitles – perfect for catching the actors' performances while still being accessible to everyone.
Plus, the snack situation is on point! You've got your classic cinema popcorn, but then they throw in tacos and a variety of sweets too. Sounds like a place where you can definitely settle in and enjoy a movie. Highly recommended!

reviews.author: The Engineer
reviews.text: Nice place and nice bar next to it! Very friendly employees as well!

reviews.author: Ivana Paić Kavur
reviews.text:
Best cinema ever!!!!
So while we booked a movie, Fly me to the moon, I booked VIP Relax seats and it was best experience ever, you see this was opened 2 years ago and they had: Comfortable seats, Royal Beds,(with charger and table maybe) VIP Relax (with table and charger) and Love box seats by Coca-Cola and they were comfortable too like the standard seats, I recommend you go to this place to watch movies

reviews.author: Ljubiša Kukulj
reviews.text: The cinema itself is pretty good, with decent tech, equipment, and seats. Nothing super special, though. They could definitely improve the freshness of the popcorn and snacks, and it'd be great if someone would actually deal with people using their phones or talking during the movie without a care in the world while others are trying to watch.

reviews.author: Sandro Jug
reviews.text:
I used to love going to the cinema and, for the most part, I still do; however, less and less so to CineStar.

The cinema experience, the picture and sound quality are usually top-notch; however, the quality of the treatment of the customers is dropping significantly. I'm not even talking about the overpriced snacks and watered down drinks, it's easy enough to just not buy them or get them somewhere else, but it's the passive-aggressive shooing of the customer at the end of the movie.

As an artist myself, I still appreciate cinema and movies as an art form, even if most of them today are soulless cash grabs. Despite that, hundreds, even thousands of people were included in the making of a movie and I watch through the credits whenever I watch a movie. The fact that the cinema workers ask you to leave your seat as soon as the credits start rolling and ask you to either leave "because there's no after-credits scene" or stand by the exit at the bottom of the cinema hall is beyond rude. I paid for the ticket for the movie, so I expect to be able to watch it until the very end.

Because of this treatment, it's obvious that CineStar is no more than a cash machine that doesn't actually care about its movies. No surprise, I know, but sad nonetheless. Thank you for ruining the cinema experience for your customers. 5-star cinemas? Hardly.

reviews.author: Aida Lifestyle Traveler
reviews.text:
Branimir Mingle Mall is a modern and beautifully designed spot in Zagreb, perfect for anyone looking to shop, relax, or enjoy a casual day out. It’s not your traditional shopping mall — it has a trendy, urban vibe that makes it feel fresh and inviting.

The mall offers a nice selection of stores, restaurants, cafés, and entertainment options, making it great for grabbing a bite, meeting friends, or simply taking a break during your day. The atmosphere is relaxed, clean, and stylish, with plenty of open areas to sit and unwind.

Its central location is another big plus — easy to reach and convenient whether you’re a local or just visiting the city. It’s also a good place if you prefer a calmer, more modern mall experience compared to some of the busier shopping centers in Zagreb.

Overall, Branimir Mingle Mall is a cool and pleasant place to spend time, shop a little, eat well, and enjoy Zagreb’s urban lifestyle.

reviews.author: Ljubica Banic
reviews.text: This is a lovely shopping centre small but nice, clean and quiet, is next to the Hilton hotel, it has a very exclusive gym, DM shop, cinema and few places to eat, charge your phone and seat in the lovely air conditioning temperature of us summer and heating at winter time. Is walking distance to the city centre. Highly recommend this place.

reviews.author: Mohil Kapoor
reviews.text: Nice mall with food food outlets and movies to watch.

reviews.author: Kre Tino
reviews.text: Branimir Mingle Mall isn’t a typical shopping mall — it features a cinema, a drugstore, a couple of restaurants and bars, a casino, and a hotel within the same complex. If you’re looking to shop, this isn’t the place. However, it can be a convenient spot to catch a movie, grab a bite to eat, have a drink, or try your luck at the casino. Its location is quite handy, being close to the city center, the railway station, and the tram station. There’s also a parking garage, which adds to the convenience. Just don’t expect much when it comes to retail options.

reviews.author: Francisco Estrada
reviews.text: This mall nice but it really needs management to control the construction on site. They are working during the day and crating dust that covers the entire place. They should put a plastic around to cover the dust but it seems like they don’t care. This cause serious health problems..

reviews.author: Ante Andric
reviews.text: Nice and comfortable place... Very professional and gentle staff... popcorn could be more fresh, but all other quality and service are for recommendation....

reviews.author: Dokica Horvat
reviews.text: Its amazing and has a lot of options to eat and the seats are comfterable

reviews.author: ivsolarevic
reviews.text: Went there on a week day about mont ago w my two kids, and had a wonderful experience. Everyone working there was helpful and patient with me and my kids, and not annoyed like we’d seen in some other cinemas! Great addition for my kids was slushy and I was missing sweet popcorn option.. Great team, big 5 stars for that!

reviews.author: Tomislav Pavic
reviews.text: Cinema like any modern cinema.Friendly staff and good loyalty program.Prices of popcorn and stuff are ...overrated as in every cinema in the world probably

reviews.author: Robin
reviews.text: Good for English movies! But there is only salty popcorn:( The staff is friendly and well organized!

reviews.author: Govind Puri
reviews.text:
Absolutely fantastic experience, a must while in Zagreb! The space is very cosy, you feel like in your living room at home. Stuff is super friendly, chairs super comfy, rotateable for full 360 cinematic experience. I watched The Soloist (Alex Hannod climbing in the Alps) and remained seated speachless for 5 min after the projection.
Most of the movies are in English, subtitled (optional without Croatian subtitle).
AWESOME!

reviews.author: Juraj Kozole
reviews.text: Such a great experience! We watched the movie Everest and the first part which was filmed in chamonix area is just stunning. Just watching every climber’s move and enjoying the atmosphere is beautiful! Will return for a second viewing for sure!

reviews.author: Viktor Pavkovic
reviews.text:
A unique experience worth the visit. We watched Soloist and it was a time well spent.

Especially interesting for children.

reviews.author: Zoran Stanko
reviews.text: Great experience.  Everyone should try this at least once. I bought tickets online and at the same time became a member which gives you 20% discount and oportunity to buy drinks/popcorn.  We watched Soloist.

reviews.author: Zoheir Touaibia
reviews.text: It was a good experience, though nothing exceptional. I think the choice of the movie played a big role, since I noticed other reviews were much more positive and enthusiastic.

reviews.author: Ivan Milić
reviews.text:
The prettiest and biggest open cinema in this part of Croatia. Built in 1954. by the renowned architect Kazimir Ostrogović, it still stands today and serves as an open cinema.
It can accomodate around 250 people during various film festivals (such as Fanatastic Film Festival Zagreb), and during regular movie screenings.
The parking is right below, and also in the garage Tuškanac.

reviews.author: Noa Mance
reviews.text: Great in the summer if the weather is warm it is a great place for a nice and relaxing evening. The films are usually many good films and all of the spots in the cinema offer a nice view, just don't forget to bring a cushion because the wooden benches do get uncomfortable otherwise.

reviews.author: c20ux
reviews.text: Picture and sound are great, nature aroud beautiful, no annoying mosquitoes. Only problem is wooden bench you sit on if movie is over 2 hours long, its so uncomfortable it starts to pull you out of the movie, but you got outdoor chair cushion to help a little. If only they would show more horror movies.

reviews.author: Anja Rosic
reviews.text: Tickets can be bought online or at the ticket office an hour and a half before the movie. The prices for the tickets and refreshments are reasonable, the popcorn was fresh and everyone's temperature was measured before going in which is responsible during these times. The chairs are mostly uncomfortable but cushions are provided which help to a certain point. If you don't like being cold I'd suggest dressing warmly and bringing a blanket. There are toilet facilities on the premises so there are no worries on that end. The quality of sound and picture was satisfactory and while it can't be to a standard of a regular movie theater, it didn't diminish the enjoyment of the experience. All in all, it is a lovely summer entertainment.

reviews.author: Luka Rendulic
reviews.text:
Amazing place in the center of Zagreb!
If u have opportunity, go and enjoy, u will not regret

reviews.author: Mak Vujanović
reviews.text: Old school cinema, one of the only places in Zagreb where you can watch classic movies on the big screen. Great atmosphere and cheap tickets, very rich programe, something for everyone, heaven for a film buff.

reviews.author: Melita Petkovic
reviews.text: A nice old theater, just like they used to be, a little worn down but that as well gives it charm. A big hall, great leg space, perfect visibility and old movies on the screen. A perfect trip down the memory lane alltogether.

reviews.author: Hrabren Dobrotić
reviews.text: Legendary Zagreb cinema 🎦. Nicely selected program, mostly classic and independent film. Affordable. They also have movie-goer club and participate in the festival organisation.

reviews.author: Hrvoje Petrovic
reviews.text: Nice small cinema, they play interesting small budget movies which are 3-4 levels over stupid Hollywood blockbusters. If you are in last row which is 15th you may feel that chairs should be placed on higher position, that's why I gave it only 4. Also bathrooms smell, and It would be nice that whole place gets some renovation from EU funded projects. I recommend movie 'Styx' - second best awarded European movie for 2018 year.

reviews.author: The Tech Expert
reviews.text: Nice movie theatre which brings a lot of good memories from the past. Great place to watch some old films. The building is either from the 18th or 19th century and looks amazing. Inside you can see the old film projectors which used tape since there were not digital like today. Since the theatre is a bit outdated, it can be renovated and modernised. Energy efficiency can be improved. I will definitely come again.

reviews.author: Eleni
reviews.text: It's such a great place! We visited Zagreb, and unfortunately, it was raining so much, so we thought we could watch an english movie. This place is amazing, people working there are really kind and the room for the movie is big and cosy. Also, next to the cinema there is an amazing bar/ caffe. This experience was worth it so much! You should definitely give it a try!

reviews.author: Haziq Hamdan
reviews.text: I was delighted to be here during Korean Film Week and the place is very nice, clean, and so on. Its a bit of a pity that they no longer serve food due to the covid outbreak recently in Croatia. But I suggest everyone living in Zagreb to go one day.

reviews.author: Bhagavat das
reviews.text: Best cinema in Zagreb. Great movies, old school vibe, cheap tickets, video and sound perfect. Never too crowded. Has a nice caffe bar next to it.

reviews.author: Pero Beljo
reviews.text: Retro cinema showing  films from cinemas golden age and contemporary films, but the noisy cafe next door is a nuisance. No toilet facilities except in the filthy cafe next door.

reviews.author: Nikolina Komorčec
reviews.text: The chairs are small, old, and broken. The hall is not tilted enough, so if there is someone taller sitting in front of you, you want see half of the screen. The sound and picture quality are good and the tickets are cheap

reviews.author: Mislav Pavelic
reviews.text: Nice spot for the Eric Bibb concert. Easy access and excellent spot to go with friends to enjoy a show

reviews.author: Pamia Monti
reviews.text: Good for gatherings or seminars🤗in the center of the city🤗it needs lots of love and restauration because it has longer history🤗regardless it still has its charm🤗  coffee shops and parking in front 🤗as well as some places to have a snack🤗

reviews.author: Tomislav Papec
reviews.text: It's great, about to watch Jim Jeffries.

reviews.author: The Tech Expert
reviews.text: Not bad, but the theater still looks 60s and it missing a lot of modern features. On the outside, the insulation and windows need to be upgraded and the inside is full of wood paneling.

reviews.author: Petra Vizjak
reviews.text: Here for Damien Rice concert. We traveled from Germany and paid the entrance 270 euros per person!!! Some folks came even from US.  It is hot, stuffy and there is no air conditioning. No bar to buy water or any other refreshment. Even water in toilet is warm. This is outrageous and no way to treat anyone. I dont care about fancy venues, but keeping people for 4 hours without AC and water is borderline inhuman. And then charging for it 270 €. Is this a joke???

reviews.author: Hrabren Dobrotić
reviews.text: A nice little stage in the centre of the city. The Academy of drama arts uses it for it's productions and as festival sight also. I've seen only good stuff here.

reviews.author: Mislav Sabolić
reviews.text: Great place, but you must know where it is to find it.

reviews.author: Boris Surbek
reviews.text: If there is a place that I would favor in Zagreb, then it is truly F22 ... And for many reasons. Let's start in order. Excellent cuisine (only local and fresh everyday ingredients), warm and pleasant atmosphere, diverse seasonal offer, something for everyone. And spoon, and main courses, and homemade brandies, wines, cheeses, and imagination in all of it. (Try the black cuttlefish risotto, cod, pasticada, lamb chops, sarma ... Professional and very pleasant staff, great clientele. Prices very fair!!! If they let you in, see for yourself.

reviews.author: Zoran Mrkonjić Toyesa
reviews.text: A very good playground for future rising actors. Croatia has great potential in upcoming young actors. Well done!

reviews.author: Rajka Bozanic
reviews.text: I saw the play Family. It was very good. It's not a representative place, but it has a great vibe and the visibility is perfect from every seat.

reviews.author: Nikola-Sascha Horvat
reviews.text: Ok

reviews.author: Andre Linoge
reviews.text: Quite a good cinema, the comfort and seats may not be perfect, but the prices are very fair.

reviews.author: Zoja Stambuk
reviews.text: Excellent cinema...atmosphere, hall, repertoire, parking, restaurant-bar, everything!!!

reviews.author: Simeon Minev
reviews.text: Cultural events happening here

reviews.author: ja jesam
reviews.text: Super

reviews.author: Sonja Cirkovic
reviews.text: I was at the excellent play DINNER WITH AN IDIOT, I laughed until I cried. The play was directed by Rebecca Agić. There should be more plays like this. I really liked how I found the place. If you go from the city center, with the 5th or 17th bus, get off at the Studenski Dom Stjepan Račić stop, enter the complex and very quickly find the Forum Cinema.

reviews.author: Vladimir P
reviews.text: A legendary student-type Cinema. Not Hi-tech, middle sized screen only, but still its very nice to see movies on it. See some off-program movies and get suprized forna fraction of price you may pay in a modern cinema. Recommended if you are fed up with mainstream cinema, or to see a bit differen atmosphere and special people who attend such shows.

reviews.author: Helena Martinuš (Helica)
reviews.text: Ok

reviews.author: Robert Gradečki
reviews.text: Brick Club is an awesome private mini cinema for up to 12 people, perfect for a small celebration. I visited with six small girls for a private cartoon screening, and the whole experience was absolutely amazing. Eespecially the free popcorn, which was a huge hit!

reviews.author: Patricia Šoić
reviews.text: I visited this cinema with a friend. Everything was perfect from the service, the decor and the overall atmosphere. The private cinema offers a special and enjoyable experience that I would definitely recommend to everyone. I can't wait to come again.

reviews.author: M
reviews.text: Top experience in top space. Watched the match Croatia - Iceland. I highly recommend it

It's a nice modern museum with the art well displayed. The signage is both in Croatian and English.
Admission is higher than most museums in Zagreb at 13 Euros for seniors and students and €19 for other adults.
A slide can be taken down from the second floor. It was fun- I did it. It was pretty fast and had turns. You end up outside (in the cold this time of the year) and have to find the entrance to re-enter.

reviews.author: Hristo Hristov (Christo Christoff)
reviews.text: Visiting this museum will increase your understanding of the meaning of the word "contemporary" next to "art". You will find pieces of art that excite, shake, surprise, appall, bewilder. You will not be left indifferent.

reviews.author: Kevin Costello
reviews.text: Some of the most intriguing and fascinating artworks I have seen. Really surprising and evocative artworks displayed over the two sections of the gallery. I had a fantastic time looking and feeling the interactive exhibits. I highly recommend it to you!

reviews.author: ΡΑΦΑΗΛΙΑ ΣΙΔΗΡΟΠΟΥΛΟΥ

The staff is very kind, and even if you mess up a bowling round by throwing the ball too early, they come immediately and help you out. Highly recommended!

reviews.author: Ratko Ivekovic

They have a solid selections of beers and other beverages and both the kids and we had a great time. The only critique I have is that more attention needs to be payed to cleanliness.

reviews.author: Kwon's Family

There were few people duirng day time. It is good for fmaily and you can easily spend an hour with active participation. Rates are moderate. Recommended.

ps: Balls are slippery. It would be better stock dry towel.
